title: Strategic Pricing Analyst
description:
Status \- Term (1 year)
  

  

In your role as Strategic Pricing Analyst at The North West Company (NWC), you will be responsible for the execution of NWC pricing strategies, overseeing required pricing adjustments, and providing summary of financial performance metrics. This includes making retail decisions that adhere to established strategy, along with modeling of alternative outcomes as to support effective decision\-making.
  

  

Joining NWC gives you the opportunity for growth and development throughout the company, a competitive salary and benefits package, and to be part of a dynamic and diverse culture.
  

  

NWC is a leading retailer to under\-served rural communities and urban neighborhood markets in the following regions: Northern Canada, Western Canada, rural Alaska, the South Pacific islands, and the Caribbean. Our stores offer a broad range of products and services with an emphasis on food. Our value offer is to be the best local shopping choice for everyday household and local lifestyle needs. Check out northwest.ca for more information.
  

  

**Your Role:*** Uses the DemandTec Pricing tool to execute the category pricing strategy defined by the Category Managers (CMs).
* Assigned to specific banners and categories, collaborating with CMs to gain in‑depth understanding of each category’s pricing approach.
* Provides informed recommendations and constructively challenges CMs to ensure strong, banner‑aligned pricing strategies.
* Completes weekly price updates for assigned categories and reviews outcomes with the Category Manager.
* Analyzes weekly competitive checks and provides recommended pricing adjustments.
* Executes the category pricing strategy for Special Ship items.
* Works collaboratively with the Category Manager and Pricing Manager to establish and achieve category strategies, performance metrics, and financial goals.
* Oversee the timely delivery of competitive price indices and retail updates in coordination with the Pricing Coordinator and Pricing Manager.
Manages the prompt execution of Special Ship pricing in partnership with the Pricing Analyst and Coordinator.  
* 

  

  

  

**Desired Skills \& Experience:*** Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Data Science, Economics, or a College Business Administration diploma combined with 2\+ years of relevant experience
* Experience in analytical roles with strong technical capabilities, including Excel, PowerQuery, Access, and SQL.
* Demonstrates excellent critical thinking and problem‑solving abilities.
* Skilled in facilitation and presentations, with a proven ability to build and manage effective relationships.
* Highly organized with strong time‑management skills.
* Possesses strong financial acumen.
* Ability to quickly learn new concepts, systems and industry best practices and apply these learnings to the continued development of the team/ support of the business.
* Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and oral.
* Ability to prioritize and accomplish work independently and within a team to meet deadlines.
* Exceptional organizational and time management skills; and,
Ability to work well individually with minimal supervision, as well as part of a team.  
* 

  

  

**Location: Gibraltar House, 77 Main Street, Wpg., MB**  

**Reports to: Manager, Strategic Pricing**  

  

We create a collaborative and constructive culture by:
  

  

Being Enterprising • Committing to our Customer • Working with Passion • Leading and Developing Others • Demonstrating Respect and Inclusivity • Building Trust
  

  

The North West Company is committed to inclusion and diversity and encourages applications from all candidates including but not limited to: all religions and ethnicities, LGBTQ2s\+, BIPOC, and persons with disabilities.
  

  

**A competitive salary is being offered along with opportunities to learn, develop and advance throughout the organization. We thank all applicants for their interest; however only those selected for an interview will be contacted.**  

  

This is an active job opening for a position that is currently available and we are actively seeking to fill.
  

  

This position adheres to the Corporate Application Process Guidelines.

title: Project Administrator
description:
**It has come to our attention that various international organizations or individuals have been offering false employment opportunities at Aecon Group Inc. Aecon Group Inc. employment policies and processes involve interviews, and candidates who seek employment are never required to pay us any sum of money. To do so would be contrary to our business conduct guidelines and ethical practices. We also do not extend formal offers of employment or execute employment agreements through social media or social chat platforms.**


**We take this matter extremely seriously and are working with the appropriate authorities to shut down such fraudulent schemes. Please remain vigilant on this matter and report any suspicious outreach to your local authorities and email any concerns to aecon@aecon.com**


**Date:** Apr 6, 2026
**Location:**Winnipeg, MB, CA
**Operating Sector:** Industrial
**Position Title:** Project Administrator


**Come Build Your Career at Aecon!**



As a Canadian leader in infrastructure development, Aecon is safely and sustainably building what matters for future generations to thrive! We lead some of the most impactful infrastructure projects of our generation, at the forefront of transformational change in transportation and energy, and partnering every day to build, connect, power, and strengthen our communities.

  



At Aecon, you can count on:


* **Safety Always.** Our number one core value. If we can’t do it safely, we don’t do it at all.
* **Integrity.** We lead by example, with humility and courage.
* **Accountability.** We’re passionate about delivering on our commitments.
* **Inclusion.** We provide equitable opportunities for everyone.

  



We lead the infrastructure industry with purpose, and our people are at the heart of everything we do. So, we invest in our people, just like they invest in us!  


At Aecon we:


* Ensure you and your family receive the services needed to **support your mental, emotional, and physical well\-being.**
* Believe in helping you build your career through our **Aecon University and Leadership Programs.**
* Are committed to supporting and investing in inclusive work environments, through initiatives like Equity, Diversity \& Inclusion training, our **Aecon Women in Trades and Aecon Diversity in Trades programs**, and our **Employee Resource Groups (ERGs)** to ensure we are building inclusion into every aspect of our culture at Aecon.
* Are a **leader in sustainable construction**. With a strong commitment to operating responsibly by **minimizing our impact** on the environment and surrounding communities.

  



Our business success relies on strong execution and continuous improvement – driven by the diversity, expertise and teamwork of our people. We’re always searching the globe for innovative, collaborative minds to join our best\-in\-class Aecon community!

 **What is the Opportunity?**

Aecon Water is a national leader in the delivery of water and wastewater infrastructure, specializing in the planning, construction, and rehabilitation of critical municipal and regional systems. We provide clients across Canada with end‑to‑end solutions, combining technical expertise, self‑perform capabilities, and a strong focus on safety, quality, and project delivery excellence.

Aecon Water Infrastructure is supporting the delivery of a major regional wastewater program in Winnipeg, Manitoba, and we are currently looking for a Project Administrator to join the team. This project includes the construction of a new wastewater treatment facility and conveyance system serving Manitoba, supporting long\-term regional growth and improved environmental stewardship.  



The Project Administrator supports the Aecon Water team by providing day‑to‑day administrative and financial coordination to ensure smooth project execution. Reporting to the Project Director, this role supports site operations through invoicing, billing assistance, record keeping, and compliance with organizational and project requirements in a fast‑paced construction environment.  



 **What You'll Do Here:**
* Process, track, and distribute invoices for approval.
* Create and process purchase requisitions.
* Receive and process packing slips for materials.
* Process and track accounts payable invoices.
* Establishes accounts with local suppliers for miscellaneous items for the site.
* Coordinate site access, office supplies and general site logistics.
* Assists with the creation and submission of project billings, including backup documentation and submission support.
* Maintains an organized filing system on site.
* Learn, interpret, and applies organizational policies, rules, and regulations.
* Assist with continuous improvement of administrative processes and systems.
* Maintain labour force tracking, including headcount and trade breakdowns, board allowance and vehicle allowance.
* Track apprentice hours and work classifications to support apprenticeship program requirements.
* Support onboarding and offboarding of site personnel.
* Accurate daily entry of hourly workforce timecards (repetitive movement data entry).
* Provide day to day administrative support to the Project Director, Project Managers, and site leadership team.
* Able to perform in a fast\-paced environment.
* Prolonged sedentary positioning.
* On site work will be required.

  


**What You Bring to the Team:**
* Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced, deadline\-oriented environment.
* Proficiency in Excel and MS Office Suite. Exposure to construction project management \& scheduling tools is an asset.
* High level of integrity, confidentiality and accountability.
* Ability to do detailed work with accuracy.
* Excellent verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills.
* Excellent customer service skills.
* Strong planning, organizational and task\-monitoring skills.

  



Aecon fosters diversity, inclusion and belonging within and across our organization. We welcome all to apply including, women, visible minorities, Indigenous peoples, persons with disabilities, and persons of any sexual orientation or gender identity.  



We are committed to adhering to the objectives and requirements outlined in the Accessible Canada Act (ACA), and to meeting the accessibility needs of persons with disabilities in a timely manner. Through the implementation of the requirements of the ACA and its applicable regulations, appropriate accommodations will be provided upon request throughout the interview and hiring process.  



As part of our commitment to fair and transparent hiring practices, Aecon may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to assist in screening and reviewing candidates. These tools are applied responsibly and in accordance with applicable privacy laws and Aecon’s AI Usage Policy. All final decisions are made by qualified Aecon team members.

title: Civil Project Manager
description:
Penn\-co is founded on the principles of inspiring and empowering communities to build a brighter future. We strive to collaborate with community leaders to achieve their goals, advocate for their needs, and provide them with the tools and resources necessary to drive positive change.

  

Are you looking for an exciting and challenging career path? Are you passionate about construction and building on your future? We are committed to providing opportunities for development for our employees in our growing organization.

 **Responsibilities**


* Plan and manage civil construction projects across multiple locations, ensuring timelines, budgets, and project requirements are met.
* Develop project schedules, sequencing plans, and daily/weekly work priorities for field crews.
* Review scope, drawings, specifications, and contract requirements to ensure accurate execution.
* Plan equipment requirements and coordinate with the Equipment Department to ensure the right equipment is on project sites.
* Coordinate workforce allocation and crew assignments for multiple, active project sites.
* Ensure that all equipment is being maintained and serviced properly. Track daily logs and maintenance reports and view all related incidents or concerns.
* Anticipate risks and constraints (weather, access, logistics, material delays) and adjust plans accordingly.
* Maintain project schedules and adjust when required to ensure completion in accordance with the contract and project milestones.
* Provide direct support to foremen and site supervisors by coordinating work plans, deliveries, and equipment movement.
* Ensure crews are equipped with the correct materials, tools, and equipment before mobilizing to remote job sites.
* Communicate daily with field teams to monitor progress, resolve issues, and support productivity.
* Coordinate travel, mobilization, accommodations, and logistics required for work in northern communities.
* Assist with progress billing, job closeout, and turnover documentation.
* Prepare short interval plans, look\-ahead schedules, and resource forecasts.
* Support quality control efforts, helping identify issues early and ensuring rework is minimized

  


**Requirements**


* 5\+ years of experience in civil construction project management, coordination, or supervision.
* Strong knowledge of civil scope such as earthworks, grading, excavation, road building, drainage, water/sewer, underground utilities and site servicing and prep work.
* Experience supporting crews across multiple job sites and managing competing priorities.
* Ability to read and interpret drawings, specifications, and civil construction scopes.
* Strong understanding of construction scheduling, budgeting, and cost control.
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, Word); scheduling or project software experience is an asset.
* Valid driver’s license.
* Ability to travel to remote northern communities as required.
* Knowledge of safety standards and field requirements.
* Strong communication and leadership skills with the ability to support and direct field teams.
* Highly organized and comfortable managing multiple moving parts at once.
* Practical, solution\-focused problem solving.
* Strong attention to safety, production, and quality outcomes.
* Calm under pressure and able to adapt to changing site conditions.

  


**Working Conditions**


* Primarily office\-based with frequent communication with multiple active job sites.
* Regular travel to northern/remote communities for site visits, startup support, or issue resolution.
* Must be able to work flexible hours during peak construction season to support field operations.

  



Penn\-co has been in business for 65 years! Check out website www.penn\-co.com to see some of the exciting projects that we have completed and are currently working on! Empowering today for a better tomorrow.

  

Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Chef d’équipe principal des données (Senior Data Team Lead)
Senior Research Associate, Cloud-Based IoT System to Support People Struggling with Drug Addictions During Pregnancy
description:
**Senior Research Associates:**



Under the general direction of, and in collaboration with researchers and faculty members, positions at this level apply extensive knowledge in their field to lead high\-level, complex research projects. Compared to Research Associates, these positions exercise greater input and judgment over the research, may be involved in setting research program objectives, and may be responsible for managing the team and project funds. Senior Research Associates would normally hold a doctoral degree or have equivalent qualifications and experience.

  

**Position Overview:**



This Senior Research Associate (Postdoctoral\-Level) \- 4 Month Term position supports an ongoing research project entitled Cloud\-Based IoT System to Support People Struggling with Drug Addictions During Pregnancy. This project aims to design and deploy an intelligent AI\-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) framework that integrates wearable sensing, secure cloud infrastructure, machine learning analytics, and health data modelling to support vulnerable populations through improved monitoring, predictive insights, and decision\-support technologies.



The research integrates multiple advanced domains including:


* Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
* Internet of Things (IoT) and Edge/Cloud Computing
* Health Data Analytics and Medical Bioinformatics
* Secure Data Processing and Privacy\-Preserving Systems


The system architecture, prototype development, and laboratory testing phases have already been completed. The project is now entering its final research phase, which focuses on advanced data analysis, system validation, and high\-impact scholarly dissemination.



This position provides an opportunity for a highly motivated postdoctoral\-level researcher to contribute to a social\-impact technology initiative at the intersection of AI, digital health, and smart sensing systems.

  


**Duties and Responsibilities:**


* Analyze datasets collected from the deployed IoT sensing infrastructure.
* Apply machine learning and AI techniques for predictive modelling, pattern detection, and data interpretation.
* Evaluate model performance and overall system effectiveness using statistical and computational methods.
* Conduct performance evaluation of the developed IoT–cloud architecture.
* Assess system reliability, communication efficiency, and scalability.
* Support refinement of algorithms and system components as needed.
* Prepare technical documentation, experimental reports, and grant deliverables.
* Contribute to Research Manitoba reporting requirements.
* Document system architecture, methodology, and evaluation results.
* Lead or co\-author peer\-reviewed journal articles and conference papers.
* Contribute to research presentations and dissemination of findings.
* Support the translation of research outcomes into high\-impact publications.
* Collaborate with research investigators and team members.
* Participate in project meetings and contribute to research planning and dissemination.
  


**Qualifications:**


* PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Electrical/Computer Engineering, Health Informatics, Bioinformatics, or a closely related field.
* Demonstrated expertise in machine learning, artificial intelligence, or advanced data analytics.
* Experience with IoT architectures, sensor data, and/or cloud\-based data systems.
* Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, R, MATLAB, or similar scientific computing tools.
* Proven ability to conduct independent research and publish in peer\-reviewed venues.
* Excellent technical writing and scientific communication skills.
* Experience with health data analytics, medical informatics, or bioinformatics is an asset.
* Knowledge of IoT communication protocols and distributed computing systems is desirable.
* Experience with AI\-driven predictive modelling or health monitoring systems is beneficial.
* Prior involvement in interdisciplinary research, particularly in healthcare technologies, is preferred.


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**


This position **is not** eligible for extended health benefits.


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*


**Note:** The work described in this posting will be conducted in\-person.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion and recognizes that a diverse staff and faculty benefits and enriches the work, learning and research environments, and is essential to academic and institutional excellence. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and encourage women, racialized persons, Indigenous persons, persons with disabilities, and 2SLGBTQ\+ persons to confidentially self\-identify at time of application.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to ensuring employment opportunities are accessible for all applicants. If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.



The personal information of applicants is collected under the authority of the University of Winnipeg Act and 36(1\)(b) of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. All personal information collected via the recruitment process is used to assess the applicant’s suitability, eligibility, and qualifications for employment, and to otherwise support recruitment activities. This information will be provided to participating members of the recruitment process. Questions regarding the collection of your personal information may be directed to the Director, HR Services, 515 Portage Avenue, Winnipeg, MB, R3B 2E9 or human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.

**Qualifications**
------------------


**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING REQUIRED:**


* Masters degree in a related study area.

OR
* Bachelors degree or completion of a Community College course in a related discipline with three years \= directly\-related experience.
* Membership/Certification with SoCRA or ACRP preferred.
* Transport Canada Dangerous Goods Handling Certification preferred.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* Three years directly related **clinical** research assistant experience required.
* Previous experience coordinating research studies is required.
* Previous experience designing data capture tools (e.g. case report forms, data collection forms etc.) is required.
* Previous experience working with research patients/ participants, consenting for participation in studies, contacting patients/participants and scheduling research participants for study visits is required.
* Previous experience managing study documents in accordance with ICH\-GCP is required.
* Experience working with REDCAP or Electronic Data Capture software preferred.
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* Effective written and verbal communication skills are required.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team is required.
* Demonstrated ability in using computer software related to word processing (e.g. Microsoft Office, Outlook) required.
* Experience with statistical software (e.g. SPSS, SAS, N Vivo) an asset
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.


**OTHER JOB\-RELATED QUALIFICATIONS:**


* Proficiency in using the UM Libraries database and web\-based resources (PubMed, PsycINFO) is an asset.
**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


### **Responsibilities:**


* Coordinates and oversees clinical and health research studies with a child health focus including interventional studies and non\-interventional studies.
* Ensures patient safety and privacy protocols are adhered to.
* Ensures the research protocol is conducted in accordance with the research ethics board approved research protocol, ICH\-GCP (Good Clinical Practice) and TCPS2\.
* Directly performs, or directs Research Assistants in the recruitment, interviews, obtains assent/consent, screens and enrolls study participants; monitors and follows\-up with participants in the various research studies and monitors the overall progress of the studies.
* Ensures the scheduling of study visits in accordance with the protocol and ensures performance of required assessments are completed.
* Ensures the collection of data per the protocol and entry into the case report form or study specific electronic data capture tool (e.g. REDCAP) in a timely manner within guidelines while ensuring data quality, via data cleaning and query resolution.
* Ensures the safe collection, processing, storage and shipment of biological specimens, such as blood, urine and fecal matter, in accordance with the protocol, institutional policy, and legislation.
* Establishes and maintains the Trial Master File Binder; collects and retains essential documents in accordance with regulations.
* Ensures the development and maintenance of study materials including, assent/consent forms, data collection tools, case report forms, source documents, and other research project related materials such as Standard Operating Procedures (SOP'S).
* Interacts with various departments and outside agencies to clarify needs for various approvals and assists researchers in finding solutions to facilitating the project.
* Navigates and ensures preparation and submission to the appropriate regulatory authorities such as the Research Ethics Board(s), institutional impact, privacy and data access committee as well as ensuring compliance with on\-going submissions including amendments and annual approval as required.
* In consultation, develops a budget for a project or does a feasibility assessment of a study budget.
* Prepares and responds to enquiries from physicians or parents in respect of project studies.
* Coordinates drug accountability and randomization practices.
* Liaises with researchers, families, and youth.
* Responds to enquiries from physicians or parents with respect to the research project.
* Collaborates closely with other research staff and hospital personnel.
* Assists in the coordination of all study visits as required by study protocol.
* Provides input into the development of service staff work schedules by planning and analyzing work flow against deliverables and budget.
* Documents all adverse events (AEs), including serious adverse events (SAEs) and adverse drug reactions (ADRS) by completing the required AE/SAE/ADR report forms and submitting notification to the study sponsors and the research ethics board.
* Maintains/upgrades/attends educational events in relation to research.
* Liaises with the various groups to support research activities.
* Acts as primary liaison between pharmaceutical companies, physicians, patients and the primary investigator and acts as representative for primary investigator during visits of monitors to conduct audits.
* Supports and leads audit readiness within the CRU.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.

title: Research and Development (R&D) Process Technician
description:
**Company**

Carbon Lock Tech is a Manitoba\-based cleantech startup focused on addressing climate change through carbon capture and sequestration. The company is currently developing systems and technologies for converting organic matter into a stable form of biocarbon that can be sequestered or “locked” into a range of valuable applications.

**Responsibilities include**:

* Operate the patented pyrolysis reactor and auxiliary systems during test campaigns, including start\-up, steady\-state operation, shutdown, and routine equipment checks using common hand and power tools.
* Install, modify, and maintain piping, valves, flanges, and mechanical connections on the reactor and support equipment to enable different test configurations and ensure reliable operation.
* Implement and verify PLC and HMI changes required for new test campaigns, including adding or reassigning I/O, updating logic, and troubleshooting control\-related issues.
* Install, wire, configure, and calibrate field instruments (pressure, temperature, flow, level) and electrical components (motors, drives, control panels) to support evolving test and data needs.
* Upgrade and maintain data acquisition paths from instruments and PLCs, ensuring accurate signal routing, scaling, and recording for test analysis and reporting.
* Troubleshoot operational issues during campaigns by interpreting instrument trends and alarms, then performing safe hands\-on corrective actions (e.g., clearing blockages, tightening connections, replacing faulty components).

**Required skills and qualifications include**:

* Graduated from a Canadian recognized technology or engineering program
* Ability to work under minimal supervision, is a must
* Strong engineering fundamentals; reporting and communication (written/verbal) required

**Process**

* Applicants must reside in Manitoba and be willing and able to work in Headingley, Manitoba

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
Who We Are
  
Price Industries Limited, North America’s leading manufacturer and distributor of air distribution products (www.priceindustries.com). The founding principles of Price have never changed – business integrity, first\-class service, innovation and a commitment to people. Our dependable quality, reliable delivery, and knowledgeable follow\-up service have made Price a market leader in supplying air distribution, critical controls, and noise control products.
  
After more than 60 years, Price remains a privately held family company with a deep heritage and commitment to innovation and service. Our long\-standing vision, values, and the Price way of doing business are the cornerstones of our success.
  
We are a dynamic, progressive and innovative organization looking for new talent in our growing Winnipeg manufacturing facility. If you thrive on new challenges, and working in a friendly, team\-driven environment, you should consider the following opportunity.
  
What You Can Expect
  
* Health and Dental \- Employees and their families enjoy paid benefits covering an extensive list of medical and dental expenses and services.
* Employee and Family Assistance Program (EFAP) \- You and your family will have free access to consultation and support for your mental, emotional and financial well\-being
* PocketPills – You and your family will have free access to Canada’s first Digital Pharmacy, PocketPills enables you to call, text or email the pharmacy care team from wherever you are. You will enjoy free delivery of your prescriptions and vitamins straight to your home.
* Life and Disability \- You will be provided with life insurance in a multiple of your salary with an option to purchase additional coverage.
* Pension Matching Program \- Invest in your future with each pay cheque through our Defined Contribution Pension Plan and we will match your contribution (up to 3% of your salary).
* Gym Subsidy Program \- Employees are eligible for a gym membership reimbursement of up to $300 per year, this includes Fitness related App’s.
* As part of our Goals, Rewards, Improvement and Teamwork program qualifying teams can enjoy a monthly reward.
* Personal and Professional Development \- We are committed to helping you reach your potential through training and education.
* Community Involvement \- Generously supporting health, charities, youth, and education opportunities is our passion, and you’re invited to be a part of that.
* Our convenient Price Café offers free Tea and Coffee.


**The successful candidate's responsibilities will include:**  

* Enable reporting and analytics processes across enterprise systems (e.g., IFS Cloud ERP).
* Develop and maintain Power BI reports, dashboards, and KPI scorecards.
* Write and optimize SQL queries to extract, validate, and analyze data.
* Support internal teams in using reporting tools to meet business requirements.
* Translate reporting capabilities into clear processes, standards, and guidance.
* Assist in report design, execution, scheduling, refresh, and lifecycle management.
* Support reporting administration activities (imports/exports, archiving, report actions).
* Define, document, and maintain KPI definitions, logic, and reporting standards.
* Develop and continuously improve reporting documentation, work instructions, and training materials.
* Document reporting governance, including ownership, access controls, and best practices.
* Act as the primary contact for business users regarding reporting enablement and support.
* Gather reporting requirements and align business needs with system functionality.
* Deliver training sessions, walkthroughs, and workshops to support adoption and self\-service.
* Support onboarding through consistent documentation and knowledge transfer.
* Support future\-state analytics enablement processes and self\-service reporting frameworks.
* Participate in report testing, validation, and module enhancement activities.
* Develop and execute test cases to validate reporting outputs and configurations.
* Troubleshoot reporting issues and support root cause analysis.
* Liaise with IT teams and vendors to resolve reporting and analytics issues.
* Align business stakeholders and technical teams for reporting enhancements and changes.
* Track assigned tasks, timelines, risks, and deliverables to ensure project success.
* Support full\-cycle analytics implementations, including planning, execution, and post\-go\-live support.
* Proactively identify and address blockers, risks, and scope gaps.\\


Qualifications
  
  

**1\. Experience:**  

* 8\+ years of experience in business systems analysis, business process enablement, or analytics/reporting support roles.
* Experience supporting ERP reporting tools and analytics solutions in an enterprise environment.
* Proven ability to develop high\-quality documentation and training materials for business processes and system functionality.
* Strong experience working with stakeholders to translate business needs into effective reporting solutions.
* Experience supporting system implementations, upgrades, and process rollouts is a strong asset.
* Experience working with large enterprise datasets, performing data validation, and ensuring reporting accuracy.
* Proven ability to translate business requirements into SQL queries, data models, and BI solutions.


**2\. Education:**  

* Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field.
* A post\-secondary degree or diploma in Business Administration, Information Technology, or a related discipline, is an asset.
* Relevant Business or Systems Analysis certifications such as CBAP are highly desirable.


**3\. Technical Skills:**  

* Strong Power BI experience, including data modeling, DAX, Power Query, dataset refresh scheduling, and workspace management.
* Strong SQL skills for querying, validating, and troubleshooting enterprise data sources.
* Experience with ERP reporting tools and data structures (IFS experience preferred).
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel (pivot tables, advanced formulas) for data reconciliation and migration validation.
* Expertise in process mapping tools such as Visio, Lucidchart, or BPMN software and documentation standards.
* Familiarity with data warehouse concepts, dimensional modeling, and reporting layers is considered an asset.


**4\. Soft Skills:**  

* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, particularly for troubleshooting and system integration issues.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills to engage with technical teams, business leaders, and implementation consultants.
* High attention to detail, particularly in data validation and accuracy during software implementations.
* Ability to work in fast\-paced environments, manage multiple priorities, and support high\-impact software implementation initiatives.


**5\. Salary Range:**  

CAD $70,000– $90,000 per year

title: Disability Claims Analyst, Individual Claims
description:
**Job Description**

**What is the opportunity?**
============================


As a Disability Claims Analyst at RBC Insurance, you will be part of a supportive, multidisciplinary team with expertise across diverse fields dedicated to ensuring excellent client service while managing risk strategies that impact on the company’s reputation, regulatory risk, client relationships, financial results, and business retention.


Your role is a unique blend of analytical precision and heartfelt empathy – where every claim is unique, and your decision directly impact financial wellbeing and peace of mind of our clients who are the center of our claims.


Ready to make a real difference? Let’s work together to support our clients and strengthen our community!

**What will you do?**

* Adjudication: proactively evaluate individual disability claims using medical evidence, occupational insights, policy terms, amongst others, to determine benefit eligibility, ensuring decisions are objective, fair, and thoroughly documented
* Client Communication: Deliver clear, empathetic, and timely communication to clients \- whether sharing approvals or navigating sensitive denials.
* Critical Analysis \& Decision\-Making: Use critical thinking to make timely decisions based on your assessment of the claim.
* Negotiation and Resolution: Articulate claim decisions clearly in writing and verbally, addressing concerns to foster resolution and mutual respect.
* Claims Management: Efficiently manage a high\-volume of cases, calculate benefit payments and proactively support clients’ return\-to\-work journeys as applicable.
* Relationship Building: Develop strong relationships with clients and key partners to facilitate recovery and positive claim resolutions.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate seamlessly with cross\-functional teams – including medical, legal and client service experts throughout the claims journey to drive consistency and results.

**What will you need to succeed?**

**Must\-Have**

* Post\-secondary education or college diploma completed.
* Preferred background in healthcare, finance, or a related field: You’ll review medical and financial details as well as policy language.
* Exceptional client service and communication skills (both verbal and written in English), with the ability to navigate emotionally sensitive conversations with empathy and professionalism.
* Excellent problem\-solving and decision\-making skills with demonstrated ability to manage complex information and make evidence\-based decisions
* Strong analytical thinking, judgement and risk\-management mindset.
* Ability to work independently with strong organizational and time management skills to strategically deliver the greatest impact.
* Proficiency in digital tools, Microsoft Outlook, Excel, Word, and ability to navigate multiple systems.
* Basic math skills with an understanding of financial information.
* Ability to meet the technological and confidentiality requirements of the role (internet connectivity, and private space)

**Nice to have:**

* Experience in claims.
* Learning Agility, ability to adapt and manage change effectively.
* Familiarity with data analytics tools such as Tableau.
* Bilingual (English/French)

**What’s in it for you?**


We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

* A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits and competitive compensation
* RBCI software tools to boost your productivity
* Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
* Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high\-performing team
* Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
* Access to a variety of job opportunities across business and geographies
* Flexible work\-life balance options

**Job Skills**

**Additional Job Details**

**Address:**


MEADOWVALE BUSINESS PARK, 6880 FINANCIAL DR:MISSISSAUGA**City:**


Mississauga**Country:**


Canada**Work hours/week:**


37\.5**Employment Type:**


Full time**Platform:**


INSURANCE**Job Type:**


Regular**Pay Type:**


Salaried**Posted Date:**


2026\-04\-06**Application Deadline:**


2026\-04\-20**Note****:** *Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the day prior to the application deadline date above*

**Our Employment Opportunities**


At RBC, we are guided by living shared values of Client First, Integrity, Collaboration, Respect and Excellence and winning together as One RBC. We believe an inclusive workplace that has diverse perspectives is core to our continued growth as one of the largest and most successful banks in the world. Maintaining a workplace where our employees feel supported to perform at their best, effectively collaborate, drive innovation, and grow professionally helps to bring our Purpose to life and create value for our clients and communities. RBC strives to deliver this through policies and programs intended to foster a workplace based on respect, belonging and opportunity for all.

**Join our Talent Community**  

  

Stay in\-the\-know about great career opportunities at RBC. Sign up and get customized info on our latest jobs, career tips and Recruitment events that matter to you.  

  

Expand your limits and create a new future together at RBC. Find out how we use our passion and drive to enhance the well\-being of our clients and communities at jobs.rbc.com.


RBC is presently inviting candidates to apply for this existing vacancy. Applying to this posting allows you to express your interest in this current career opportunity at RBC. Qualified applicants may be contacted to review their resume in more detail.

title: Strategic Pricing Analyst - Term
description:
Status \- Term (1 year)
  

  

In your role as Strategic Pricing Analyst at The North West Company (NWC), you will be responsible for the execution of NWC pricing strategies, overseeing required pricing adjustments, and providing summary of financial performance metrics. This includes making retail decisions that adhere to established strategy, along with modeling of alternative outcomes as to support effective decision\-making.
  

  

Joining NWC gives you the opportunity for growth and development throughout the company, a competitive salary and benefits package, and to be part of a dynamic and diverse culture.
  

  

NWC is a leading retailer to under\-served rural communities and urban neighborhood markets in the following regions: Northern Canada, Western Canada, rural Alaska, the South Pacific islands, and the Caribbean. Our stores offer a broad range of products and services with an emphasis on food. Our value offer is to be the best local shopping choice for everyday household and local lifestyle needs. Check out northwest.ca for more information.
  

  

**Your Role:*** Uses the DemandTec Pricing tool to execute the category pricing strategy defined by the Category Managers (CMs).
* Assigned to specific banners and categories, collaborating with CMs to gain in‑depth understanding of each category’s pricing approach.
* Provides informed recommendations and constructively challenges CMs to ensure strong, banner‑aligned pricing strategies.
* Completes weekly price updates for assigned categories and reviews outcomes with the Category Manager.
* Analyzes weekly competitive checks and provides recommended pricing adjustments.
* Executes the category pricing strategy for Special Ship items.
* Works collaboratively with the Category Manager and Pricing Manager to establish and achieve category strategies, performance metrics, and financial goals.
* Oversee the timely delivery of competitive price indices and retail updates in coordination with the Pricing Coordinator and Pricing Manager.
Manages the prompt execution of Special Ship pricing in partnership with the Pricing Analyst and Coordinator.  
* 

  

  

  

**Desired Skills \& Experience:*** Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Data Science, Economics, or a College Business Administration diploma combined with 2\+ years of relevant experience
* Experience in analytical roles with strong technical capabilities, including Excel, PowerQuery, Access, and SQL.
* Demonstrates excellent critical thinking and problem‑solving abilities.
* Skilled in facilitation and presentations, with a proven ability to build and manage effective relationships.
* Highly organized with strong time‑management skills.
* Possesses strong financial acumen.
* Ability to quickly learn new concepts, systems and industry best practices and apply these learnings to the continued development of the team/ support of the business.
* Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and oral.
* Ability to prioritize and accomplish work independently and within a team to meet deadlines.
* Exceptional organizational and time management skills; and,
Ability to work well individually with minimal supervision, as well as part of a team.  
* 

  

  

**Location: Gibraltar House, 77 Main Street, Wpg., MB**  

**Reports to: Manager, Strategic Pricing**  

  

We create a collaborative and constructive culture by:
  

  

Being Enterprising • Committing to our Customer • Working with Passion • Leading and Developing Others • Demonstrating Respect and Inclusivity • Building Trust
  

  

The North West Company is committed to inclusion and diversity and encourages applications from all candidates including but not limited to: all religions and ethnicities, LGBTQ2s\+, BIPOC, and persons with disabilities.
  

  

**A competitive salary is being offered along with opportunities to learn, develop and advance throughout the organization. We thank all applicants for their interest; however only those selected for an interview will be contacted.**  

  

This is an active job opening for a position that is currently available and we are actively seeking to fill.
  

  

This position adheres to the Corporate Application Process Guidelines.

title: Fire Assay Technician - Drive-in/Drive-out
description:
**Company Description**  

We are SGS – the world’s leading testing, inspection and certification company. We are recognized as the global benchmark for quality and integrity. Our 99,600 employees operate a network of 2,600 offices and laboratories, working together to enable a better, safer and more interconnected world

 **Job Description**  

This position will be located in Red Lake, ON (16A Young St, P0V 2M0\), on a drive in/drive out basis (2 weeks on/off). 12\-hour day and night shifts required.


Accommodations and meal per diem will be provided.


This position will carry out Fire Assay techniques following written work instructions to determine various mineral and metal concentrations.

* Perform Fire Assay techniques, such as fluxing, fusing, and cupelling samples according to written procedures.
* Carry out routine equipment maintenance under direction of supervisor.
* Complete and maintain accurate, up to date work order documentation, fusion sheets and logs in every respect.
* Carries out routine equipment maintenance under the direction of his/her supervisor.
* Follow all Quality system protocols and report discrepancies to Supervisor.
* Maintain a clean work environment which includes proper disposal of crucibles, cupels and fire assay lead wastes.
* Organize and label waste disposal.
* May be asked to work in other areas of the Geochemistry laboratory sections when required.
* May be assigned and coordinate special or *ad hoc* projects as needed.

  

**Qualifications**  

* Education: High school diploma (or equivalent) is required.
* Experience: Minimum of 1 year of relevant laboratory work experience preferred or equivalent lab experience through education.
* Must be able to read, understand and follow work instructions in a safe, accurate and timely manner.
* Candidates must be proficient in using various type of computer software (Word, Excel, CCLAS, Oracle, etc.).
* Candidates must demonstrate verbal and written communication skills.
* The incumbent must have time management skills and strong attention to detail
* Work well under pressure
* Ability to manage and co\-ordinate multiple projects in fast\-paced, highly professional environment.
* Extended hours and shift work may be required.
* **Must be comfortable working with ovens and in high temperature environments.**
* **Good hand/eye coordination when handling samples \& lab equipment.**
* Ability to work well with others \& independently.
* Travel to other SGS locations or client location may be required from time to time.

  

**Additional Information** **Compensation:** $23 to $25 per hour

**Some benefit highlights:**

* 80% coverage Health, Dental and Vision
* Paid time off
* RRSP Program Eligibility


SGS is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and as such we recruit, hire, train, and promote persons in all job classifications without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression and Indigenous status, or any other characteristics protected by law.

Shared Health leads the planning and coordinates the integration of patient\-centered clinical and preventive health services across Manitoba. The organization also delivers some province\-wide health services and supports centralized administrative and business functions for Manitoba health organizations.

**Position Overview**
---------------------



Under the general supervision of the Charge or designate, the General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is responsible for clinical analysis and reporting on patient samples according to established protocols. The General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is also responsible for assisting in general lab projects such as SOPs, chemical inventories, etc. The incumbent will maintain a safe environment in accordance with professional and provincial guidelines and ensure compliance with quality assurance programs and maintain standards put forth by Accreditation. Functions are conducted in accordance with the Mission and Values of Shared Health Diagnostic Services, current policies, procedures and applicable legislation.


**Experience**
--------------


* Previous related experience preferred.
**Education (Degree/Diploma/Certificate)**
------------------------------------------


* Graduate of an approved Medical Laboratory Technology program.
* Successful completion of the CMLTM Prior Learning Assessment process for a discipline specialty, as deemed by the Employer may also be considered.
**Certification/Licensure/Registration**
----------------------------------------


* Current registration or eligible for registration with the College of Medical Laboratory Technologists of Manitoba (CMLTM) required.
* Current membership with the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science (CSMLS) considered an asset.
**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------


* Must be able to communicate effectively in English, both written and oral.
* Windows computer environment required.
* Microsoft Office programs preferred.
* Follow oral and written instructions precisely with attention to detail.
* Demonstrated ability to work both independently and as a team member.
* Proficient analytical skills.
* Work under pressure.
* Maintains confidentiality with all information entrusted to the position.
* Fluent language skill (written and oral) in both French may be required.
* Ability to perform duties as described.
**Physical Requirements**
-------------------------


* Working shifts, weekends and holidays may be required.
* Participating in standby and callback duty may be required.

title: Research Associate
description:
**Who Are We, And What Do We Do?**

At **Corteva Agriscience,** you will help us grow what’s next. No matter your role, you will be part of a team that is building the future of agriculture – leading breakthroughs in the innovation and application of science and technology that will better the lives of people all over the world and fuel the progress of humankind.


The CHRD **Research Associate** will assist with the day\-to\-day field research operations for the Integrated Field Sciences (IFS) unit of the Crop Health Research \& Development organization encompassing seed applied technology, biologicals, fertility enhancement, and crop protection research projects at internal and off\-site locations in Manitoba. The CHRD Research Associate will help lead the implementation of field experiments, assist with collecting and reporting data, and help train and manage the summer crew. This position will also be a partner to the commercial team at Corteva to support the demonstration and launch of our new product concepts. This role offers opportunities to build strong technical, operational, and leadership capabilities within the Integrated Field Sciences team. The CHRD Research Associate will gain exposure to multiple stages of the product development pipeline.


This is a full‑time, permanent role within CHRD, supporting Corteva’s long‑term CHRD discovery and development research efforts in Manitoba. This role involves a combination of office‑based planning and hands‑on field work. This position will require commuting regularly to Carman, MB. Seasonal workload variability is expected, with peak activity during the growing season. The position requires outdoor work in variable weather conditions and regular travel to internal and off‑site trial locations within the province of Manitoba. This role is ideal for candidates seeking a stable, long‑term career in applied field research within a collaborative and science‑driven organization.


**What You’ll Do:**

* Contributes to the overall planning and implementation of the field research program and ensures that local needs are incorporated as appropriate.
* Collaborate with team members to conduct field work in a timely fashion (i.e. land preparation, seeding, fertilizing, spraying, harvesting), including the transportation of equipment (i.e. trailering) to off\-site locations to perform field operations.
* Supervises the daily tasks of summer assistants and shares the responsibility to create an effective performance plan for each intern.
* Executes field research in a scientific manner, collects data in a timely fashion, and provides input into the final project summarizations.
* Provides day‑to‑day oversight of the Manitoba field equipment fleet, including routine inspections, basic upkeep, and coordinating servicing or repairs when required to ensure safe and reliable field operations.
* Demonstrates a strong commitment to safety and is an accountable advocate for the safety culture of Corteva Agriscience.
* Manages the day\-to\-day operation of the field research stations as well as the off\-site trials in Manitoba.
* Partners with cross‑functional teams including Regulatory, Supply Chain, and Commercial to support research objectives, product advancement, and demonstration and launch activities.

Success in this role is demonstrated by:


* Effective execution of assigned field trials in alignment with study protocols and timelines.
* Accurate, timely, and high‑quality data collection and reporting.
* Strong leadership, training, and supervision of seasonal field staff.
* Consistent demonstration of safety leadership and compliance with Corteva standards.
* Productive collaboration with R\&D and commercial partners.

**What You Need:**

The following requirements MUST be met for consideration of this position:


* Minimum Diploma in Agriculture or equivalent in a biology‑related science.
* Class 5 driver’s license and ability to operate field equipment.
* Demonstrated understanding of agronomic practices and safe pesticide use.

Preferred Qualifications and Assets:


* B.Sc. in Agriculture or related discipline.
* Class 3 driver’s license.
* Field research experience with data collection and interpretation.
* Experience coordinating or leading field trials and seasonal staff.
* Familiarity with ARM software and strong mechanical aptitude.

**\*No visa sponsorship or relocation is available for this position** .


**Benefits – How We’ll Support You:**

* Numerous development opportunities offered to build your skills
* Be part of a company with a higher purpose and contribute to making the world a better place
* Health benefits for you and your family on your first day of employment
* Four weeks of paid time off, plus paid holidays
* Excellent parental leave which includes 16 weeks of supplementary top\-up for both mothers and fathers.
* Future planning with our competitive retirement savings plan and tuition reimbursement program
* 
* Check out life at Corteva! www.linkedin.com/company/corteva/life

  

Are you a good match? Apply today! We seek applicants from all backgrounds to ensure we get the best, most creative talent on our team.

title: EDI Business Analyst
description:
**EDI Business Analyst \- (On Site/Hybrid)**

**Build an Aviation Career You’re Proud Of**

At StandardAero, we use our ingenuity and know\-how to find solutions for the simple to the most complex challenges in aviation. Together, we get the job done and done well. Our stability, resources, and respectful culture supports you in building a solid career with a great team you can count on day in and day out for the long term.


As an EDI Business Analyst, you will be responsible for supporting EDI integrations with StandardAero’s EDI trading partners to include EDI Value\-Added Networks (VANs).


The EDI Business Analyst will engage with both trading partners and internal teams to onboard new integrations, troubleshoot production issues, and continuously improve the processes and tooling that support EDI operations. Extensive EDI expertise, and familiarity with broader end to end business process flows (i.e., procure to pay, order to cash, acquire to retire) is essential.


This role serves as a bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, identifying and documenting requirements, troubleshooting issues, and managing delivery of bugs and enhancements that support StandardAero’s operational excellence.  



**What you'll do:**

* Responsible for EDI trading partner onboarding within Oracle EBS and Aeroxchange
* Manages day to day interactions with Aeroxchange to include new trading partner onboarding
* Monitor EDI error queues, and alert emails; take corrective actions promptly.
* Perform minor mapping updates and EDI configuration changes
Collaborate with cross‑functional teams including Supply Chain and IT ERP team personnel.  
* 

**Business Process Analysis**

* Work with business stakeholders to understand operational needs and translate them into functional requirements.
* Map current and future\-state processes, identifying opportunities for improvement or automation.
Support change management activities, including documenting training materials and assisting with user adoption.  
* 

**Enhancement \& Project Support**

* Participate in projects, upgrades, and integrations, ensuring requirements are clearly defined and validated.
* Collaborate with IT, Product Owner, PMO, and business groups to design, test, and implement new features and configurations.
* Perform functional testing, regression testing, and support UAT with end users.
* Assist in defining software development project plans, including scoping, scheduling, and implementation.
* Data \& Reporting
* Assist in developing dashboards, queries, and reports to support business decision\-making.
Ensure data quality and consistency across ERP modules and integrated systems.  
* 

**Required Qualifications:**

* Must be authorized to work in Canada and/or the U.S.
* Post\-Secondary Education in Business, Business Analysis, Computer Science, or Computer Engineering Technology
* 5\+ years of EDI Functional experience supporting Oracle EBS (version 12\.2 preferred)
* Strong functional knowledge of procure to pay process flows
* Experience working with EDI value added networks (VANs)
* Ability to analyze, troubleshoot, and document EDI mappings and processing flows
* Experience in eliciting requirements end\-users for insight on functionality, interface, problems, and/or usability issues.
Ability to translate business needs into clear requirements and documentation.  
* 

**Preferred Characteristics:**

* Experience and /or training in Agile software development
* ERP System experience (SAP, Oracle, etc.)
* Implemented solutions in ERP system, work orders, shop operations, supply chain, etc.
* Writing Oracle/SQL scripts, databases.
* Ideally 3\+ years experience in ERP space, 5 years IT overall
* Manufacturing environment experience or MRO facility preferred.
* Experience in aerospace, aviation MRO, or manufacturing environments.
* Familiarity with SQL queries, reporting tools, or system integrations.
* Strong written, oral, and interpersonal communication skills and to be able to communicate effectively with non\-technical staff.
* Keen attention to detail and proven analytical and problem\-solving abilities.
* Excellent problem\-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and stakeholder\-management skills.  
* 

**Benefits That Make Life Better**

* Comprehensive healthcare coverage
* Company pension with 100% match up to 5%
* Paid vacation starting on day one
* Bonus opportunities
* Health \& Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts
* Short\- and Long\-Term Disability
* Life \& AD\&D Insurance
Ongoing learning and training opportunities  
* 

**Raising the Standard of Excellence since 1911**

With over a century of proven excellence, StandardAero has become an industry leader in MRO services and customized solutions in the aerospace field. Our shared values and learning\-based culture inspire our team to exceed their potential and power our customers’ missions worldwide. With on\-the\-job training, advancement opportunities, and excellent benefits, StandardAero invites you to experience a fulfilling and meaningful career with us.


**Inclusivity Is Our Standard**

StandardAero offers equal employment opportunities for all. Our supportive environment celebrates diversity with no room for harassment or discrimination of any kind. We invite you to bring your authentic self to our team and experience our welcoming culture.

title: Youth Pickleball Program Coordinator (2 Positions)
description:
**Organization:** Pickleball Manitoba Inc. (PMI)  
**Location:** Various communities across Manitoba, Winnipeg  
**Term:** May 19th – August 14th, 2025 (12 weeks)

**Wage:** $18\.00/hr  
**Hours:** Full\-time (35–40 hours/week)

**Position Overview**

Pickleball Manitoba Inc. is seeking two enthusiastic and energetic summer students, preferably enrolled in Physical Education, Recreation Studies, Kinesiology, or a related field, to support the delivery of a province\-wide youth pickleball roadshow.

This exciting program introduces youth (ages 12–17\) to pickleball through fun, skill\-building clinics hosted in community centers and parks across Manitoba. The successful candidates will help plan, coordinate, and deliver engaging programming while promoting active living and sport development.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Assist in the planning and delivery of a 6\-week youth pickleball roadshow (July 2 – August 14th)
* Travel to communities across Manitoba to support and lead 1\-2\-day youth clinics
* Help instruct participants in fundamental skills (grip, stance, strokes, serving, gameplay)
* Lead warm\-ups, drills, and fun, skill\-based games
* Promote sportsmanship, teamwork, and a positive learning environment
* Support clinic setup, equipment management, and teardown
* Assist with participant registration and on\-site coordination
* Act as ambassadors for pickleball and Pickleball Manitoba
* Provide feedback and support continuous program improvement

**Qualifications**

* Currently enrolled in or recently completed a program in Physical Education, Recreation Studies, Kinesiology, or related field (preferred)
* Experience working with youth in sport or recreation settings
* Strong communication and leadership skills
* Energetic, positive, and team\-oriented attitude
* Ability to travel and work flexible hours, and prepared to stay overnight in provided accommodations while delivering programming in rural Manitoba if necessary due to clinic scheduling.
* Valid driver’s license (necessary)
* First Aid/CPR certification (asset)
* Experience with pickleball or other racquet sports is an asset, but training will be provided regardless.

**Program Details (What You’ll Help Deliver)**

* **Duration:** 12 weeks total with 6\-week youth roadshow in rural Manitoba beginning in June.
* **Format:** 1 to 2\-day clinics in various communities
* **Focus:** Skill development, fun, and introduction to gameplay
* **Activities Include:**
* Skill instruction (groundstrokes, volleys, serving, dinking)
* Movement and positioning drills
* Singles and doubles gameplay
* Fun, skill\-based games and competitions

**What You’ll Gain**

* Hands\-on experience in sport program creation following the Pickleball Long Term Athlete Development Program, program delivery and event scheduling and coordination
* Opportunity to travel and work in communities across Manitoba
* Leadership development and coaching experience
* Networking opportunities in sport and recreation sectors
* Pickleball NCCP coaching level 1, Respect in sport courses.

**How to Apply**

Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your interest and relevant experience to:  
**info@pickleballmanitoba.ca**

**Application Deadline:** April 24th, 2026

Job Types: Full\-time, Seasonal  
Contract length: 12 weeks

Pay: From $18\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Company car
* Flexible schedule
* On\-site parking

Work Location: In person

Project Manager (digital media)
description:
**About the Role**

Are you a detail\-driven project manager who thrives at the intersection of media, technology, and client experience? Talewind Digital is looking for a Project Manager to lead complex, multi\-disciplinary projects from intake through delivery — keeping scope, timelines, budgets, and clients all moving in the right direction.

In this role, you'll own end\-to\-end project delivery across our full range of services: media platform implementations, LMS deployments, onsite and virtual filming, livestreaming, video series, and podcast production. Working closely with our Account Management team, you'll be the operational engine behind exceptional client outcomes.

**What You'll Do**

**Project Delivery \& Client Satisfaction**

* Lead end\-to\-end delivery of media and technology projects, ensuring all work is on time, on scope, and on budget
* Partner with Account Managers to align delivery with client goals and success criteria
* Provide proactive, solutions\-oriented communication throughout the full project lifecycle
* Drive post\-project processes including client satisfaction follow\-up and reviews

**Scoping, Budgeting \& Financial Ownership**

* Lead requirements gathering, estimation, and resource planning for new projects
* Prepare detailed budget proposals and present scope and pricing recommendations alongside Account Managers or Sales
* Maintain accurate financial tracking, including margin monitoring and cost\-to\-complete analysis
* Manage scope changes through formal change order processes

**Planning, Execution \& Team Coordination**

* Lead project intake meetings to define goals, timelines, budgets, and available resources
* Coordinate internal teams, third\-party vendors, and client contributors using Talewind Digital's project management tools
* Run milestone and status reviews, keeping all stakeholders informed and aligned
* Identify risks early and take corrective action to keep projects on track

**Quality \& Client Growth**

* Define measurable success criteria with clients and ensure appropriate tracking is in place
* Oversee quality control for all deliverables — including media and digital assets — before client delivery
* Identify opportunities for client growth and surface them to Account Management or Business Development

**Leadership \& Collaboration**

* Serve as the primary project lead for strategic and high\-value clients
* Contribute insights during creative strategy sessions that help shape project direction
* Mentor Account Management team members on project management best practices and media production workflows

**What You Bring**

**Experience \& Education**

* 5\+ years of client\-facing project management or project coordination experience
* Post\-secondary education in Business, Marketing, Project Management, Video Production, Communications, or a related field
* Experience in video production, livestreaming, podcasting, SaaS/software deployments, or LMS systems is a strong asset

**Skills \& Knowledge**

* Strong understanding of media production, digital events, media platforms, and/or LMS systems
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a professional, adaptable tone
* Highly organized, proactive, and detail\-focused with solid project management fundamentals
* Proven ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously and lead cross\-functional teams

**AI Proficiency**

* Comfortable using AI tools (such as ChatGPT, Claude, Notion AI, or similar) to enhance productivity — including drafting project documentation, summarizing meeting notes, generating status reports, and streamlining repetitive tasks
* Able to critically evaluate AI\-generated outputs and apply sound judgment before sharing with clients or teams
* Curious about emerging AI applications in media production and project management, and willing to explore how new tools can improve workflows and client outcomes
* Understands the importance of data privacy and responsible AI use in a client\-facing environment

**Who You Are**

You're someone who naturally takes ownership, communicates with clarity, and keeps projects from going sideways before anyone else notices the risk. You're comfortable in fast\-paced environments, confident leading cross\-functional teams, and always looking for smarter ways to get great work done. You care about your clients' success as much as your own — and it shows in how you show up every day.

**Why Talewind Digital**

At Talewind Digital, we work at the cutting edge of media and technology — helping organizations bring their content and events to life in meaningful ways. You'll join a collaborative, driven team that values initiative, continuous improvement, and doing great work together.

* Work with a small, talented creative team where your input matters.
* Winnipeg\-based company, casual dress, hybrid work environment.
* Comprehensive benefits plan.
* Holiday closure from Christmas to New Years, so you can rest and recharge without using your vacation time.
* Free Parking
* Hybrid work \- 3 days in office, 2 days work from home

*Talewind Digital is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and encourage anyone who meets the qualifications to apply.*

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Winnipeg, MB R3L 0J4

Analyst I, QC Laboratory Services
description:
Preparedness today, safer tomorrow.
  
Emergent is a leading public health company that delivers protective and life\-saving solutions to
  
communities around the world. Here, you will join passionate professionals where our culture is informed by our values and commitment to protecting and saving lives.
  
Job Summary
  
This 6 month term perform's GMP compliant testing as required to support Raw Material testing, In\-process, Finished Product Release \& Stability, nonclinical / clinical studies, as well as assay or process validations.
  
  

Maintain laboratory documentation and perform preventative maintenance and calibration on laboratory equipment per established procedures.
  
  

Participate in the execution of investigations, deviations, critical reagent and standard qualifications, validation/verification protocols and reports, supplier qualifications, CAPAs and change controls.
  
Provide training to peers in an effective manner.
  
Responsibilities
  
Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Receive test samples, standards, chemicals, and reagents; maintain accurate laboratory records and inventory for all laboratory materials. Demonstrate competence when analytical testing with supervisory or senior analyst guidance. Be competent with analytical/biological/Compendia methodologies and techniques. Report results and complete LIMS data entry in a timely manner. Perform peer review of QC data, review, and approval in LIMS. Adhere to all effective STM’s/SOPs and safety requirements and document laboratory work consistent with cGMP requirements. Perform preventative maintenance / calibration on laboratory equipment in accordance with established procedures. Execute critical reagent and standard qualification studies. Maintain a clean and sanitary work area in accordance with standard laboratory practice and procedures. Make detailed observations during performance of experimental tasks. Contribute to troubleshooting/laboratory investigation efforts under supervisory or senior analyst guidance. Contribute to revision of assay forms, TMDs, and SOPs. Revise lab documentation including assay forms, test method documents, and standard operating procedures in accordance with document management procedures. Provide training in basic QC tasks to peers in an effective manner. Execute development and validation studies. Identify opportunities for improvements within QC procedures and documentation. Perform assigned tasks effectively with supervisor or senior analyst oversight.
  
The above statements are intended to describe the general nature of work performed by those in this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, and other duties may be assigned.
  
Education, Experience \& Skills
  
University degree or technical diploma in chemistry, biology, microbiology, or related field of study. 0\-2 years directly related experience in a recognized professional or technical/scientific field and experience in a pharmaceutical cGMP environment is an asset, but is not required. Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office \- Word, Excel. Basic knowledge of pharmaceutical GMPs. Knowledge of laboratory techniques and safety precautions. WHMIS and biohazard safety awareness. Competent in laboratory safety and generally\-accepted lab practice, including maintaining adequate written laboratory records. Ability to work in a fast paced and highly regulated environment. Able to multi\-task and adapt to changing priorities. Able to communicate and articulate information in both written and verbal forms. Strong organizational and interpersonal skills; can work collaboratively with others. Able to interpret analytical data. Able to contribute ideas/solutions to aid in troubleshooting and investigations. Ability to pass pre\-employment medical and background check.
