title: Summer Student - Lab Positions
description:
**May 4 – August 14, 2026 (15 Weeks)**



G.W. Seier founded Vita Health Products in Winnipeg, Manitoba in 1936\. Since then, the company has grown into one of Canada’s leading manufacturers of over\-the\-counter (OTC) pharmaceutical and natural health products.



Based in Winnipeg, IVC Vita Health Products is a vertically integrated manufacturer, marketer, and distributor of nutritional supplements and OTC medications. As part of the IVC Nutrition Corporation of Companies, we contribute to the Canadian healthcare system and bio\-economy by developing, manufacturing, and distributing high\-quality health products that support self\-care and wellness.



We are committed to supporting the next generation of talent by providing meaningful, hands\-on work experiences that build skills and prepare students for careers in the biotechnology, pharmaceutical, and healthcare sectors.


**Job Summary**



The Science Laboratory Technology Co\-op Student will support the Quality Control Laboratory team in performing analytical testing of raw materials and bulk products used in the production of natural health and pharmaceutical products.



This role offers practical, hands\-on experience in a regulated laboratory environment, providing exposure to industry\-standard instrumentation, quality systems, and Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP). The student will work under the guidance of experienced professionals and contribute to real\-world quality assurance processes within the healthcare and bio\-economy sector.


**Learning Outcomes \& Student Development**



By the end of the placement, the student will:


* Gain hands\-on experience with analytical instrumentation (HPLC, GC, FTIR, ICP, etc.)  

	+ Develop working knowledge of GMP, GDP, and Data Integrity principles in a regulated environment
	+ Build practical skills in wet chemistry and laboratory testing methodologies
	+ Learn how to document and interpret laboratory results in compliance with industry standards
	+ Gain exposure to quality control processes in pharmaceutical and natural health product manufacturing
	+ Strengthen problem\-solving skills through participation in investigations (e.g., OOS results)
	+ Develop professional communication and teamwork skills within a cross\-functional environment


**Responsibilities**


* Perform testing in accordance with Standard Test Methods (STM) and/or compendial methods  

	+ Conduct wet chemistry and spectroscopy testing on raw materials and bulk products
	+ Perform chemical identification testing following approved analytical procedures
	+ Operate laboratory equipment including NIR, FTIR, UV, Karl Fischer, Hardness Analyzer, Disintegration, Dissolution, HPLC, GC, and ICP (training provided)
	+ Prepare solutions and reagents required for laboratory testing
	+ Maintain accurate and complete documentation in compliance with cGMP, GDP, and Data Integrity requirements
	+ Report any out\-of\-specification (OOS) results or deviations promptly
	+ Assist in investigations and root cause analysis activities
	+ Follow all laboratory safety and quality standards
	+ Clean and maintain laboratory glassware and equipment
	+ Support additional laboratory and quality\-related activities as required


**Skills, Education \& Experience**


* Currently enrolled in a Canadian post\-secondary program in:
* Science Laboratory Technology
* Biotechnology
* Chemistry/Biochemistry
* Microbiology
* Pharmaceutical Sciences
* or a related STEM or healthcare field
* Strong attention to detail and organizational skills  

	+ Interest in laboratory work within pharmaceutical, biotechnology, or natural health product industries
	+ Basic laboratory knowledge (pipetting, solution prep, instrumentation) is an asset
	+ Familiarity with GMP or regulated environments is an asset


**Student Eligibility**

To be eligible for this position, candidates must:


* Be enrolled in a Canadian post\-secondary institution (college, undergraduate, or graduate program)  

	+ Be a Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or a protected person under Canadian law
	+ Be legally entitled to work in Canada
	+ Be returning to studies following the work term
	+ International students are not eligible for this role


**Term Details**


**Start Date:** May 4, 2026  

**End Date:** August 14, 2026  

**Duration:** 15 weeks  

**Schedule:** Full\-time (40 hours/week)


**Compensation:** $23–$25 per hour (based on skills and experience)


**Why Join IVC Vita Health?**


* Gain hands\-on experience in a regulated healthcare manufacturing environment  

	+ Work with advanced laboratory instrumentation and quality systems
	+ Receive mentorship and training from experienced professionals
	+ Contribute to products that support the health and well\-being of Canadians
	+ Build industry\-relevant skills aligned with careers in the bio\-economy and life sciences sector

title: Research Assistant, Endangered Bats in Central Canada
description:
Research Assistants are normally students, in a University of Winnipeg undergraduate program, employed to assist with tasks related to research. Work is performed within defined guidelines and under supervision of a more senior researcher or faculty member.


**Position Overview:**



We are hiring research assistants to collect data on endangered bat species in Canada.
  

Projects include radiotracking bats at maternity colonies and caves/mines scattered throughout Manitoba and Ontario. You will be maintaining equipment, capturing, measuring, radiotracking, and releasing bats.
  

  

You will primarily be camping or in cabins and away from Winnipeg for periods of 3\-6 weeks at a time.


**Duties:**


* Prepare field equipment supplies for field research
* Distribution, monitoring and maintenance of acoustic and microchip monitors
* Field research such as bat counts, radiotracking, capturing and measuring bats
* Assisting with data organization, management, and analysis
* Outreach activities such as presentations, both online and in person


**Qualifications:**


* Currently enrolled or completed degree in biology or related major


* Experience with camping and hiking
* Experience handling bats
* Proficiency with Microsoft word, excel and powerpoint


* Class 5 drivers license
* Ability to lift up to 30 lbs
* Willingness to work nights and weekends, potentially away from Winnipeg for extended periods of time (Up to 6 weeks at a time)
* Willingness to work outdoors in all weather conditions
* Ability to work alone and in a group
* Great communication skills, both written and verbal
* Highly motivated and ability to work with minimal supervision
* Rabies Vaccination


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**


The period of work will be from **June 1, 2026 \- September 18, 2026** and the estimated total hours of work will be **500\-560**.


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*


**Note:** The work described in this posting will be conducted remotely during this time /in\-person.

title: Research Assistant, Lean Programming and Combinatorics on Words
description:
Research Assistants are normally students, in a University of Winnipeg undergraduate program, employed to assist with tasks related to research. Work is performed within defined guidelines and under supervision of a more senior researcher or faculty member.


**Position Overview:**



The research assistant will use the Lean programming environment to axiomatize and prove theorems in combinatorics on words.


**Duties:**



The research assistant will use the Lean programming environment to axiomatize and prove theorems in combinatorics on words, such as the theorem of Fine\-Wilf, cube\-freeness of the Thue\-Morse words, etc.

Knowledge of functional programming such as Haskell. Experience working in LEAN and uploading libraries to GITHUB

title: HR Tech Business Analyst
description:
**Company Description** **Work with Us. Change the World.**


At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations.


There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world.


We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us.

 **Job Description**  

AECOM is seeking an **HR Tech Business Analyst** to be based in one of AECOM's locations in Canada. This position has the flexibility to work from a virtual / home location 100% of the time.

**Job Responsibilities include, but are not limited to the following:**

* Apply advanced understanding of Workday, ServiceNow, and MS Office.
* Conduct thorough research and analysis of system issues, while gathering data to support the development of new enhancements, service requests, and incident resolutions.
* Proactively identify and advocate progress towards small\-scale enhancement projects, including scoping requirements, coordinating cross\-functional teams, managing timelines, and ensuring successful delivery of solutions that align with business objectives.
* Practice strong responsiveness and attention to detail, ensuring timely follow\-up, accurate system updates, and high\-quality delivery of work.
* Support management of Americas regional ticket backlog, prioritizing enhancements and incidents to ensure timely resolution.
* Foster a global perspective by collaborating with colleagues across various regions to ensure alignment on international priorities and facilitate cohesive, globally integrated changes.
* Demonstrate awareness of Agile ways of working, supporting iterative delivery, cross\-functional collaboration, and continuous improvement.
* Represent business needs and facilitate end\-user feedback to influence Workday configuration and enable self\-service adoption.
* Support emerging AI capabilities and tools, with the ability to identify opportunities to enhance HR processes, insights, and user experience.
* Work closely with Workday Business Analyst Lead and the Workday technical support team to develop requirements, test configurations, and vet solutions with key regional stakeholders.
* Partner with People Services, HR, IT, and Finance to represent the Americas region’s requirements for Workday\-integrated systems, while leading change management and communication strategies to support system enhancements and releases.
* Ensure the Workday security model is configured to meet Americas region needs, including consulting with end users to develop requirements for new roles, decommission old roles, or edit existing roles.
* Maintain a strong understanding of strategic priorities and proactively influence stakeholders to remain aligned.
* Analyze data to identify patterns and trends that inform process improvements and system configurations, ultimately enhancing the overall user experience.

  

**Qualifications** **Minimum Requirements**

* Bachelor's and 4\+ years of relevant experience or demonstrated equivalency of experience and/or education.

**Relevant experience includes**:

* Demonstrated experience working with HR technology within a large, complex regional or global environment.
* Strong Workday HCM knowledge, including identifying and diagnosing system issues, partnering with stakeholders to gather requirements, and delivering enhancement solutions.
* Experience delivering small to medium\-scale system enhancements or improvement initiatives.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Familiarity with Agile ways of working, supporting iterative delivery, cross\-functional collaboration, and continuous improvement.
* Understanding of emerging AI capabilities and tools, with the ability to identify opportunities to enhance HR processes, insights, and user experience.

  

**Additional Information**  

* Relocation support is not available for this position.

**About AECOM**


AECOM is proud to offer comprehensive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees. Depending on your employment status, AECOM benefits may include medical, dental, vision, life, AD\&D, disability benefits, paid time off, leaves of absences, voluntary benefits, perks, flexible work options, well\-being resources, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, service recognition awards, retirement savings plan, and employee stock purchase plan.


AECOM is the global infrastructure leader, committed to delivering a better world. As a trusted professional services firm powered by deep technical abilities, we solve our clients’ complex challenges in water, environment, energy, transportation and buildings. Our teams partner with public\- and private\-sector clients to create innovative, sustainable and resilient solutions throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm that had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. Learn more at aecom.com.

**What makes AECOM a great place to work**


You will be part of a global team that champions your growth and career ambitions. Work on groundbreaking projects \- both in your local community and on a global scale \- that are transforming our industry and shaping the future. With cutting\-edge technology and a network of experts, you’ll have the resources to make a real impact. Our award\-winning training and development programs are designed to expand your technical expertise and leadership skills, helping you build the career you’ve always envisioned. Here, you’ll find a welcoming workplace built on respect, collaboration and community \- where you have the freedom to grow in a world of opportunity.


As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in your potential and are here to help you achieve it. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. Artificial intelligence will be used to support the screening, assessment, and selection of applicants for this role. Final hiring decisions are made by the hiring team.

title: SAP BUSINESS SYSTEMS ANALYST
description:
**SAP Business Systems Analyst**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



Under the direction of the SAP Business Systems Analyst Lead or a senior Business Systems Analyst, the Business Systems  

Analyst (BSA) Level II supports the adoption, configuration, and ongoing support of SAP HRMS solutions. This suite includes  

e\-Recruitment, Payroll, Employee Self Service (ESS), Manager Self Service (MSS), Organizational Management (OM), Training \&  

Event Management and related interfaces such as integration between LDAP directories and Microsoft Active Directory (AD).  

The BSA Level II participates in requirements analysis, configuration, testing, and process improvement activities, working  

collaboratively within the SAP Applications team. The role focuses on supporting existing HRMS functionality, assisting with  

enhancements, and contributing to solution implementation and product adoption efforts.  

As part of the ongoing S/4HANA implementation, the BSA Level II will support project activities related to SAP SuccessFactors and  

Employee Central Payroll (ECP). This includes assisting with functional design, integration support, data migration activities, and  

testing within a hybrid SAP landscape under the guidance of senior team members.


***Responsibilities:***


* Participate in system testing related to enhancements, defect resolution, upgrades, support pack installations, and SAP


projects.
* Assist with SAP system enhancements, including:
* Gathering and documenting business requirements
* Supporting the development of functional and technical specifications
* Coordinating with developers
* Developing and executing test scripts
* Analyzing test results
* Assisting with the rollout of system changes to users


* Work with internal and external partners (e.g., SAP, consultants, contractors) to support solution analysis and issue resolution


using standard SAP tools and functionality.

description:
Department of Psychology
Faculty of Arts

New Regular Continuing Part\-Time (Grant Funded)

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Melissa Duval \- melissa.duval@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------



**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING:**
* Undergraduate degree in psychology, social work, nursing, or a related health/social sciences field is required.
* Master's degree in is psychology, social work, nursing, or a related health/social sciences field is preferred.
* Undergraduate or Master's degree in psychology, social work, nursing, or a related health/social sciences field is preferred.

  


**EXPERIENCE:**
* Minimum of three years of directly related experience with at least one year of experience in psychology, education, social work, or related service settings required.
* Experience collaborating with a multi\-person team in an online or hybrid work environment strongly preferred.
* Experience with community\-based organizations supporting children and families preferred.
* Familiarity with REDCap or other electronic data capture tools preferred.
* Experience with project budget coordination and/or financial processing preferred.
* Experience working with websites or eHealth\-related platforms preferred.
* Experience with social media platforms (e.g., Instagram, Facebook, TikTok) is an asset.
* Experience supporting Indigenous family well\-being or working with Indigenous communities is an asset.
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**
* Strong interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to work respectfully with diverse teams and communities required.
* Excellent organizational, communication, and time\-management skills required.
* Ability to work both collaboratively and independently with minimal supervision required.
* Proficiency in using online communication and collaboration tools (e.g., Zoom, MS Teams, OSF, Slack, Google Docs) required.
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) required.
* Cultural safety and trauma\-informed communication skills considered an asset.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.


**OTHER JOB RELATED QUALIFICATIONS:**
* Must complete research ethics training (CORE) and personal health information training (PHIA).
* Must complete specialized research tools training as required by the project.
* Must successfully clear a Child Abuse Registry Check.
* Must complete the 8\-week anti\-racism training offered by the First Nations Health and Social Secretariat of Manitoba.
**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


* Coordinate program delivery, including meeting with participants and community members partners, reviewing questionnaire and focus group data, Lead the planning and coordination of community events and weekly programming at local community organizations, and discussing recommended family mental health resources.
* Coordinate, contribute to and be knowledgeable to the ethics\-approved project guidelines and follow all necessary procedures.
* Ensures the collection of data per the protocol and entry into the case report form or study specific electronic data capture tool (e.g. REDCAP) in a timely manner within guidelines while ensuring data quality, via data cleaning and query resolution.
* Maintains and updates existing research protocols with supervisory input on items such as curriculum material development, intervention, and ethics protocols.
* Liaises with the various groups to support research activities (University of Manitoba Departments ORS, REB and various HSC Departments, the Manitoba Métis Federation, the First Nations Health and Social Secretariat of Manitoba, etc.).
* Assists in participant recruitment efforts by coordinating with local service organizations and developing recruitment materials and packages and maintaining records of participant information and data.
* Culturally Safe Engagement: Ensure research practices and participant engagement are culturally appropriate and trauma\-informed, aligning with Indigenous community protocols and values.
* Training \& Mentorship: Support and mentor research assistants or trainees, particularly those from Indigenous communities, in data collection, recruitment, and program delivery.
* Logistics Coordination: Manage logistics related to research visits, such as travel arrangements for team members and community partners.
* Communicates with community service organizations and health providers about lab activities by phone, email, and in\- person meetings, as needed.
* Support knowledge dissemination through the creation of social media posts, public\-facing reports, and presentations relevant to Indigenous family mental health research.
* Capacity Building: Facilitate workshops or training sessions to build local capacity for delivering or sustaining the program.
* Co\-creation of Knowledge Products: Collaborate with community partners in developing culturally relevant materials (e.g., infographics, policy briefs, plain\-language summaries).
* Feedback Loops: Organize feedback sessions with participants and community stakeholders to refine program delivery.
* Monitors budget and expenditures in support of research activities, trainee appointments, and participant compensation, and other operating costs.
* Performs other duties as assigned or required related to the daily functioning of the research team. (e.g. ordering supplies, helping to prepare ethics submissions, proof\-reading documents, supporting the development of grant submissions, purchasing paperwork, travel planning, scheduling meetings).
* Compliance \& Reporting: Prepare progress reports for funding agencies and community partners, ensuring alignment with grant and ethics requirements.
* Data Governance: Adhere to principles of Indigenous data sovereignty (e.g., OCAP® or equivalent) when managing research data.
* Documentation: Maintain comprehensive project records including meeting minutes, correspondence, and version control for materials.
* Other duties as assigned.
**Additional information**
--------------------------

  



The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).

title: Expression of Interest - Project Managers
description:
**Corporate Profile**

Manitoba Hydro International Ltd. (MHI) provides innovative solutions to help customers in the energy and telecommunications sectors. We are a subsidiary of one of Canada’s most highly regarded energy utilities, Manitoba Hydro, and proudly bring over a century’s worth of utility best practice experience and our global expertise to every customer we serve. For more information, visit mhi.ca

The Manitoba Hydro International Utility Services (MHIUS) division assists power utilities, governments, and private sector clients around the world in the efficient, effective, and sustainable delivery of electricity. Utilizing the experience and human resources of its parent company, Manitoba Hydro, MHIUS provides electric utility expertise in the planning, design, construction supervision, and operations management of generation, transmission, distribution, and commercialization to its international clients. In response to current industry trends, MHIUS has developed specialized expertise in the areas of utility management and training services. MHIUS has established itself as an ethical, environmentally responsible provider of high\-quality utility services to the international power sector for over four decades.

Please refer to www.mhi.ca for more information.

**Job Description**

Manitoba Hydro International (MHI) is seeking qualified Project Managers for consulting contracts. As a Project Manager, you will be responsible for project related services from the pre\-planning phase through to the closure for assigned projects as well as providing support to the PMO. Projects may be part\-time or full\-time and may require international travel. Services are on as needed basis, commencing in summer 2026\.

Please express your interest by including your cover letter, daily rate, and CV including a detailed list of projects managed by **Friday, May 8, 2026**.

The ideal candidate will possess:

* A four\-year degree in a relevant discipline from an institution of recognized standing.
* Additional project management training, PMP considered an asset.
* Strong organizational and leadership abilities.
* Strong computer skills including experience with scheduling software and the Microsoft Office suite of software.
* Mature judgement and proven ability to carry out assigned duties with minimum direction and supervision.
* Demonstrated ability in providing mature leadership and delegating authority related to planning, organizing, and motivating the activities of a group of engineers and technical support staff.
* Demonstrated application of Project Management theory including scoping, estimating, scheduling, cost control, risk management and change control in a consulting environment.
* Possess and maintain valid Passport.

This expression of interest (EOI) will be used to evaluate the level of interest in these positions and used to recruit for current and subsequent vacancies over the next year. Until the scope of interest is determined, there is no commitment to make any selections from this EOI.

This EOI is being posted for consideration in conjunction with a posting on MERX. Selections will be made from both postings.

Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time, Fixed term contract

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Winnipeg, MB R3P 1A3

Shared Health leads the planning and coordinates the integration of patient\-centered clinical and preventive health services across Manitoba. The organization also delivers some province\-wide health services and supports centralized administrative and business functions for Manitoba health organizations.

**Position Overview**
---------------------



Under the general supervision of the Charge or designate, the General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is responsible for clinical analysis and reporting on patient samples according to established protocols. The General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is also responsible for assisting in general lab projects such as SOPs, chemical inventories, etc. The incumbent will maintain a safe environment in accordance with professional and provincial guidelines and ensure compliance with quality assurance programs and maintain standards put forth by Accreditation. Functions are conducted in accordance with the Mission and Values of Shared Health Diagnostic Services, current policies, procedures and applicable legislation.


**Experience**
--------------


* Previous related experience preferred.
**Education (Degree/Diploma/Certificate)**
------------------------------------------


* Graduate of an approved Medical Laboratory Technology program.
* Successful completion of the CMLTM Prior Learning Assessment process for a discipline specialty, as deemed by the Employer may also be considered.
**Certification/Licensure/Registration**
----------------------------------------


* Current registration or eligible for registration with the College of Medical Laboratory Technologists of Manitoba (CMLTM) required.
* Current membership with the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science (CSMLS) considered an asset.
**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------


* Must be able to communicate effectively in English, both written and oral.
* Windows computer environment required.
* Microsoft Office programs preferred.
* Follow oral and written instructions precisely with attention to detail.
* Demonstrated ability to work both independently and as a team member.
* Proficient analytical skills.
* Work under pressure.
* Maintains confidentiality with all information entrusted to the position.
* Fluent language skill (written and oral) in both French may be required.
* Ability to perform duties as described.
**Physical Requirements**
-------------------------


* Working shifts, weekends and holidays may be required.
* Participating in standby and callback duty may be required.
* Rotate through various workstations as required.
* Exposure to all hazards and dangers incumbent in working in a laboratory.
* For more definitive information, refer to the Job Hazard Analysis Assessment of this position within the specific laboratory.

The successful candidate will be responsible for any service charges incurred. title: Research Associate, "What They Can Teach Us" Lesson Plan Creation
description:
**\*This is a term position ending December 31, 2026\***


**Research Associates:**



Under the general direction of, and in collaboration with researchers and faculty members, positions at this level apply extensive knowledge in their field to perform high\-level, complex research activities, and will normally hold a Master’s degree. Individuals with training beyond a Master’s degree who have limited experience may also be appointed into this classification level.


**Position Overview:**



The Research Associate is responsible for creating a series of detailed lesson plans relating to the content of the “What They Can Teach Us” website and podcast series. The lesson plans should vary in length and complexity and be informed by the Manitoba K\-12 curriculum, particularly addressing schools that offer German language education.


**Duties and Responsibilities:**


* Determine the appropriate methodology to carry out identified research goals.
	+ The Research Associate should have a comprehensive understanding of the Manitoba K\-12 curriculum which will inform their lesson planning and the plans’ educational outcomes.
* Interpret research findings and perform editorial tasks.
	+ The RA will undertake a thorough analysis of the source material available on the “What They Can Teach Us” website and within the podcast episodes, integrating their findings into the lesson plans.
* Organize and host seminars, focus groups, community meetings, special events, and other forms of public engagement.
	+ The RA may assist in presenting their lesson plan offerings to local educational professionals via short in\-person or virtual presentations, or via email.
* Contribute to overall activities and internal operations related to the research project.
	+ The RA may be consulted on the placement and overall accessibility of lesson plans on the website as well as on their implementation by educational professionals.
* Perform other duties, including administrative support, as required or assigned.


**Qualifications:**


* Master’s degree, preferably in a Humanities\-related field
* Experience conducting independent research
* Extensive knowledge of the Manitoba K\-12 curriculum
* Experience in the field of German language education
* Experience developing manuals and lesson plans, including pre\-activity; activity; post\-activity; assessment and rubrics
* Experience designing lesson plans for different educational levels (elementary, middle and high school preferred)
* Ability to suggest different uses of the same material for different subjects (math, music, geography)
* Familiarity with Historical Thinking Concepts
* Strong written and verbal communication in English
* Strong written and verbal communication in German
* Strong written and verbal communication in French an asset
* Competence with Microsoft Office and Google suites


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*

title: Senior Research Assistant, Thin Films for UltraCold Neutron Storage
description:
Senior Research Assistants are normally graduate students, or University of Winnipeg undergraduate students, employed to assist in the execution and evaluation of research projects in their area of study. The positions may oversee Research Assistants in areas such as the use of proper techniques, analysis, and documentation.



Senior Research Assistants work under the general supervision or direction of more senior researchers or faculty members, and are expected to exercise judgment in prioritizing, planning and organizing their own work within defined parameters.


**Position Overview:**



The researcher will assist pulsed laser deposited coatings made in the UCN Guide Coating Facility at the University of Winnipeg. They will be responsible the creation of the coatings, repairing/improving coating equipment and processes and assist in coating quality data collection and analysis. Travel may also be required to test the coatings in Canada and Internationally.


**Duties:**


* Assist with the "tube" coating responsibilities of the facility, which entails running the Excimer laser, loading and unloaded tubes to be coated, monitoring the facility while the coating is happening, and performing coating evaluation analysis.
* The person with also help supervise other research personnel in the building and commissioning of a new vacuum chamber in the facility.


**Qualifications:**


* Physics or engineering major or degree recipient who has taken UW\-PHYS\-3901 or equivalent and earned a minimum of an A.
* Experience with pulsed laser deposition of thin films.
* Experience with surface science and adhesion testing of thin films.
* Python programming language and experience working with an Arduino.
* Ability to lift 25 lbs.


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**
  


The period of work will be from **May 4, 2026 \- August 28, 2026**, and the estimated total hours of work will be **560 hours.**


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*

description:
**CancerCare Manitoba** is a provincial agency which provides clinical and academic services and leadership for cancer control and the treatment of blood disorders. Our shared vision of a **world free of cancer** drives every member of our team. CancerCare Manitoba is the perfect place to thrive if you're seeking a fulfilling career in an organization that is committed to its employees' well\-being and professional development.

  


**Competition \# 132**\-26 761020306


**Posting End Date:** April 17, 2026


**City:** Winnipeg


**Work Location:** MacCharles

**Union:** Non\-Union

**Job Summary:**

  



The Clinical Research Assistant will support the Manitoba Tumour Bank and the Paul Albrechtsen Research Institute at CancerCare Manitoba (CCMB).  




The Clinical Research Assistant will primarily be responsible for:


* Screening and recruiting new project participants
* Assisting with organization and scheduling of study appointments
* Obtaining informed consent from eligible participants
* Assisting in the collection of biospecimens from patients


This position will actively ensure adherence to research protocols, procedures, reporting requirements and/or facility regulations are being followed. The Clinical Research Assistant will also be assisting with research project administrative activities.

  


**Qualifications:**

  


**Required:**

* Undergraduate degree in a health\-related field


\-or\-
* A diploma in a health\-related field with a minimum of 3 years of experience in a research environment.


An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

  


*Skills and Abilities*

  


* Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills.
* Ability to communicate effectively with a multidisciplinary team
* Comprehensive knowledge of Microsoft Office programs (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
* Strong time management skills, attention to detail, ability to multi\-task, flexibility, initiative and independent decision\-making skills.
* Critical/analytical thinking skills.
* Strong problem\-solving skills.

title: International Finance & Business Systems Analyst
description:
**Who We Are:**  

Price Industries Limited, North America’s leading manufacturer and distributor of air distribution products (www.priceindustries.com). The founding principles of Price have never changed – business integrity, first\-class service, innovation and a commitment to people. Our dependable quality, reliable delivery, and knowledgeable follow\-up service have made Price a market leader in supplying air distribution, critical controls, and noise control products.
  
After more than 60 years, Price remains a privately held family company with a deep heritage and commitment to innovation and service. Our long\-standing vision, values, and the Price way of doing business are the cornerstones of our success.
  
We are a dynamic, progressive and innovative organization looking for new talent in our growing Winnipeg manufacturing facility. If you thrive on new challenges, and working in a friendly, team\-driven environment, you should consider the following opportunity.
  
  

**What You Can Expect:**  

* Health and Dental \- Employees and their families enjoy paid benefits covering an extensive list of medical and dental expenses and services.
* PocketPills \- You and your family will have free access to Canada’s first Digital Pharmacy, PocketPills enables you to call, text or email the pharmacy care team from wherever you are. You will enjoy free delivery of your prescriptions and vitamins straight to your home.
* Employee and Family Assistance Program (EFAP) \- You and your family will have free access to consultation and support for your mental, emotional and financial well\-being
* Life and Disability \- You will be provided with life insurance in a multiple of your salary with an option to purchase additional coverage.
* Pension Matching Program \- Invest in your future with each pay cheque through our Defined Contribution Pension Plan and we will match your contribution (up to 3% of your salary).
* Gym Subsidy Program \- Employees are eligible for a gym membership reimbursement of up to $300 per year, this includes Fitness related App’s.
* As part of our Goals, Rewards, Improvement and Teamwork program qualifying teams can enjoy a monthly reward.
* Personal and Professional Development \- We are committed to helping you reach your potential through training and education.
* Community Involvement \- Generously supporting health, charities, youth, and education opportunities is our passion, and you’re invited to be a part of that.


Location
  
  

Winnipeg, MB
  
  

Reports To
  
  

Vice President, International
  
  

Position Summary
  
  

The International Finance \& Business Systems Analyst works closely with the Vice President, International to support financial oversight of the company’s international operations and to lead hands on business systems implementation initiatives.
  
  

This role combines financial analysis and coordination across multiple international entities with direct responsibility for implementing new business systems as the organization transitions away from legacy platforms that are no longer supported. The position requires a practical, detail oriented professional who is comfortable operating across finance, operations, and systems, and who can drive implementation work from requirements definition through go live and stabilization.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

International Finance \& Reporting
  
  

* Provide financial analysis and insight for the company’s international entities, including review of monthly results, trends, and variances.
* Prepare consolidated international financial reporting on a monthly basis for internal management and leadership use.
* Act as a point of coordination with international accounting firms, supporting statutory reporting, local compliance, and ongoing accounting matters.
* Support periodic financial reviews, assessments, and ad hoc analysis related to international operations.
* Manage and administer the company’s international credit insurance policy, including reporting requirements, limit monitoring, and coordination with insurers and brokers.
* Support continuous improvement in international financial processes, reporting consistency, and documentation.


Business Systems Implementation \& Process Design
  
  

* Lead hands on implementation of new business systems, from requirements definition through configuration, testing, and go live support.
* Work directly with business users and functional leaders to gather, document, and validate detailed business and functional requirements.
* Develop and maintain current state and future state process maps, ensuring alignment between business needs and system design.
* Act as a primary business owner for system implementation activities, coordinating closely with software vendors, implementation partners, and internal stakeholders.
* Translate business requirements into practical system configuration decisions and implementation approaches.
* Participate actively in data preparation, migration planning, validation, and reconciliation to ensure data accuracy and integrity during system transitions.
* Develop and execute user acceptance testing (UAT) plans, working hands on with end users to validate system functionality and usability.
* Support change management activities, including documentation, training materials, and end user support during rollout.
* Identify implementation risks, process gaps, and dependencies, and work proactively to resolve issues.
* Support post implementation stabilization, issue resolution, and continuous improvement of systems and processes.


Qualifications \& Experience
  
  

* Minimum 5 years of experience in a finance and/or accounting role, preferably within a multi entity or international environment.
* Strong financial analysis skills with experience preparing and interpreting management level financial reports.
* Experience working with external accounting firms, auditors, or financial advisors.
* Hands on experience with business system implementation, including requirements gathering, process mapping, testing, and deployment support.
* Working knowledge of process mapping techniques and documenting end to end business workflows.
* Comfortable working with technical system concepts and collaborating closely with vendors or implementation partners.
* Ability to bridge finance, operations, and systems, translating business needs into clear, implementable solutions.
* Intermediate to expert level Excel skills.
* Strong organizational and communication skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and workstreams simultaneously.


Key Attributes
  
  

* Detail oriented with a strong analytical and problem solving mindset.
* Practical and solutions focused, with a willingness to work hands on through implementation challenges.
* Comfortable operating in environments undergoing system change and modernization.
* Collaborative and able to work closely with senior leadership and cross functional teams across regions.


Should this excellent opportunity interest you, please submit your resume, including salary expectations.
  
  

We appreciate the interest shown by all applicants, however only those being considered for an interview will be contacted.
  
  

We welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need.

Job Summary
  
We are seeking a Cyber Security Analyst to strengthen our operational security capabilities and support our overall cybersecurity posture. This role focuses on day\-to\-day security operations, policy development, and awareness initiatives, enabling senior team members to concentrate on strategic priorities.
  
  

**The Successful candidate's responsibilities will include:**  

Operational Security Support
  
* Monitor and review alerts in Sophos Central and other security platforms
* Investigate suspicious emails and assist with email security reviews
* Investigate and respond to cybersecurity incidents including malware outbreaks, phishing attempts, and unauthorized access
* Support software requests by reviewing for security and compliance concerns
* Assist in reviewing and granting permissions for enterprise applications or integration requests
* Monitor risky users/sign\-ins and accounts missing MFA configurations
* Assist in cleaning up and optimizing policies/configurations (Sophos, Azure, Mimecast, Active Directory, etc.)
* Participate in Zscaler ZIA testing, troubleshooting, and communications
* Review vulnerability scans and coordinate them into actionable tasks


Security Awareness \& Training
  
* Administer and monitor phishing simulation campaigns
* Create phishing templates and follow\-up webinars for user education
* Develop content for security awareness initiatives (e.g., Cybersecurity Awareness Month)


Policy \& Standards Development
  
* Contribute to the review and improvement of cybersecurity standards and playbooks
* Assist in updating the Business Continuity Plan (BCP), Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP), and Cyber Incident Response Plan (CIRP)
* Communicate changes across the organization


Collaboration \& Continuous Improvement
  
* Work with IT infrastructure and application teams to embed security into system designs
* Research emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and cybersecurity trends
* Propose enhancements to security processes, playbooks, and incident response strategies


**The ideal candidate for this position will have:**  

* Diploma or degree in Information Security, Computer Science, or a related field
* Exposure to cybersecurity tools and operational tasks (Sophos, Zscaler, MFA, phishing campaigns)
* Strong attention to detail and ability to follow security protocols
* Proactive, collaborative, and eager to learn advanced security concepts


Preferred Skills
  
* Experience with Microsoft Defender, Azure AD, Exchange, Intune, and other Microsoft platforms
* Experience with Secure Email Gateway solutions
* Knowledge of scripting (PowerShell, Python) for automation
* Familiarity with Identity \& Access Management (IAM) processes

title: Bilingual Disability Claims Analyst, Individual Claims
description:
**Job Description**

**What is the opportunity?**


As a Bilingual Disability Claims Analyst at RBC Insurance, you will be part of a supportive, multidisciplinary team with expertise across diverse fields dedicated to ensuring excellent client service while managing risk strategies that impact on the company’s reputation, regulatory risk, client relationships, financial results, and business retention.


Your role is a unique blend of analytical precision and heartfelt empathy – where every claim is unique, and your decision directly impact financial wellbeing and peace of mind of our clients who are the center of our claims.


Ready to make a real difference? Let’s work together to support our clients and strengthen our community!

*The role is available in the following provinces: Quebec, Ontario, New\-Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Alberta and Manitoba*

**What will you do?**

* Adjudication: proactively evaluate individual disability claims using medical evidence, occupational insights, policy terms, amongst others, to determine benefit eligibility, ensuring decisions are objective, fair, and thoroughly documented
* Client Communication: Deliver clear, empathetic, and timely communication to clients \- whether sharing approvals or navigating sensitive denials.
* Critical Analysis \& Decision\-Making: Use critical thinking to make timely decisions based on your assessment of the claim.
* Negotiation and Resolution: Articulate claim decisions clearly in writing and verbally, addressing concerns to foster resolution and mutual respect.
* Claims Management: Efficiently manage a high\-volume of cases, calculate benefit payments and proactively support clients’ return\-to\-work journeys as applicable.
* Relationship Building: Develop strong relationships with clients and key partners to facilitate recovery and positive claim resolutions.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate seamlessly with cross\-functional teams – including medical, legal and client service experts throughout the claims journey to drive consistency and results.

**What will you need to succeed?**

**Must\-Have**

* Post\-secondary education or college diploma completed.
* Preferred background in healthcare, finance, or a related field: You’ll review medical and financial details as well as policy language.
* Bilingualism (English and French) required, as you will regularly serve our clients and do business with RBC partners and/or employees across Canada with English and French speaking needs
* Exceptional client service and communication skills (both verbal and written), with the ability to navigate emotionally sensitive conversations with empathy and professionalism.
* Excellent problem\-solving and decision\-making skills with demonstrated ability to manage complex information and make evidence\-based decisions
* Strong analytical thinking, judgement and risk\-management mindset.
* Ability to work independently with strong organizational and time management skills to strategically deliver the greatest impact.
* Proficiency in digital tools, Microsoft Outlook, Excel, Word, and ability to navigate multiple systems.
* Basic math skills with an understanding of financial information.
* Ability to meet the technological and confidentiality requirements of the role (internet connectivity, and private space.

**Nice to have:**

* Experience in claims.
* Learning Agility, ability to adapt and manage change effectively.
* Familiarity with data analytics tools such as Tableau.

**What’s in it for you?**


We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

* A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits and competitive compensation
* RBCI software tools to boost your productivity
* Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
* Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high\-performing team
* Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
* Access to a variety of job opportunities across business and geographies
* Flexible work\-life balance options

At RBC, we are guided by living shared values of Client First, Integrity, Collaboration, Respect and Excellence and winning together as One RBC. We believe an inclusive workplace that has diverse perspectives is core to our continued growth as one of the largest and most successful banks in the world. Maintaining a workplace where our employees feel supported to perform at their best, effectively collaborate, drive innovation, and grow professionally helps to bring our Purpose to life and create value for our clients and communities. RBC strives to deliver this through policies and programs intended to foster a workplace based on respect, belonging and opportunity for all.

title: Project Engineer
description:
Job Description  


Project Engineer
Job Location: Steinbach, Manitoba, CAN
Job Requisition ID: 15077
Join our global diversified pharmaceutical company enriching lives through our relentless drive to deliver better health outcomes to our patients. We are all in it together to make a difference. Be a part of a culture that doesn't just wait for change but actively creates it—where your skills and values drive our collective progress and impact.

  

**ROLE TITLE:** **PROJECT ENGINEER**


**DEPARTMENT:** **ENGINEERING**

**REPORTS TO:** **SENIOR MANAGER, ENGINEERING**

**HOURS****:** **8:00 – 16:30\-STEINBACH, MANITOBA**



We are seeking a Project Engineer to work within a diverse engineering team. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Engineering you will serve as a site Subject Matter Expert (SME) for major facility projects and systems, providing technical guidance and project execution for the Engineering team. This role is responsible for managing complex projects, from defining user requirements and evaluating vendors, to overseeing implementation, commissioning, and performance assessments.
 

**Responsibilities**:


* Lead engineering and site\-wide projects related to facility and utility systems, applying sound project management methodologies and documentation to ensure successful project delivery (scope, user requirements, risk management, scheduling, budgeting, resourcing).
* Ensure all new facility and utility systems are designed with robust, engineered safety controls. Verify and document the effectiveness of all safety systems during installation and commissioning to uphold site safety standards.
* Serve as SME for OSD facility systems such as HVAC, purified water, environmental controls, compressed air, and building automation; provide guidance and technical support to the engineering team.
* Ensure compliance with cGMP, regulatory standards (e.g. Health Canada, FDA, EMA), and company policies by supporting inspections, audits, investigations, CAPA development, and Change Requests.
* Conduct engineering studies to develop and implement improved methods of operation or processing for new and existing systems, maximizing efficiency in production through technology, equipment, and energy optimization.
* Prepare and maintain accurate engineering documentation such as requirements documents, PM records, calibration plans, engineering studies, and detailed drawings, ensuring data integrity and regulatory readiness.
* Champion continuous improvement by identifying and executing CI projects, performing cost\-benefit and payback analyses utilizing Lean methodologies as appropriate.
* Promote and uphold site\-wide occupational health, safety, and wellness standards by participating in and leading safety programs and initiatives.
* Foster a culture of teamwork, proactive problem\-solving, and knowledge\-sharing within the engineering function and across departments.

  


**Education and Experience**:
* Bachelor’s degree in science \- eligible for registration with APEGM\- preferably with a focus on mechanical or industrial engineering.
* Minimum 3 years experience of related manufacturing experience as an Engineer
* Demonstrated leadership and project management skills.
* Demonstrated ability to work under minimal supervision.
* Able to management multiple concurrent projects and work in a fast\-paced environment.
* Working knowledge in Microsoft Office is required; CMMS (Computerized maintenance Management Software) is an asset.
* Experience running technical projects within a manufacturing environment
* Pharmaceutical or food manufacturing experience is an asset

  


**Required competencies/Abilities**:



Additionally, successful candidates will demonstrate:
* Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines and commitments.
* Excellent decision\-making and problem\-solving skills
* Be a strong collaborator and work effectively with all levels of employees
* Able to initiate and lead change
* Have a high level of verbal and written communication skills
* Have the capability to manage multiple tasks

  



**Why Bausch Health?**  


Bausch Health is a dynamic, growing company with a dedicated team committed to excellence and to helping Canadians by providing them with important, high\-quality healthcare solutions. It is a progressive, exciting place to work where opportunities abound for current and new employees to help make a difference for Canadians.  



We strongly believe the success of Bausch Health coincides with the achievement of personal and professional goals. We are a progressive, exciting place to work – where opportunities abound for new and current employees.  



Our team members work in a dynamic environment where professional and personal growth is encouraged. You have the satisfaction of knowing that you are working with dedicated individuals who share a common vision of innovation and an unrelenting focus on improving people’s lives with our health care products.  



Bausch Health accomplishes its commitment to diversity and inclusiveness by simultaneously nurturing the diversity of thinking, ideas and outlook that already exists among our employees. Through consistent and aggressive efforts, we attract, develop, and retain talented people with a variety of perspectives from all backgrounds and cultures. Working in an environment that enables them to apply their collective talents to our shared principles and commitments will enable us to deliver the greatest value to our customers and the patients we serve.  



By cultivating a winning attitude, we empower employees to seize opportunities that support the company’s mission to improve people’s lives.  



What we have to offer our employees in our state\-of\-the\-art Steinbach manufacturing site:
* Annual Bonus Plan


* Insurance coverage (Medical – Dental – Vision – Short \& Long\-Term Disability – Life – AD\&D)


* Employee Assistance Program (EAP)


* Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)


* Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP)
* Gym on site


* Tuition Reimbursement


* INSPIRE: Employee Recognition Program


* Service Awards


* Employee Referral Program


* Top Up Maternity Leave

title: SR Project Manager
description:
We are looking for an experienced and results\-driven Senior Project Manager to lead and deliver complex strategic initiatives that drive enterprise transformation and achieve business objectives. As a key member within the Enterprise Portfolio Management Office (EPMO), you will oversee complex, cross\-functional, and high\-impact projects that align with organizational and strategic goals.

  

**Position Overview**



The Senior Project Manager applies their advanced knowledge and experience in managing programs, large and complex projects, internal and external partners, and cross\-functional project teams. This role is accountable for delivering projects on time, within scope, and within budget, while managing risks, resources, stakeholder expectations, and ensuring quality standards are met. The Senior Project Manager serves as a strategic partner to business leaders, providing guidance on project planning, execution, and governance.


**Key Accountabilities and Responsibilities**


* Lead the end\-to\-end planning, execution, and implementation of large, multi\-year, complex, high risk projects. Ensuring adherence to waterfall, agile, and hybrid project management standards and best practices.
* Coordinate internal resources and third parties/vendors.
* Collaborate and communicate effectively with various business units and technology leaders, cross\-functional teams, project team members, and external parties to ensure projects are well defined, planned, and delivered.
* Contribute to the evolution of internal processes and methodology, including coaching and mentorship on Project Management and project delivery.
* Work closely with a project’s sponsors and delivery leaders to develop the project charter, including defining the project scope, objectives, approach, measures of success, stakeholder engagement, and communication plan.
* Develop integrated project plans and dependency maps demonstrating strong financial and project acumen (including cost estimates, budget, and resourcing requirements), apply appropriate estimation models, and refine and manage performance against them, customizing planning methods to suit the project.
* Manage project budgets and costs as well as providing estimates and forecasts, including competent analysis of capitalization, amortization, and expense management.
* Perform risk planning and analysis (qualitative \& quantitative), including risk identification, risk response planning, and engaging with governance stakeholders to ensure compliance of products towards the corporate risk profile, standards, and organizational governance processes.
* Seek opportunities to improve personal, team, and organizational efficiency.


**Qualifications:**



To be considered for this role, we’d like you to have…


* 8\+ years of project management experience, with at least 3 years in a senior or lead role.
* Project Management Certification or equivalent is highly desirable.
* Proven ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously.
* Proficiency in project management tools and software. (JIRA, Smartsheet, etc.)


To excel as our Senior Project Manager, you’ll need…


* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
* Proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
* Ability to work independently and prioritize work for multiple projects.
* Experience facilitating meetings and committees with diverse audiences (from technical teams to VPs) and maintaining strong stakeholder relationships.
* Experience with Waterfall, Agile, Scrum and / or Product Management.

  


**Why join WCLC?**


***Flexibility \& Work\-Life Balance:*** We offer a hybrid work environment that allows you to balance your professional and personal life. Enjoy the flexibility to work from home and the office, ensuring you have the best of both worlds.


***Collaborative Culture:*** Work with a diverse and talented team committed to driving positive change.


**Who we are**



You may not be very familiar with WCLC, but you’re almost certainly familiar with the products we help bring to players throughout the Prairie Provinces and the North every day; lottery games like LOTTO 6/49, SPORT SELECT, and dozens of instant scratch tickets.


WCLC is the non\-profit organization authorized to manage, conduct, and operate lotteries – at retail and online – and other gaming\-related activities for the governments of Alberta, Saskatchewan, and Manitoba as well as the Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut. We work closely with our provincial partners to bring a first\-rate lottery experience to our players and meet their changing needs with new technology and tools.


We know it’s our people that make this possible, which is why we invest in building talented, adaptable, and accountable teams who are proud to live and work in our region. We’d love to talk to you about how you fit into that.


We offer a challenging, collaborative work environment, competitive benefits, and a good work\-life balance.

description:
**Sample Intake Technician**

**Summary** 

Duracan is seeking a Sample Intake Technician to support our Steinbach location. This role is responsible for receiving, verifying, and logging client samples, as well as providing administrative and customer service support.

The Sample Intake Technician plays a key role in maintaining accurate sample intake, documentation, and coordination with laboratory operations to ensure sample integrity and traceability.

**Position Details**

* Location: Steinbach, MB
* Hours: 25–40 hours per week
* Schedule: Monday to Friday
* Hours and schedule may vary based on operational needs
* Travel to Winnipeg is required; work at the Winnipeg laboratory location may be required as needed

**Degree of Supervision** 

Works under general supervision with the ability to perform daily tasks independently. Receives oversight and direction from the Laboratory Manager, with support from the Sample Intake Manager as required. The role requires independent work and responsibility to follow established procedures and escalate any issues or uncertainties promptly.

**Responsibilities**

**Sample Intake \& Documentation**

* Receive client samples and verify labeling, condition, and documentation
* Review submission forms for completeness and accuracy
* Log samples into the laboratory system with a high degree of accuracy
* Ensure proper identification, handling, and organization of samples
* Maintain accurate records to support sample traceability

**Client Service \& Communication**

* Provide front\-desk support for clients submitting samples
* Answer and direct phone calls and emails
* Assist clients with submission forms and general inquiries (non\-technical)
* Maintain a professional and customer\-focused approach at all times

**Logistics \& Coordination**

* Coordinate and manage the movement of samples within laboratory operations
* Prepare samples for internal transfer and ensure proper handling and tracking
* Transport samples between locations as required
* Provide operational support at the Winnipeg laboratory location as needed
* Maintain organized tracking of incoming and outgoing samples

**Administrative Support**

* Perform data entry and maintain accurate records
* Prepare and process documentation
* Maintain an organized and professional workspace
* Support general administrative and operational tasks as required

**Quality \& Compliance**

* Follow company procedures for sample handling, documentation, and intake
* Identify and report discrepancies, damaged samples, or incomplete documentation
* Maintain confidentiality of client and laboratory information
* Support quality system requirements related to sample receipt, documentation, and traceability

**Additional Responsibilities**

* Support additional administrative or operational tasks as needed
* Work collaboratively with team members to support coverage and daily operations
* Duties and responsibilities may be adjusted based on operational requirements
* Maintain timely communication with management regarding daily activities, issues, and sample intake status

**Qualifications**

* Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
* Excellent communication and customer service abilities
* Comfortable with data entry and computer systems
* Ability to work independently with minimal supervision
* Flexibility to support operations at multiple locations, including Winnipeg
* Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation
* Previous administrative or laboratory support experience is an asset

**Education \& Experience (Assets)**

* Diploma or degree in a relevant field (science, environmental, or administrative) or equivalent experience is considered an asset
* Experience in a laboratory, environmental, or sample intake setting is an asset
* Familiarity with laboratory safety protocols and handling procedures is preferred

**Additional Skills (Assets)**

* Strong data entry skills with a high level of accuracy
* Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast\-paced environment
* Proficiency with computer systems (experience with Mac\-based systems is an asset)
* Ability to handle sensitive information with professionalism and discretion

**Work Environment**

* Office\-based with handling of environmental samples
* Work may involve handling samples that could contain potentially hazardous materials
* Adherence to laboratory safety protocols and use of appropriate PPE is required at all times

**Schedule**

* Monday to Friday
* Overtime as required

**Benefits**

* Casual dress
* Company events
* On\-site parking

**Flexible Language Requirement:**

* French not required

**Supplemental Pay Types:**

* Bonus pay
* Overtime pay

title: Senior Research Assistant, Magnet Coils for Neutron EDM Experiment
description:
Senior Research Assistants are normally graduate students, or University of Winnipeg undergraduate students, employed to assist in the execution and evaluation of research projects in their area of study. The positions may oversee Research Assistants in areas such as the use of proper techniques, analysis, and documentation.



Senior Research Assistants work under the general supervision or direction of more senior researchers or faculty members, and are expected to exercise judgment in prioritizing, planning and organizing their own work within defined parameters.


**Position Overview:**



The student will assist in the construction of main magnetic field generating coil for the TRIUMF UltraCold Advanced Neutron (TUCAN) electric dipole moment (EDM) experiment. The student will also work with shim coils and spin\-flipping coils needed in the experiment. The student will be based at TRIUMF (on the UBC campus in Vancouver) for most of the summer, in order to complete the work.


**Duties:**


* Prepare equipment and materials for research
	+ Student will assist in the construction the main coil for the TUCAN EDM experiment, using precision measurement equipment for alignment.
* Assist in data collection, data entry, analysis and interpretation.
	+ Student will acquire and analyze data on physical dimension measurements of the coil, and will eventually acquire data on magnetic field generated by the coil
* Assist in preparing article, publications, reports and presentations.
	+ Student will prepare weekly progress reports and presentations, and a final report on the work at the end of the summer.

**Qualifications:**


* Academic Major: Physics or Honours Physics having completed 2nd\-year Physics courses.
* Overall GPA: minimum 3\.5
* Ability to use precision measurement tools, such as height gauges on a granite table.
* Ability to analyze data in Python and present results using PowerPoint or equivalent.
* Requires a lot of standing to perform the measurements and assembly of the coils.
* Student is expected to relocate to Vancouver, BC to work at TRIUMF, Canada's Particle Accelerator Centre.


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**
  


The period of work will be from **May 4, 2026 \- August 28, 2026** and the estimated total hours of work will be **560\-630 hours.**


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*


**Note:** The work described in this posting will be conducted in\-person.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion and recognizes that a diverse staff and faculty benefits and enriches the work, learning and research environments, and is essential to academic and institutional excellence. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and encourage women, racialized persons, Indigenous persons, persons with disabilities, and 2SLGBTQ\+ persons to confidentially self\-identify at time of application.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to ensuring employment opportunities are accessible for all applicants. If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.



The personal information of applicants is collected under the authority of the University of Winnipeg Act and 36(1\)(b) of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. All personal information collected via the recruitment process is used to assess the applicant’s suitability, eligibility, and qualifications for employment, and to otherwise support recruitment activities. This information will be provided to participating members of the recruitment process. Questions regarding the collection of your personal information may be directed to the Director, HR Services, 515 Portage Avenue, Winnipeg, MB, R3B 2E9 or human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.

description:
Clearpoint Health Network (CHN) is one of Canada’s largest trusted provider of independent surgical and multi\-disciplinary medical centres across Canada. Working in close collaboration with hospitals, insurance providers, regional health organizations and workers’ compensation boards, Clearpoint delivers high\-quality care across Canada meeting the needs of patients, while alleviating capacity pressure on public health systems.



Maples Surgical Centre a division of Clearpoint Health Network Inc. We offer various services, including surgeries and consultations, to relieve some of the pressure on the public healthcare system. Maples Surgical Centre, Specialties include Orthopaedics, Gynecology, Plastics, ENT, Urology, Spine, Cosmetics and others as required.



Maples Surgical Centre, a division of Clearpoint Health Network is now recruiting for Casual Medical Device Reprocessing Technician / Sterile Processing Tech II to Join Our Team

* Free Parking


**Permanent roles include:**


* Health \& Dental Benefits
* RRSP Match up to 3%
* Paid Vacation \& Personal Time Off


**Position Summary**



The medical device reprocessing (MDR) technician is responsible for the cleaning, disassembly, reassembly and sterilization of surgical supplies, equipment and reusable medical devices. Principles of aseptic technique shall be used, and routine infection control practices shall be followed. The MDR technician will ensure that reusable medical devices are in proper working order, perform biological indicator, chemical indicator and other quality assurance testing and report problems to the supervisor.



MDR technicians are on their feet, walking or standing for much of their shift. They may be required to bend, reach, lift and move equipment and supplies. MDR technicians are exposed to strong chemicals and bio\-hazardous materials while doing their jobs. Their work areas can be noisy and busy and, at times, can be challenging or stressful.


**Key Responsibilities**


* Collect, disassemble, clean, disinfect, assemble, function test, package, sterilize, store and distribute reusable medical devices, equipment and supplies used in the surgical suite
* Operate and maintain a variety of equipment used to reprocess reusable medical devices
* Assemble and provide reusable medical device sets and carts as ordered for a variety of surgical procedures
* Inventory, monitor, order, replenish and rotate supplies in the work area
* Cleaning and disinfecting work areas, and inspecting and maintaining supplies to ensure supplies are functioning and surgical areas are well stocked
* Follow established safety and infection control protocols
* Document and report on activities within the work area
* Provide leadership and guidance to co\-workers
* Maintain surgical inventory, and stock supplies as per protocol
* Support peri\-operative suite as requested
* Assist with OR cleaning and turnovers between cases


**Qualifications**


* Completion of an accredited Medical Device Reprocessing program
* Must be able to read, write, speak and understand English fluently
* Recent experience in an acute hospital medical device reprocessing environment as asset

* Preference given to a candidate with previous experience within the entrepreneurial ecosystem.

**Key responsibilities**
