In addition to salary, full\-time and part\-time permanent employees are eligible for an annual bonus plan, leave of absence top\-up programs and provided with generous vacation time, personal days, premium free benefits and pension plan.  


The salary offered for this role is determined with consideration to various factors, including but not limited to: your work location, local labour market conditions, external market salary data, internal pay equity and the knowledge, skills, experience and anticipated proficiency in the role. The salary offered is estimated to be within the following range: $90,000 \- $140,000\. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are still encouraged to apply.  


**About Us**  

At Wawanesa, we offer a hybrid work environment that offers flexibility to our employees in balancing in\-office (2 days per week OR 15 hours per week in a Wawanesa office) and remote work. You may work from any of the following locations: Winnipeg, MB; Wawanesa, MB; Vancouver, BC; Calgary, AB; Edmonton, AB; Lethbridge, AB; Toronto (North York), ON; Kitchener, ON; Ottawa, ON; Montreal, QC; Quebec City, QC, Moncton, NB; Dartmouth; NS.  


The Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company (“Wawanesa Mutual”), founded in 1896, is one of Canada’s largest mutual insurers, with over $3\.5 billion in annual revenue and assets of $10 billion (CAD). Wawanesa Mutual, with its National Headquarters in Winnipeg, is the parent company of Wawanesa Life, which provides life insurance products and services throughout Canada, and Western Financial Group, which distributes personal and business insurance across Canada. Wawanesa proudly serves more than 1\.7 million members in Canada, and we are home to more than 3,300 employees distributed across the Canadian regions and communities where we operate. We give back to organizations that strengthen communities, donating more than $3\.5 million annually to charitable organizations, including over $2 million annually in support of people on the front lines of climate change. We are also proud to be recognized as one of Manitoba’s Top Employers. To learn more visit wawanesa.com.  

We are currently looking for dedicated, driven, and enthusiastic individuals who thrive in an environment that welcomes change and are looking for an opportunity for diverse experience and advancement on a growing team. **Job Overview**

As an Actuarial Consultant/Sr Consultant (Personal Lines Pricing – Regulated Automobile, Maritimes), you’ll take a lead role in the auto pricing analysis for the Maritime region, working with a high degree of autonomy. This position involves mentoring actuarial analysts and applying actuarial expertise to address complex business problems and provide strategic insights.  

  

Note: we are open to hiring at either the Senior Actuarial Analyst, Actuarial Consultant or Senior Actuarial Consultant level.**Job Responsibilities**
* Researches, extracts, and manipulates complex data from relevant sources, and assesses data quality.
* Supports the business and overall team by performing analyses/reviews and completing ad\-hoc requests/special projects in a timely manner with minimal support from peers and leaders.
* Identifies emerging industry trends and analyzes the impact they will have on Wawanesa.
* Provides regular project updates to stakeholders. Anticipates obstacles and raises them in advance.
* Leads projects from beginning to end. Includes implementation of final product. May include training and documentation of the project.
* Provides feedback, instruction, and development guidance to help others excel in their current or future job responsibilities.
* Builds trust and credibility with peers. Develops strong working relationships across teams.
**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or other related discipline.
* More than 3 years of actuarial experience an asset.
* 4 CAS exams are an asset.
* Communication/Building Relationships
	+ Advanced oral and written communication skills, demonstrating the ability to convey business terminology and insights that are meaningful and well received
* Computer/Programming Skills
	+ Proficient with Excel, SQL, R, Python, Radar and/or other related software/programming languages/actuarial software.
* Problem solving
	+ Advanced knowledge of actuarial science, mathematics, probability, statistics, principles of finance and business, and strong ability to apply this knowledge to problems in insurance.
* Detail oriented
	+ Strong attention to detail when performing analysis with minimal technical errors.
* Conflict Management/Teamwork
	+ Ability to work efficiently and co\-operatively in a team environment.
 **Diversity Equity, Inclusion \& Belonging**  

  

At Wawanesa, we are committed to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) and believe that our strength lies in the diversity of our people – this is supported by having a representative workforce.  


We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, including racialized persons, women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, members of the 2SLGBTQIA\+ community, gender\-diverse and neurodiverse individuals, and anyone who can contribute to the further diversification of thought and ideas.  


We aim to ensure our recruitment process is accessible to all candidates. If you require accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please reach out in confidence to jobs@wawanesa.com.  


All Wawanesa job applicants are subject to Wawanesa's Privacy Policy.  


Please note that the recruitment process for this position may involve the use of AI tools to screen, assess, or select applicants. All final decisions are taken or reviewed by human recruiters and human hiring leaders in compliance with all applicable legislation.

title: Lab Coordinator - Technician 4 (AESES)
description:
Faculty of Kinesiology \& Rec. Management  

Position number: 20159
Date posted: April 29, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Lab Coordinator \- Technician 4 (AESES)

Existing Regular Continuing Full\-Time (Budget Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: Yes
  

Work schedule: 35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:30 am to 4:30 pm)
  

Salary: $26\.20 to $37\.62 per hour ($47,684\.00 to $68,468\.40 per annum)
  

Expected Start Date June 22, 2026
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Dr. Stephen Cornish \- stephen.cornish@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------



**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING:**
* Masters degree in a related study area.
* *\-**or**\-*
* Bachelors degree in a related discipline and a minimum of three years directly\-related experience.
* Certified Exercise Professional preferred .
* First Aid/CPR certification.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* Knowledge of both the theoretical basis and practical application of exercise and fitness testing procedures is required.
* Technical knowledge of equipment used in labs (e.g.; treadmills, spirometers, ECG monitoring, oxygen uptake testing, gas analyzers, BioAmps, Biodex isokinetic dynamometer, cycle ergometers, and grip force transducers) are required.
* Knowledge of methods for biological signal acquisition (e.g. motion analysis systems, force platforms, EMG, eye tracking, force transducers, Biodex, metabolic carts) and methods for digital signal processing (using programs such as LabVIEW, MATLAB, etc.).
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* Effective communications skills, both written and oral, are required Computer proficiency and troubleshooting skills are required.
* Ability to safely administer exercise testing procedures and correctly interpret the data acquired is required Ability to plan, organize, and administer projects is required.
* Ability to work within time and budget constraints is required.
* Ability and willingness to learn new techniques is required.
* Ability to keep abreast of developments in the areas of expertise is required Ability to work both independently and as a member of a team is required.
* Ability to physically perform duties as assigned, which may include mobility and/or moderate to heavy lifting.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.


**OTHER JOB RELATED QUALIFICATIONS:**


* Experience assisting with undergraduate labs is preferred Instructional experience in formal settings is preferred.
* Proficiency in the use of research based computer programs is preferred Demonstrated research experience preferred.
**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


* After conferring with the faculty member(s) responsible for the course, train the lab demos in the procedures to be employed and the proper use of equipment to be used in the lab.
* Prepare lab demos for the independent delivery of lab content to ensure that all labs in a course are consistent.
* Support lab demos in acquiring knowledge of the subject area and equipment usage.
* Assist in the development of lab demos’ teaching methods, delivery of content, and communication, skills.
* Oversee proper use and readiness of all laboratory facilities operated by the Faculty of Kinesiology and Recreation Management.
* Organize the lab equipment and materials to better facilitate instructor and student usage.
* Plan, maintain, and prepare equipment for use in research projects and in undergraduate course labs.
* Identify repairs needed to equipment and communicate needs to supervisors.
* Ensure that all equipment is catalogued and stored properly.
* Acquire training on how to use all equipment.
* Assist in the training of lab demos, research assistants, researchers, and students on how to use equipment safely.
* Cooperate with the Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System (WHMIS) Coordinators of the Active Living Centre, Frank Kennedy Centre, Extended Education Complex, Max Bell Centre, and Investors Group Athletic Centre to ensure safety standards are attained and maintained in all Faculty of Kinesiology and Recreation Management labs.
* Investigate and recommend lab equipment and supplies for the daily operation of the Faculty of Kinesiology and Recreation Management’s research and undergraduate teaching laboratories.
* Document and maintain a detailed up to date inventory and maintenance schedule of all research and teaching equipment in the Faculty of Kinesiology and Recreation Management, including the condition of each item.
* Develop and oversee standard procedures for issue and use of laboratory equipment and shared research space, including the booking of research laboratory space for stakeholders external to the FKRM following the approval of the Associate Dean (Research and Graduate Studies).
* Facilitate the development and implementation of SAFE Work procedures for FKRM teaching and research laboratories.
* Keep a log of parking passes used for research participants and manage the inventory.
* Keep a log of equipment being signed out and manage the inventory.
* Schedule the use of labs independently or in conjunction with other booking services that exist in Faculty (i.e., Facilities) for users, including Researchers, Recreation Services, Bison Sports, and potentially outside users.
* Assist with the development of technical service agreements for outside users of space or when researchers are using the space on contracts on behalf of outside entities.
* Provide lab tours upon request from the Dean or Associate Deans of the Faculty of Kinesiology and Recreation Management.
* Facilitate the repair of equipment by outside companies, when necessary.
* Provide laboratory instruction when requested by supervisors.
* Assist in developing, updating, and maintaining lab manuals and other lab materials when requested by course instructors.
* Assist in setting expectations for the objectives of each lab to be carried out when requested by course instructors.
* Assist in creating marking keys for questions, assignments, and quizzes when requested by course instructors.
* Other duties as assigned.
**Additional information**
--------------------------

  

  

The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).



If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact UM.Accommodation@umanitoba.ca or 204\-474\-7195\. Please note this contact information is for accommodation reasons only.



Application materials, including letters of reference, will be handled in accordance with the protection of privacy provisions of "The Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act" (Manitoba). Please note that curriculum vitae will be provided to participating members of the search process.

title: Production Research Assistant
description:
**At Bayer we’re visionaries, driven to solve the world’s toughest challenges and striving for a world where ,Health for all, Hunger for none’ is no longer a dream, but a real possibility. We’re doing it with energy, curiosity and sheer dedication, always learning from unique perspectives of those around us, expanding our thinking, growing our capabilities and redefining ‘impossible’. There are so many reasons to join us. If you’re hungry to build a varied and meaningful career in a community of brilliant and diverse minds to make a real difference, there’s only one choice.**  


**Production Research Assistant**  

Bayer CropScience is seeking a highly motivated Seed Production Research Assistant to deliver operational aspects of indoor protected culture plant breeding operations for its Canola R\&D program. This is a unique opportunity to work with leading edge technology and talented R\&D professionals that deliver the best Canola products.  



This is a permanent, full\-time position, based in Winnipeg, Manitoba at the One Research Road (Smartpark) facility.  


**What you will do:**
* Manage the day\-to\-day organization and maintenance of growth room projects including, but not limited to seeding, tissue sampling, transplanting, self \& cross pollinating, harvesting \& threshing
* Manage general plant care and maintenance of growth rooms
* Identify and report issues that may arise with facility, equipment or with plant health including need to control pests or diseases in growth rooms
* Use of herbicides or other chemical applications under supervision and following the company’s policies and procedures
* Efficiently \& effectively carryout all detailed record keeping, chemical spraying and other technical objectives
* Maintain tidiness \& orderliness within the workplace
* Supervise and train temporary employees as required
* Work closely with other research staff to coordinate and implement process improvements
* Participate in task\-related data collection, document creation and process review for job\-related activities
* Comply and maintain Health Safety and Environment (HSE), Quality Management System (QMS) and Compliance programs at Bayer.
* Assist in other projects \& performs other duties as required
* Foster an inclusive environment of professionalism and safe working practices ensuring Safety and Compliance requirements are met or exceeded within Squad.

  



**Who you are:**
* Degree or diploma in agricultural/biological science or related field or equivalent
* Ability to manage physical nature of watering, plant care, growth room maintenance
* Willingness to work in varied weather conditions (cool or warm)
* Ability to lift up to 30 lbs
* Able to work overtime during week and weekends when necessary
* Possess strong ability to work accurately and pay attention to detail
* Outstanding organizational skills and ability to prioritize daily tasks
* Highly motivated, results oriented, with the ability to work with people individually or in a team to gain commitment to achieve results
* Comfortable with change and ability to learn new procedures
* Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal
* Ability to communicate complex ideas in a clear, concise and actionable manner
* Strong computer literacy, with experience using Excel, Word, PowerPoint


***DESIRED SKILLS/EXPERIENCE:***
* Previous experience in Canola and/or plant breeding
* Knowledge of crop production or growth room production methodologies
* Experience in Hybrid crop research \& development.
* Ability and willingness to obtain pesticide applicator license

  


**What we offer:**
* Competitive compensation and rewards package
* Collaborative, diverse and inclusive culture
* Career development and global opportunities
* Work\-life flexibility programs such as flex hours, employee discounts, volunteer days, wellness, and other employee offerings

  



Bayer is committed to fostering, cultivating and preserving a culture of Inclusion and Diversity. We embrace and encourage our employees’ differences and believe that our people are our most valuable asset. The collective sum of individual life experiences, knowledge, innovation, self\-expression, unique capabilities that our employees invest in their work represents our culture, reputation, and Bayer’s values and purpose.  



The base salary range for this role is between $47,200 and $70,800\. Additional compensation may include a bonus or incentive compensation. Additional benefits may include insured benefits such as health care, vision, dental, disability, and contributory retirement savings plans, etc. This salary range is an estimate and may vary based on factors including, but not limited to, market data and the applicant’s skills, prior relevant experience, job\-related knowledge, specific degrees and certifications, and other relevant factors. This role is posted for a vacant position.  


***This posting will be available for application until May 13, 2026\.***  



All referral candidates must first be submitted via our internal referral system by a current Bayer employee in order to participate in the referral program. Once a Bayer employee has successfully referred you in our system, you will receive an automated email with instructions on how to complete your application.  


**YOUR APPLICATION**

If your background and personal experience fit this profile, please send us your complete application at www.career.bayer.cn If you have any recommendations, please kindly send mail to cnreferral@bayer.com  


**L****ocation:**

Canada : Manitoba : Winnipeg  


**Division:**

Crop Science  


**Reference Code:**
868517

BELFOR is a global leader in property restoration, helping families and businesses recover from fire, water, storm, and other disaster\-related damage. Reporting to the General Manager, the Project Manager is the main generator of revenue for the branch. You’ll assess job sites, prepare estimates, and keep clients, adjusters, and consultants informed. * Strong customer service, communication, and organizational skills.

 **Nice to Have*** Post\-secondary education in Construction, Business, or Project Management.

. **Why Join Us:*** Competitive compensation package;

To begin a career with Defence Construction Canada (DCC), you must be a Canadian Citizen or a permanent resident of Canada. As a condition of employment with DCC, a minimum reliability security status clearance is required and some roles may require secret security status clearance.

*DCC is responsible for the Personnel Security Clearance process*. Typically to be eligible for a "Reliability" clearance, you must have five years of verifiable background information and to be eligible for a "Secret" clearance, you must have 10 years of verifiable background information. The process usually involves reference inquiries, verification of qualifications, criminal records checks, and credit checks (as required) and may require fingerprints. For more information about obtaining a security clearance, please review the Standard on Security Screening.


The appropriate security clearance is a condition of employment and it is the candidate’s or employee’s responsibility to ensure, they are able to obtain and maintain their required security level clearance to begin or continue their employment with DCC. Failure to obtain or maintain, the appropriate level of security clearance means either the candidate or employee will not receive a job offer, the conditional offer will be rendered null and void or their employment will be terminated with cause. A thorough security clearance process will be applied to all candidates or employees considered for an appointment; this process includes inquiries, verifications and assessments regarding matters such as, but not limited to:

* criminal record checks

  

* reference checks

  

* previous employment

  

* open source checks

  

* credit/financial status
  

DCC's Code of Business Conduct (Code) is a condition of employment. It serves to enhance public confidence in the integrity of DCC Employees and provides clarity to Employees on a number of issues, including real or perceived conflicts of interest. Before applying to this (or any) position at DCC, candidates shall familiarize themselves with the Code, because new employees are required to certify that they have read, understood, and agreed to observe the Code upon commencing work at DCC. Employees are further required to review their obligations under the Code and notify the President's Office of any changes in their circumstances at the earliest possible opportunity, and to complete the Annual Update once a year.

  

Be part of a diverse and inclusive workplace!

title: Project Manager – Bobsled Construction (Design-Build)
description:
We don't want paper pushers. We want builders.

At Bobsled Construction—working in seamless integration with Monteyne Architecture—we are pioneering a return to the "Master\-Builder" ethos. We tackle complex urban infill, adaptive reuse, and heritage sites where others see only constraints. We unite the expertise of the architect with the discipline of the builder to create architecture of clarity, quality, and craft.

We are looking for an experienced Project Manager who is as comfortable in the mud evaluating site logistics as they are running a cost\-variance report. If you prefer to manage a job entirely from behind a desk, this is not the role for you.

The Reality of the Role

As a Project Manager at Bobsled, you are the operational lynchpin. You hold ultimate accountability for the Big Four: Safety, Schedule, Cost, and Quality. You will be intimate with field operations, actively supporting your Site Superintendents and removing obstacles before they derail the master schedule.

What You Will Actually Do:

* Command the Schedule: Build, maintain, and enforce the Master Project Schedule using MS Project. You will track critical path milestones and hold subcontractors accountable to their commitments.
* Guard the Budget: Establish initial cost code allocations, verify progress payments, and provide accurate financial forecasting. You treat the project's budget with the same respect as the physical materials.
* Drive Pre\-Construction: Lead financial start\-up meetings, conduct constructability reviews, and execute subcontractor buyout to ensure zero scope\-gaps.
* Enforce Safety \& Quality: Ensure SiteDocs is heavily utilized and that our safety culture is proactive, not reactive. You will enforce our standards of craftsmanship across all self\-performed and subcontracted work.
* Lead the Communication: Prepare internal Project Risk Reports and client\-facing Monthly Status Reports. You will act as the primary bridge between the design studio, the client, and the boots on the ground.

The Evidence We Need (Requirements):

* Minimum 5–7 years of direct project management experience overseeing commercial builds, urban infill, or complex renovation projects from pre\-construction to closeout.
* A Degree or Diploma in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, Architecture, or an equivalent combination of technical training and extensive field leadership experience. (Gold Seal Certification or PMP is a strong asset).
* Fluency in our core tech stack: Procore, Bluebeam, MS Project, and SiteDocs.
* A solutions\-focused mindset. When a structural anomaly appears on a heritage site, you don't panic; you collaborate with the design team to engineer a fix.
* Demonstrable courage to have difficult conversations with trades, clients, and internal stakeholders when a project's integrity is on the line.

Compensation \& Benefits:

We are looking for people who want to build a lasting legacy, and we invest in our team accordingly.

* Competitive base salary aligned with your operational experience.
* Comprehensive Health and Dental benefits (active after 90 days).
* Group RRSP with employer matching (3% to 5%) through a Deferred Profit Sharing Plan (DPSP) after 12 months.
* A truly cross\-functional environment where you sit at the same table as the architects designing the buildings you construct.

How to Apply:

Don't send us a generic cover letter. Send your resume along with a brief outline (max 500 words) of the most complex construction problem you've solved in the last two years and how you kept the project on track.

Pay: From $85,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental care
* Extended health care
* RRSP match
* Vision care

Work Location: In person

* Participate in and provide support for testing to ensure the integrity of data, satisfaction of customers and defined benefits are realized for any new/changed reports, dashboards or analysis.
* Participate in and provide support for testing, as required.
* Other related duties as assigned.

**Primary Qualifications**
--------------------------


* Completion of a recognized degree or diploma with a focus in Computer Science, Business, HR Analytics, or related discipline, or an equivalent combination of education, certification, and experience.
* Minimum 5 years of technical experience in IBM Cognos Analytics, including administration support.
* Experience includes developing Cognos 11 or 12 reports using Framework Manager, Report Studio, Event Studio, and Data Modules.
* Demonstrated experience in SQL is required.
* Minimum 2 years of demonstrated proficiency in business analysis.
* Understanding of data warehousing concepts.
* Experience with other reporting tools such as PowerBI or Tableau would be an asset.
* Experience with HTML, JAVA, etc. would be an asset.
* Strong analytical skills and exceptional skills in creative problem solving.
* Strong organizational and time management skills, coupled with the ability to execute tasks in a fast\-paced environment.
* Detail orientated along with the ability to grasp overall corporate strategic objectives with an emphasis on divisional and departmental strategic objectives.
* Strong communication skills, both verbal and written.
* Track record of strong business partnership and cross functional collaboration within a dynamic, fast paced environment.
* Upgrading and professional development is required to keep up to date on changes in the profession.
* Highly customer service oriented, with the ability to create a positive and meaningful customer experience.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Power Point and Outlook).
* The core competencies for this position include achieving quality results, adaptability/managing change, communication, customer service, decision making and problem solving, integrity and building trust, teamwork and cooperation and valuing diversity. These competencies are deemed important for the success of the position and organization.
* Satisfactory work performance in the preceding twelve (12\) months is a requirement. Attendance rate will be a factor in the pre\-screening process.

**Secondary Qualifications**
----------------------------


* Experience with project management methodology specifically agile would be an asset.
* Working knowledge of HR business processes, functions and supporting systems is an asset.

Iris Group of Companies is a growing design\-build construction team based in Winnipeg, delivering commercial, tenant improvement, and institutional projects. **Position Overview**

We are looking for a highly organized and proactive **Project Coordinator** to support our project managers, site teams, and electrical division. This role is critical to keeping projects on track, ensuring communication between trades, suppliers, consultants, and our in\-house electrical team, while maintaining accurate project documentation.

This is a fast\-paced role suited for someone who can take ownership, communicate clearly, and push projects forward.

* Assist Project Managers with day\-to\-day project coordination

title: Administrative Assistant/Project Coordinator
description:
**Administrative Assistant/Project Coordinator**

  



Nor\-Tec Group is a full service electrical contractor. We have been servicing Winnipeg and the far reaches of most of Manitoba for 40 years. Our experience has been from Institutional projects to Industrial projects to Municipal projects and a variety of Manufacturing Plant projects. We are well versed in servicing all of the electrical needs for a wide variety of clients.

  

We are looking for an Administration Assistant/Project Coordinator to assist our Project Managers and other support staff at various sites. We provide a unique experience to those looking to gain exposure in a dynamic organization. We are a leader in the construction industry, equipping our team of skilled professionals with vision, guidance and tools, required to bring construction projects to life.

  



Working side by side with our team of Project Managers and Superintendents, you will provide project coordination support to the project team.

  



**Responsibilities of this position would include:**


* Monitor, track and log, PCN’s, shop drawings, site instructions, RFI’s, and deliveries. Request for status updates on these items as required.
* Prepare shop drawings for submittal and release. Monitor, File and request for updates in status of shop drawings during the submittal process.
* Assist in the preparation of regularly scheduled reports, time sheets and safety.
* Create, set up and collate all O\&M information/manuals.
* Act as the point of contact for internal and external clients, suppliers, field staff, management, other trades, etc. Provides status updates from all parties and provides them to Project Managers, support staff, etc.
* Assist, create, and update project schedule. Plan/Schedule meetings and take meeting minutes.
* Other job duties as assigned.

  


**Currently looking for an individual that possess the following skills and education:**


* Graduated with a Highschool Diploma, GED or equivalent standards.
* 1\-2 years of experience in Project Coordinator and Administration.
* Must be able to communicate effectively in English orally and written.
* Proficiency in MS Office (MS Excel, MS Word, MS Outlook, MS Projects in particular)
* Proven ability to work within a team and independently with minimal supervision. This includes the ability to think critically and resolve conflicts that may arise between sub trades and other applicable third parties.
* Excellent time management skills. Must be able to manage and prioritize multiple tasks meeting tight deadlines.
* Must have attention to detail.

  


**Assets for this position would be:**

  

* Experience and knowledge of Electrical Industry practices and standards.
* Currently enrolled, or graduated from a Business Administration program and/or Construction Project Management program from a recognized post\-secondary intuition.

  


**Conditions of Employment:**

  

* Candidate has Canadian citizenship or has a valid work permit.
* Must have current and valid Class 5 driver’s license and use of a personal motor vehicle for travel to jobsites.

  



A combination of skills, experience and education are considered. Nor\-Tec Group provides an environment that encourages forward thinking and provides the opportunity to advance. We want our employees to challenge themselves and be able to take on additional responsibilities as required. We offer a compensation package that includes a competitive salary, and benefits. Compensation will be based off of the candidates, skills, experience and education.

  



We encourage interested applicants to apply and submit a cover letter and resume. Nor\-Tec Group will only contact those to be interviewed and thanks all other applicants for their interest.

title: Construction Project Manager
description:

Superior Projects is a growing general contracting company with lots of energy and passion for the next challenge. We love what we do and are proud to deliver on all our construction projects. Our genuine care and construction made simple mantra is the foundation for how we operate.

We are currently seeking a reliable and hardworking Project Manager to join our team. Project locations range from Southeastern Manitoba to Winnipeg and the surrounding area. If you are motivated, have a strong work ethic, and are looking to gain experience in the construction industry, we want to hear from you!

**About the Opportunity:**

The Project Manager is responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of construction projects, from planning and budgeting to execution and completion. They coordinate with stakeholders, ensure projects adhere to safety regulations and timelines, and maintain quality standards. This role requires strong leadership, problem\-solving skills, and the ability to manage multiple tasks efficiently.

**Job Summary \& Key Responsibilities:**

* Oversee all stages of construction projects, from planning and permitting through to execution and final inspection, ensuring timely completion within budget constraints
* Collaborate with stakeholders, clients, subcontractors, and internal teams to maintain clear communication, set realistic expectations, and ensure seamless project delivery
* Manage scheduling, documentation, budgeting, and on\-site supervision, optimizing resource allocation and ensuring strict adherence to project timelines
* Maintain high standards of safety, quality, and craftsmanship by conducting regular site inspections and enforcing best practices

**Qualifications \& Requirements:**

* Excellent verbal and written communication
* 5\+ years experience in construction project management with comprehensive knowledge of building science, general contracting, codes, and construction best practices

* Highly organized, detail\-oriented, and confident
* Demonstrates critical thinking and problem\-solving
* Team\-oriented mindset
* Calm, composed and works well under pressure
* Looking for a long\-term career opportunity

* Proficient in Microsoft Office software’s, and MS Project or equivalent

title: Project Coordinator
description:
Under the direction of the Project Director – Winnipeg Sewage Treatment Program, the Project Coordinator provides leadership and guidance in the execution of projects and initiatives through an integrated multi\-disciplinary team approach that strives for innovation, excellence and efficiency. Projects may include, but are not limited to capital projects, including planning, design, consultant selection, contract administration, start\-up and turnover. This position will manage projects throughout the project’s life\-cycle and ensure effective teamwork and communication, high standards of work quality and organizational performance, and continuous learning.

**As the***Project Coordinator,* you will:

* Provide project management expertise and guidance to the Department to successfully complete and/or implement projects and departmental initiatives, while achieving the benefits of the investment.
* Develop and manage the procurement process which includes solicitation and contract administration tasks.
* Develop Business Cases per the investment planning manual.
* Coordinate the Design Management process.
* Coordinate the Project Turnover process.

**Your education and qualifications include:**


* Bachelor Degree in a related field such as Commerce, Engineering, Architect, or Finance or an acceptable equivalent combination of education, training, and experience.
* Post\-secondary education in Project Management would be preferred.


* Attainment of a Project Management Professional (PMP) designation/Prince II certification and certification in Asset Management, and ADKAR at the time of hire is preferred. Applicants who have not yet attained these certifications are required to obtain within a six (6\) year timeframe.


* Successful completion of the associated professional designations in addition to the education listed above is preferred including obtaining a P. Eng., C.E.T./A.Sc.T., MRAIC, CMA, CGA, CA, or CPA.
* Certificate in Quality Management is preferred.


* 3\-5 years of experience related to the duties listed above in Project Delivery, preferably in a Public Sector environment.


* Knowledge in the industry sector that is applicable to the department that the position will reside in.
* Previous success in leading projects including change initiatives, navigating complex environments and influencing desirable outcomes through positive and collaborative relationship management.


* Experience with supervision and management of design consultants and contractors.


* Excellent organizational and time management skills in order to be flexible and responsive to changing priorities and circumstances as well as ability to work on diverse projects simultaneously in stressful and fast paced environment.
* Previous experience managing and leading projects including organizing, coordinating, planning, and maintaining schedules and controlling a large number of projects.
* Excellent analytical, problem\-solving and decision\-making skills.


* Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to communicate with all levels of staff, stakeholders and the general public.


* Demonstrated ability and experience with Microsoft Office Suite including experience with Project Management Systems such as Microsoft Project or similar software.


* Demonstrated commitment towards continuous learning, growth and the achievement of high performance.


* Excellent interpersonal skills including the ability to maintain relations with consultants, government agencies, developers, suppliers and contractors relative to facility and equipment design and improvements, and formulates sound recommendations.


* Proven ability to set priorities, solve problems and meet deadlines under pressure.


* Experience directly related to project management of large scale municipal and/or facility, or infrastructure projects.


* Knowledge of standards, codes, legislation, and environmental requirements applicable to municipal and public facilities.
* **IMPORTANT**: Applicants who have been educated outside of Canada must have education which is comparable to the minimum qualification in Canada. Applicants submitting foreign credentials require an official academic assessment report issued by a recognized Canadian assessment service https://canalliance.org/en/ at application.

**Conditions of employment:**

* The successful candidate must maintain legal eligibility to work in Canada. If the successful candidate possesses a work permit, it is their responsibility to ensure the permit remains valid.
* A Police Information Check satisfactory to the employer will be required from the successful candidate, at their expense.
* Must possess and maintain a valid Class 5 Manitoba Driver’s License. The successful applicant will be required to provide a driver’s notice in force document upon request.

title: Research Assistant, On Nonsmooth and Nonconvex Optimization Problems
description:
Research Assistants are normally students, in a University of Winnipeg undergraduate program, employed to assist with tasks related to research. Work is performed within defined guidelines and under supervision of a more senior researcher or faculty member.


**Position Overview:**



We are seeking a motivated research assistant to work on a project focused on nonsmooth and nonconvex optimization problems with applications in statistics. The successful applicant will contribute to developing and evaluating solution methods for a certain class of nonsmooth and nonconvex optimization problems. The work involves mathematical developments, simulation studies, data analysis, and literature review. While research assistants are typically from the University of Winnipeg, in this case, applicants from other Canadian universities are welcome to apply.


**Duties:**


* Conduct a thorough literature review on relevant methods.
* Contribute to the development of theoretical aspects of the methods.
* Implement methods using R and perform simulation studies to evaluate their performance.
* Analyze real\-world datasets using the developed methods.
* Assist in preparing research findings for submission to peer\-reviewed journals.
* Write and format reports and manuscripts using LaTeX.
* Participate in regular meetings to discuss progress and collaborate with the research team


**Qualifications:**


* Strong background in mathematics and in statistical theory and methods.
* Experience with R programming, data analysis, and simulation.
* Interest in optimization methods and high\-dimensional data analysis.
* Ability to work independently, meet deadlines, and attend regular in\-person meetings at the University of Winnipeg.
* Strong communication skills and attention to detail.
* Familiarity with LaTeX or experience contributing to academic publications is an asset.


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**


The period of work will be from **June 22, 2026 \- December 8, 2026** and the estimated total hours of work will be **0\-400 hours**.


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*



The University of Winnipeg is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion and recognizes that a diverse staff and faculty benefits and enriches the work, learning and research environments, and is essential to academic and institutional excellence. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and encourage women, racialized persons, Indigenous persons, persons with disabilities, and 2SLGBTQ\+ persons to confidentially self\-identify at time of application.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to ensuring employment opportunities are accessible for all applicants. If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.



The personal information of applicants is collected under the authority of the University of Winnipeg Act and 36(1\)(b) of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. All personal information collected via the recruitment process is used to assess the applicant’s suitability, eligibility, and qualifications for employment, and to otherwise support recruitment activities. This information will be provided to participating members of the recruitment process. Questions regarding the collection of your personal information may be directed to the Director, HR Services, 515 Portage Avenue, Winnipeg, MB, R3B 2E9 or human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.

description:

Job Description:


The Vendor Master Data Analyst is responsible for maintaining the accuracy, integrity, and compliance of supplier information within the company’s financial systems. This role ensures that suppliers are properly onboarded, verified, and maintained according to company and regulatory requirements. The Analyst also supports process improvements, participates in 1099 issuance, and negotiates payment terms and methods with suppliers to optimize efficiency and cash flow

**Key Responsibilities**

* Review and process supplier onboarding and maintenance requests, ensuring all required documentation (e.g., W\-9s, banking information) is complete, accurate, and compliant with internal controls.
* Maintain supplier master data across multiple systems (e.g., Workday, Corpay, OnPay, CCC1, ASE), ensuring data integrity, accuracy, and consistency.
* Negotiate and establish charge accounts, appropriate payment terms and payment methods with suppliers to support company objectives and improve cash flow.
* Communicate and coordinate with internal stakeholders and suppliers to resolve issues related to supplier setup, payment terms, tax information, or other data discrepancies
* Support the preparation and issuance of annual 1099 tax forms, ensuring accuracy and compliance with IRS regulations.
* Identify and analyze data issues, trends, or process gaps and recommend improvements to enhance data quality and operational efficiency.

**Qualifications**

* Post\-secondary education in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
* 1–3 years of experience in supplier maintenance, accounts payable, or vendor master data management.

description:

**Company:** Wawanesa Insurance  



**Job ID:** 10011  

**Employment Type:** Existing Role  

  

**Working Business Language**: English. This role is considered a head\-office role and will be required to communicate with internal stakeholders across Canada where the primary business language utilized is English.  

**Salary:** At Wawanesa, salary is only one component of a holistic, comprehensive and competitive offering that we provide to our employees. In addition to salary, full\-time and part\-time permanent employees are eligible for an annual bonus plan, leave of absence top\-up programs and provided with generous vacation time, personal days, premium free benefits and pension plan.  


The salary offered for this role is determined with consideration to various factors, including but not limited to: your work location, local labour market conditions, external market salary data, internal pay equity and the knowledge, skills, experience and anticipated proficiency in the role. The salary offered is estimated to be within the following range: $90,000 \- $140,000\. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are still encouraged to apply.  


**About Us**  

At Wawanesa, we offer a hybrid work environment that offers flexibility to our employees in balancing in\-office (2 days per week OR 15 hours per week in a Wawanesa office) and remote work. You may work from any of the following locations: Winnipeg, MB; Wawanesa, MB; Vancouver, BC; Calgary, AB; Edmonton, AB; Lethbridge, AB; Toronto (North York), ON; Kitchener, ON; Ottawa, ON; Montreal, QC; Quebec City, QC, Moncton, NB; Dartmouth; NS.  


The Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company (“Wawanesa Mutual”), founded in 1896, is one of Canada’s largest mutual insurers, with over $3\.5 billion in annual revenue and assets of $10 billion (CAD). Wawanesa Mutual, with its National Headquarters in Winnipeg, is the parent company of Wawanesa Life, which provides life insurance products and services throughout Canada, and Western Financial Group, which distributes personal and business insurance across Canada. Wawanesa proudly serves more than 1\.7 million members in Canada, and we are home to more than 3,300 employees distributed across the Canadian regions and communities where we operate. We give back to organizations that strengthen communities, donating more than $3\.5 million annually to charitable organizations, including over $2 million annually in support of people on the front lines of climate change. We are also proud to be recognized as one of Manitoba’s Top Employers. To learn more visit wawanesa.com.  

We are currently looking for dedicated, driven, and enthusiastic individuals who thrive in an environment that welcomes change and are looking for an opportunity for diverse experience and advancement on a growing team. **Job Overview**

Working with a high degree of autonomy, the Actuarial Consultant is responsible for leading development of reporting that leads to actionable business insights used by the Personal Pricing department. The successful candidate will collaborate with Data Analysts and apply their knowledge to complex actuarial analysis in the pursuit of solving business issues and answering business questions.  


Note: we are open to hiring at the Senior Actuarial Analyst, Actuarial Consultant or Senior Actuarial Consultant level**Job Responsibilities**
* Researches, extracts, and manipulates complex data from relevant sources, and assesses data quality.
* Supports the business and overall team by performing analyses/reviews and completing ad\-hoc requests/special projects in a timely manner with minimal support from peers and leaders.
* Evaluates procedures and processes, and recommend alternatives or improvements.
* Evaluates existing processes to find areas for increased efficiency Identifies opportunities for automation of routine tasks and implementation of advanced techniques while ensuring ethical and responsible application of solutions.
* Develops new approaches to problem solving and assess impacts of solution options with minimal support from leaders Identifies emerging industry trends and analyzes the impact they will have on Wawanesa.
* Encouraged to represent Wawanesa at external events that may include universities, actuarial clubs, industry organizations, and charitable causes
* Commits to professional development and continuous experiential learning to build cross\-functional knowledge, technical skills, and mastery in competencies.
* Focuses learning activities on bringing new knowledge and skills to team in order to address any existing gaps.
* Provides regular project updates to stakeholders. Anticipates obstacles and raises them in advance. Leads projects from beginning to end. Includes implementation of final product. May include training and documentation of the project.
* Actively contributes to the mentorship, training and development of actuarial analysts.
* Provides feedback, instruction, and development guidance to help others excel in their current or future job responsibilities Builds trust and credibility with peers.
* Develops strong working relationships across teams.
**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or other related discipline.
* More than four years of actuarial experience an asset 7 CAS exams or ACAS designation an asset.
* Advanced oral and written communication skills, demonstrating the ability to convey business terminology and insights that are meaningful and well received.
* Ability to clearly document major projects and key processes .
* Effective at comfortably communicating analysis and results to senior leaders in written and/or oral form, and influencing direct leader to accept recommendations.
* Discusses ideas in terms familiar to colleagues in different functional areas and teaches the necessary actuarial concepts when needed.
* Proficient with Excel, SQL, R, Python and/or other related software/programming languages/actuarial software AWS (S3 and Sagemaker) or Power BI experience would be an asset.
* Experience with Databricks or similar platform would be an asset.
* Ability to easily adapt to new enterprise system environments
* Ability to identify areas for improved efficiency and suggest solutions.
* Advanced knowledge of actuarial science, mathematics, probability, statistics, principles of finance and business, and strong ability to apply this knowledge to problems in insurance
* Ability to develop efficient and viable solutions to problems and assess impacts of these solution options Strong attention to detail when performing analysis with minimal technical errors
* Ability to identify errors and areas of concern that may significantly impact analysis.
* Ability to peer review through reasonability checks Ability to balance materiality/efficiency and accuracy/detail.
* Ability to prioritize and complete multiple concurrent assignments, both individually and as a leader of a group, in a timely manner while proactively keeping leader informed of progress.
* Develops timelines for project plans Ability to work efficiently and co\-operatively in a team environment.
* Ability to respectfully challenge status quo.
* Seeks input from others to get stronger results.
* Keeps abreast of current industry and professional standards and applies them directly to requirements and decisions.
 **Diversity Equity, Inclusion \& Belonging**  

  

At Wawanesa, we are committed to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) and believe that our strength lies in the diversity of our people – this is supported by having a representative workforce.  


We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, including racialized persons, women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, members of the 2SLGBTQIA\+ community, gender\-diverse and neurodiverse individuals, and anyone who can contribute to the further diversification of thought and ideas.  


We aim to ensure our recruitment process is accessible to all candidates. If you require accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please reach out in confidence to jobs@wawanesa.com.  


All Wawanesa job applicants are subject to Wawanesa's Privacy Policy.

**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* Strong copywriting and editing skills with the ability to tailor messaging across platforms and audiences is required.
* Proven ability to develop and execute integrated marketing strategies aligned with organizational goals, including audience segmentation and multi\-channel communication approaches Is required.

**Key responsibilities**
------------------------



**MARKETING AND COMMUNICATIONS:**

* **Design and Visual Content:** Develop visually appealing marketing and communications materials using design tools (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite), including brochures, event materials, presentations, digital graphics, and other promotional assets. Ensure digital content and materials meet university accessibility standards. Maintain and update a broad library of branded templates and documents to **support consistent, professional communications across the organization.**
* **Campaign Coordination:** Lead the planning and end\-to\-end execution of integrated marketing campaigns to promote programs, events, and organizational initiatives, including social media, paid advertising, and email marketing. Manage campaign timelines, coordinate deliverables, monitor performance, and track expenditures to ensure campaigns **remain within budget, while providing regular reporting to leadership.**

Digital Platform and Systems Management: Oversee and maintain the organization's digital infrastructure, including the Executive Education website, online registration platform, email subscriber lists, and online forms. Coordinate with internal teams and external vendors to ensure platforms are accurate, functional, and up to date.

Coordinate with external vendors and suppliers for stock asset procurement and platform updates. Conduct periodic reviews of peer and competitor marketing activities to benchmark approaches and identify opportunities to strengthen program promotion.

description:

**Location:** Winnipeg, MB (Hybrid), CA Lloydminster, AB (Hybrid), CA Calgary, AB (Hybrid), CA Ottawa, ON (Hybrid), CA North York \- Toronto, ON (Hybr, CA Thunder Bay, ON (Hybrid), CA Dartmouth, NS (Hybrid), CA Wawanesa, MB (Hybrid), CA Moncton, NB (Hybrid), CA Lethbridge, AB (Hybrid), CA Edmonton, AB (Hybrid), CA Kitchener, ON (Hybrid), CA Montreal, QC (Hybrid), CA Vancouver, BC (Hybrid), CA
**Company:** Wawanesa Insurance  



**Job ID:** 10011  

**Employment Type:** Existing Role  

  

**Working Business Language**: English. This role is considered a head\-office role and will be required to communicate with internal stakeholders across Canada where the primary business language utilized is English.  

**Salary:** At Wawanesa, salary is only one component of a holistic, comprehensive and competitive offering that we provide to our employees. In addition to salary, full\-time and part\-time permanent employees are eligible for an annual bonus plan, leave of absence top\-up programs and provided with generous vacation time, personal days, premium free benefits and pension plan.  


The salary offered for this role is determined with consideration to various factors, including but not limited to: your work location, local labour market conditions, external market salary data, internal pay equity and the knowledge, skills, experience and anticipated proficiency in the role. The salary offered is estimated to be within the following range: $90,000 \- $140,000\. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are still encouraged to apply.  


**About Us**  

At Wawanesa, we offer a hybrid work environment that offers flexibility to our employees in balancing in\-office (2 days per week OR 15 hours per week in a Wawanesa office) and remote work. You may work from any of the following locations: Winnipeg, MB; Wawanesa, MB; Vancouver, BC; Calgary, AB; Edmonton, AB; Lethbridge, AB; Toronto (North York), ON; Kitchener, ON; Ottawa, ON; Montreal, QC; Quebec City, QC, Moncton, NB; Dartmouth; NS.  


The Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company (“Wawanesa Mutual”), founded in 1896, is one of Canada’s largest mutual insurers, with over $3\.5 billion in annual revenue and assets of $10 billion (CAD). Wawanesa Mutual, with its National Headquarters in Winnipeg, is the parent company of Wawanesa Life, which provides life insurance products and services throughout Canada, and Western Financial Group, which distributes personal and business insurance across Canada. Wawanesa proudly serves more than 1\.7 million members in Canada, and we are home to more than 3,300 employees distributed across the Canadian regions and communities where we operate. We give back to organizations that strengthen communities, donating more than $3\.5 million annually to charitable organizations, including over $2 million annually in support of people on the front lines of climate change. We are also proud to be recognized as one of Manitoba’s Top Employers. To learn more visit wawanesa.com.  

We are currently looking for dedicated, driven, and enthusiastic individuals who thrive in an environment that welcomes change and are looking for an opportunity for diverse experience and advancement on a growing team. **Job Overview**

Working with a high degree of autonomy, the Actuarial Consultant is responsible for leading development of reporting that leads to actionable business insights used by the Personal Pricing department. The successful candidate will collaborate with Data Analysts and apply their knowledge to complex actuarial analysis in the pursuit of solving business issues and answering business questions.  


Note: we are open to hiring at the Senior Actuarial Analyst, Actuarial Consultant or Senior Actuarial Consultant level**Job Responsibilities**
* Researches, extracts, and manipulates complex data from relevant sources, and assesses data quality.
* Supports the business and overall team by performing analyses/reviews and completing ad\-hoc requests/special projects in a timely manner with minimal support from peers and leaders.
* Evaluates procedures and processes, and recommend alternatives or improvements.
* Evaluates existing processes to find areas for increased efficiency Identifies opportunities for automation of routine tasks and implementation of advanced techniques while ensuring ethical and responsible application of solutions.
* Develops new approaches to problem solving and assess impacts of solution options with minimal support from leaders Identifies emerging industry trends and analyzes the impact they will have on Wawanesa.
* Encouraged to represent Wawanesa at external events that may include universities, actuarial clubs, industry organizations, and charitable causes
* Commits to professional development and continuous experiential learning to build cross\-functional knowledge, technical skills, and mastery in competencies.
* Focuses learning activities on bringing new knowledge and skills to team in order to address any existing gaps.
* Provides regular project updates to stakeholders. Anticipates obstacles and raises them in advance. Leads projects from beginning to end. Includes implementation of final product. May include training and documentation of the project.
* Actively contributes to the mentorship, training and development of actuarial analysts.
* Provides feedback, instruction, and development guidance to help others excel in their current or future job responsibilities Builds trust and credibility with peers.
* Develops strong working relationships across teams.
**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or other related discipline.
* More than four years of actuarial experience an asset 7 CAS exams or ACAS designation an asset.
* Advanced oral and written communication skills, demonstrating the ability to convey business terminology and insights that are meaningful and well received.
* Ability to clearly document major projects and key processes .
* Effective at comfortably communicating analysis and results to senior leaders in written and/or oral form, and influencing direct leader to accept recommendations.
* Discusses ideas in terms familiar to colleagues in different functional areas and teaches the necessary actuarial concepts when needed.
* Proficient with Excel, SQL, R, Python and/or other related software/programming languages/actuarial software AWS (S3 and Sagemaker) or Power BI experience would be an asset.
* Experience with Databricks or similar platform would be an asset.
* Ability to easily adapt to new enterprise system environments
* Ability to identify areas for improved efficiency and suggest solutions.
* Advanced knowledge of actuarial science, mathematics, probability, statistics, principles of finance and business, and strong ability to apply this knowledge to problems in insurance
* Ability to develop efficient and viable solutions to problems and assess impacts of these solution options Strong attention to detail when performing analysis with minimal technical errors
* Ability to identify errors and areas of concern that may significantly impact analysis.
* Ability to peer review through reasonability checks Ability to balance materiality/efficiency and accuracy/detail.
* Ability to prioritize and complete multiple concurrent assignments, both individually and as a leader of a group, in a timely manner while proactively keeping leader informed of progress.
* Develops timelines for project plans Ability to work efficiently and co\-operatively in a team environment.
* Ability to respectfully challenge status quo.
* Seeks input from others to get stronger results.
* Keeps abreast of current industry and professional standards and applies them directly to requirements and decisions.
 **Diversity Equity, Inclusion \& Belonging**  

  

At Wawanesa, we are committed to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) and believe that our strength lies in the diversity of our people – this is supported by having a representative workforce.  


We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, including racialized persons, women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, members of the 2SLGBTQIA\+ community, gender\-diverse and neurodiverse individuals, and anyone who can contribute to the further diversification of thought and ideas.  


We aim to ensure our recruitment process is accessible to all candidates. If you require accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please reach out in confidence to jobs@wawanesa.com.  


All Wawanesa job applicants are subject to Wawanesa's Privacy Policy.  


Please note that the recruitment process for this position may involve the use of AI tools to screen, assess, or select applicants. All final decisions are taken or reviewed by human recruiters and human hiring leaders in compliance with all applicable legislation.

title: Senior Research Associate, Independent Advice on Manitoba Hydro’s Integrated Resource Plan
description:
**Senior Research Associates:**



Under the general direction of, and in collaboration with researchers and faculty members, positions at this level apply extensive knowledge in their field to lead high\-level, complex research projects. Compared to Research Associates, these positions exercise greater input and judgment over the research, may be involved in setting research program objectives, and may be responsible for managing the team and project funds. Senior Research Associates would normally hold a doctoral degree or have equivalent qualifications and experience.


**Position Overview:**



The primary objective of this study is to provide independent expert advise surrounding Manitoba Hydro’s approach to *meaningful* engagement with the public.



The Senior Research Associate will serve as a quantitative data analyst with expertise in managing and analyzing survey datasets, including conducting descriptive and inferential statistical analyses to assess the reliability and robustness of findings. The role will involve performing cross\-tabulations and statistical tests to identify differences across demographic groups, determining whether observed variations are statistically significant, and applying additional quantitative techniques—such as regression modelling, weighting, data cleaning, and quality\-assurance checks—to ensure the accuracy and interpretability of results.



They will collaborate with the Principal Investigator in establishing the research objectives and analytical protocols and will independently conduct the analysis using appropriate statistical methods. They will create data visualizations illustrating the results in an accessible way. They will also assist in summarizing the findings according to industry best practices and will participate in other knowledge mobilization opportunities.


**Duties and Responsibilities:**


* Collaborate with principal investigator to establish research objectives, analytical protocols and guidelines.
* Oversee project management of the analysis of research results and data outputs.
* Conduct appropriate descriptive and inferential data analysis on survey data.
* Create data visualizations (charts, graphs) for presentations and publication.
* Present project findings to stakeholders via conference presentations.
* Co\-author an article about the project for publication in an academic journal.


**Qualifications:**


* Master’s degree or higher in Statistics, Psychology, Community Health Sciences, Science with equivalent qualifications and experience
* Experience supporting academic and community projects with their research and evaluation needs
* Experience using SPSS software in conducting data analysis
* Experience performing data analysis in an academic or community setting
* Experience conducting independent research and data analysis
* Experience leading research projects
* Analytical and decision\-making skills
* Excellent communication skills
* Excellent writing skills in an academic environment
* Prior academic publication history is an asset.


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**


**Note:** The work described in this posting will be conducted remotely and in\-person.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion and recognizes that a diverse staff and faculty benefits and enriches the work, learning and research environments, and is essential to academic and institutional excellence. title: Research Associate, Zinc-Mediated Mitigation of Cadmium Uptake in Flax
description:
**\*This is a term position until December 31, 2027\***


**Research Associates:**



Under the general direction of, and in collaboration with researchers and faculty members, positions at this level apply extensive knowledge in their field to perform high\-level, complex research activities, and will normally hold a Master’s degree. Individuals with training beyond a Master’s degree who have limited experience may also be appointed into this classification level.


**Position Overview:**



The Research Associate (RA) will contribute to a multidisciplinary project focused on understanding and mitigating cadmium accumulation in flax through zinc treatments. The role involves experimental design, implementation of greenhouse trials, and advanced analytical techniques including hyperspectral imaging and heavy metal quantification. The RA will work closely with a research team to evaluate both foliar and soil\-applied Zn strategies and assess plant physiological responses under Cd stress.


**Skills and Abilities:**


* Strong background in plant science, agronomy, soil science, or a related field is required.
* Experience with plant physiology experiments and stress response analysis is required.
* Knowledge of hyperspectral imaging techniques is an asset.
* Ability to design, execute, and troubleshoot experiments independently.
* Proficiency in data analysis and statistical software (e.g., R, Python, SAS).
* Strong organizational and documentation skills.
* Effective written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a research environment.


**Duties and Responsibilities:**


* Design and implement experiments, data collection protocols, and standards.
* Determine the appropriate methodology to carry out the identified research goals.
* Conduct high\-level and complex academic research and literature searches.
* Collect and manage research data, conduct advanced analysis of research results and document findings.
* Interpret research findings, perform editorial tasks, and present project status and insights to stakeholders.
* Organize and host seminars, focus groups, community meetings, special events, and other forms of academic and community engagement.
* Identify and assist in securing funding opportunities; prepare and submit documentations required by funding agencies.
* Support project procurement and staff supervision.
* Contribute to overall activities and internal operations related to the research project.
* Perform other duties, including administrative support, as required or assigned.


**Qualifications:**


* Master’s or PhD degree in a relevant field of study.
* Experience in conducting independent research, start to finish.
* Experience in writing technical reports, including peer\-reviewed articles, is required.


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* **Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.**


This position **is** eligible for extended health benefits, **excluding** pension.


***Note****: This position is represented by the Public Service Alliance of Canada \- Research Capacity Unit.*



The University of Winnipeg is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion and recognizes that a diverse staff and faculty benefits and enriches the work, learning and research environments, and is essential to academic and institutional excellence. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and encourage women, racialized persons, Indigenous persons, persons with disabilities, and 2SLGBTQ\+ persons to confidentially self\-identify at time of application.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to ensuring employment opportunities are accessible for all applicants. If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.



The personal information of applicants is collected under the authority of the University of Winnipeg Act and 36(1\)(b) of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. All personal information collected via the recruitment process is used to assess the applicant’s suitability, eligibility, and qualifications for employment, and to otherwise support recruitment activities. This information will be provided to participating members of the recruitment process. Questions regarding the collection of your personal information may be directed to the Director, HR Services, 515 Portage Avenue, Winnipeg, MB, R3B 2E9 or human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.

title: Data Collector (N2) - Critical Care Med Info Mgmt & Research
Comp System Validation & Data Mgmt Analyst - IT3 (AESES)
description:
Department of Community Health Sciences
Rady Faculty of Health Sciences  

Position number: 37631
Date posted: May 4, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Comp System Validation \& Data Mgmt Analyst \- IT3 (AESES)

New Project Full\-Time (Budget Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: No
  

Work schedule: 35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:00 am to 4:00 pm)
  

Salary: $33\.08 to $47\.72 per hour ($60,205\.60 to $86,850\.40 per annum)
  

Appointment Dates June 8, 2026 to June 4, 2027
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Andrea Toyne \- andrea.toyne@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------


**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING REQUIRED:**


* Post\-secondary degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, IT Operations, Cybersecurity, Clinical Trials and Regulatory Affairs, or a related scientific/technological discipline.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* At least one year of direct experience in Computerized System Validation (CSV) where it was a primary \& full\-time responsibility, is a very strong asset.
* Direct experience with Quality Assurance in software development is preferred.
* Experience ensuring compliance with ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5, 21 CFR Part 11, the Tri\-Council Policy Statement on the Ethical Conduct for Research Involving Humans (TCPS), and the Personal Health Information Act of Manitoba (or equivalent local privacy legislation) is a strong asset.
* Experience with clinical research involving human participants is an asset.
* Experience with full\-stack web development/administration, including HTTP server technology, computer networking, server\-side scripting, database administration, and knowledge of Microsoft Internet Information services (IIS) and virtualization environments, are all assets.
* Experience with Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems, such as REDCap, is highly valued.
* Experience with Health Canada regulated clinical trials and the associated data collection processes is preferred.
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* A strong understanding of Computerized System Validation and software Quality Assurance principles, including risk\-based testing and validation documentation, is essential.
* Practical working knowledge of probability theory and statistics.
* Excellent oral and written communication skills are essential, including the ability to clearly communicate technical information to both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Ability to set priorities, multi\-task, and work effectively under time\-constrains required.
* Must be able to work in complex team environment with researchers, clinicians, and representatives from external agencies, such as government departments.
* Must be able to work independently with minimal supervision.
* Ability to diagnose and resolve software and system problems is required.
* Ability to develop strong interpersonal working relationships is required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.
  


**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


**Responsibilities:**


**Develop and implement of a Computerized System Validation (CSV) framework for Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems (REDCap) at the University of Manitoba:**


* Develop a Computerized System Validation Plan for REDCap systems at the University of Manitoba.
* Improve, write, and finalize Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and develop associated workflow processes described by the Validation Plan.
* Ensuring that the REDCap infrastructure administered by IT department complies with standards that the Analyst will help develop. Analyst will work directly with the IT department to configure REDCap application stack in compliance with the Validation Plan and SOPs. Analyst will be directly responsible for performing, documenting, and validating installations and upgrades to REDCap application stack.
* Develop and use a REDCap database project to identify Critical\-to\-Quality factors in EDC processes, determine associated risks, perform and record testing, and report validation status throughout the system lifecycle.
* Monitor current and emerging regulatory guidance (e.g., ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5\) and ensures that corresponding updates are implemented and documented within Validation Plan and SOPs.
* Collaborates with external institutions involved in centralized validation efforts, providing in\-kind consultation and testing support.
* Develops a genericized version of the Validation Plan/SOPs suitable for adoption by other institutions running REDCap, and takes an active role in a pan\-Canadian validation committee for institutions running REDCap. If possible, the analyst will be supported by the DCC Lead in the authorship of a publication in trade/scientific journals detailing their methodology.
* Develops and implements a framework for testing the accuracy, safety, and security of external software modules integrated into REDCap systems.
* Responds to audits and inspections by regulatory authorities. Provides, explains, and contextualizes CSV documentation in the context of regulatory inspections.
* Develops and enforces risk\-proportionate quality assurance (QA) standards and processes for statistical programming. Ensures that data processing activities and study status reports provided to investigators are accurate, validated, and compliant with established QA standards.


**Data Management:**


* Develops and maintains an understanding of REDCap project design in the context of clinical research and Health Canada regulated clinical trials.
* Designs, maintains, and improves REDCap database projects for research and operational uses.
* Performs basic descriptive analysis, data quality monitoring, data processing, and visualizations of imperfect datasets using the R programming language.
* Assists with REDCap system administration, including user and project management, compliance monitoring, and training coordination.
* Schedules and coordinates training events in consultation with relevant user and stakeholder groups.
* Responds to REDCap enquiries from the research community and troubleshoots research database issues.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.

--------------------------
