description:
**Job Posting: Program Coordinator**  
**Location:** North East Winnipeg  
**Organization:** S.P.I.K.E. Inc.  
**Employment Type:** Full\-Time

S.P.I.K.E. Inc. is seeking a dedicated and organized Program Coordinator to join our team in North East Winnipeg. Reporting to the Program Manager, this position is responsible for the day\-to\-day operations of our client care programs, ensuring services are delivered in line with individualized care plans, financial goals, and regulatory standards.

This role plays a critical part in ensuring the well\-being of our clients while supporting staff and maintaining program efficiency. If you’re passionate about making a difference and have strong leadership, communication, and problem\-solving skills, we encourage you to apply.

Key Responsibilities

**Client Care and Well\-Being**

* Oversee the physical, emotional, and social well\-being of all clients.
* Ensure care plans are current, followed, and tailored to each individual.
* Work closely with clients, families, and interdisciplinary teams to address needs.
* Monitor progress and adjust care plans as necessary.
* Support clients in achieving personal goals and enhancing independence.
* Ensure clients are enrolled in and maintaining essential benefits such as OAS, Pharmacare, and Manitoba Supports.

**Financial and Care Plan Management**

* Maintain and update financial and care plans in compliance with policies and standards.
* Ensure adherence to client care budgets and proper allocation of resources.
* Monitor expenditures and promote cost\-effective practices.
* Fulfill reporting requirements related to service agreements with CLDS and RCL.
* Submit regular financial and care plan updates to management.
* Obtain quotes for special purchases or emergencies up to $500\.

**Staff Supervision and Development**

* Supervise and support staff in the homes, ensuring quality service delivery.
* Conduct performance evaluations and assist with professional development.
* Ensure staff are trained and up to date on care practices and safety protocols.
* Assist with scheduling and conflict resolution.
* Lead staff meetings and foster a collaborative work environment.

**Compliance and Reporting**

* Maintain accurate, confidential records in accordance with FIPPA, PHIA, and The Adults Living with an Intellectual Disability Act.
* Submit regular reports on client care, staff performance, and operations.
* Monitor and report on residence maintenance and safety.
* Audit daily operations to ensure efficiency and regulatory compliance.

**Collaboration and Communication**

* Serve as a point of contact for clients, staff, and families.
* Work with external service providers and community resources to support client needs.
* Participate in organizational committees, such as health and safety or pension planning.

**Other Duties**

* Perform additional tasks and projects as assigned by management.

**Requirements:**

* Valid Class 5F MB driver’s license
* Clear Criminal Record Check with Vulnerable Sectors Search
* Clear Adult and Child Abuse Registry Checks
* First Aid and CPR Level C
* NVCI Certification and ASL (asset)

**Location:** In\-person at our facilities.

**Apply Now!** Ready to make a difference? Send your resume and cover letter to careers@spikeinc.org

**S.P.I.K.E. Inc. is an equal opportunity employer.**

**Join us in transforming lives!**

title: Project Manager II
description:
**Assiniboine Credit Union is hiring for the position of a permanent Full\-Time Project Manager II to work at the PMO \& Integration department.**


**About this opportunity:**


* This role requires to work 37\.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday.
* This role will be an hybrid position: some days working from home, and other days at the Head Office (540 St Anne's Rd, Winnipeg MB, R2M 5R7\).
* The position offers a competitive compensation plan, including health and dental benefits, matched pension, and 3 weeks of vacation.

  



Reporting to the Manager, Project Management, Project Manager II is a senior project management professional responsible for leading large\-scale, complex, and high\-impact initiatives that advances ACU’s strategic priorities. This role owns the delivery of multi\-stream projects with significant organizational risk, complexity, and interdependencies, requiring advanced judgment, autonomy, and stakeholder influence.



Project Manager II acts as a trusted advisor to leaders, enforces and evolves PMO standards, and mentors Project Manager I peers. Drawing on deep experience and PMI/PMBOK best practices, this role ensures disciplined delivery, strong governance, and sustained business outcomes aligned with Assiniboine Credit Union/ Caisse Assiniboine (ACU)’s commitment to rural and agricultural communities, francophone services, values\-based banking and ACU’s triple bottom line accountability to people, planet, and prosperity.

  


**Responsibilities:**

**Project Management:**

* Lead the end\-to\-end delivery of complex, cross\-functional projects, ensuring alignment with approved scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and dependencies.
* Own and manage project planning, governance, and delivery execution, exercising independent judgment to resolve issues and escalating only material risks.
* Provide executive\-level project reporting and decision support, including status, risk, change impacts, and recommendations.
* Lead cross\-functional project teams, fostering accountability, collaboration, and high performance.
* Ensure effective risk, issue, and quality management, including smooth transitions to operations, and measurement of post\-implementation outcomes and benefits realization.
* Oversee third\-party vendors and contractors; supporting contract negotiations, and ensure delivery and contractual compliance.
* Ensure all project documentation meets PMO standards and audit requirement; capture lessons learned to strengthen delivery practices.


**Business Analysis \& Stakeholder Engagement:**


* Act as a trusted advisor to business units, IT/Digital teams, and vendors to support informed decision making and solution delivery.
* Support complex business process analysis and redesign initiatives
* Oversee the translation of business needs into functional requirements and manage scope and changes throughout the solution lifecycle.
* Support build\-versus\-buy assessments and oversee user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure solutions meet business needs
* Lead stakeholder engagement activities, including workshops and interviews to identify opportunities and drive effective change adoption.


**Continuous Improvement**


* Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance PMO processes, tools, and delivery practices.
* Drive the consistent adoption and evolution of project management standards across the organization.
* Mentor and coach Project Manager I peers, supporting PMO capability development and maturity.
* Apply lessons learned to improve future project delivery outcomes and overall value realization.

  


**EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE**



The competencies for the position would typically be acquired through 5\-7\+ years of progressive project management experience and a related post\-secondary degree or diploma, or an equivalent combination of education and experience. A Project Management Professional (PMP) designation is required.



Business Analysis certification (CBAP) and Organizational Change Management training are considered strong assets. Practical and operational business experience and knowledge within the financial services sector is required. Additional formal certifications are preferred.


**Key Occupational Skills**


* Strong understanding and application of PMBOK practices within regulated environments
* Ability to lead complex, multi\-stream projects, managing scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and dependencies
* Strong portfolio\-level judgment, including prioritization, sequencing, and trade\-off analysis
* Proven ability to lead and influence cross\-functional teams and senior stakeholders without direct authority
* Advanced stakeholder engagement and executive\-level communication skills
* Understanding of technological trends and the ability to apply them to delivery effective business solutions
* Ability to translate business needs into functional requirements and oversee solution validation and user acceptance testing (UAT)
* Strong analytical and systems thinking skills, with experience in business process redesign and solution enablement
* Demonstrated coaching and mentoring capability to support PMO maturity and peer development
* Experience managing third‑party vendors, including contract and performance oversight
* Advanced knowledge in quality control and assurance processes, particularly related to testing leading and facilitation
* Strong continuous\-improvement mindset, with the ability to strengthen project delivery standards, tools, and practices

  


**WHO WE ARE?**



As a Certified B Corporation® and one of Manitoba’s Top Employers (2024\), the culture at Assiniboine Credit Union / Caisse Assiniboine is supportive of the individual while focused on the greater community. With the widest geographic reach of any credit union in Manitoba, you’ll be motivated by shared values and encouraged to always think about how, through financial sustainability, we can do more for our members, our employees and our community.



Choosing to work at Assiniboine Credit Union means that you’ll be working for a uniquely Manitoban credit union that:


* puts people, planet and prosperity first,
* values diversity and inclusion,
* is committed to serving its urban, rural, agricultural, and Francophone communities,
* is focused on social and environmental responsibility,
* supports employees through training programs and long\-term career development,
* offers a competitive total compensation program which includes bonuses, a matched pension program, and health and dental benefits.


If doing things differently and doing more is part of who you are, join us on a rewarding journey where your personal aspirations and career goals can align.

  

**If you are interested in applying for the Project Manager II role, please submit your application and updated resume by Friday, May 15th, 2026, 4:00 pm.** We thank everyone who applies, but only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.  

  

*We are committed to workforce diversity and actively recruit people with diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives reflecting the community in which we live and work. Applicants may request reasonable accommodation related to the materials and activities used throughout the selection process.*

  


*\#ACU2026*

840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Andrea Toyne \- andrea.toyne@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------


**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING REQUIRED:**


* Post\-secondary degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, IT Operations, Cybersecurity, Clinical Trials and Regulatory Affairs, or a related scientific/technological discipline.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* At least one year of direct experience in Computerized System Validation (CSV) where it was a primary \& full\-time responsibility, is a very strong asset.
* Direct experience with Quality Assurance in software development is preferred.
* Experience ensuring compliance with ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5, 21 CFR Part 11, the Tri\-Council Policy Statement on the Ethical Conduct for Research Involving Humans (TCPS), and the Personal Health Information Act of Manitoba (or equivalent local privacy legislation) is a strong asset.
* Experience with clinical research involving human participants is an asset.
* Experience with full\-stack web development/administration, including HTTP server technology, computer networking, server\-side scripting, database administration, and knowledge of Microsoft Internet Information services (IIS) and virtualization environments, are all assets.
* Experience with Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems, such as REDCap, is highly valued.
* Experience with Health Canada regulated clinical trials and the associated data collection processes is preferred.
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* A strong understanding of Computerized System Validation and software Quality Assurance principles, including risk\-based testing and validation documentation, is essential.
* Practical working knowledge of probability theory and statistics.
* Excellent oral and written communication skills are essential, including the ability to clearly communicate technical information to both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Ability to set priorities, multi\-task, and work effectively under time\-constrains required.
* Must be able to work in complex team environment with researchers, clinicians, and representatives from external agencies, such as government departments.
* Must be able to work independently with minimal supervision.
* Ability to diagnose and resolve software and system problems is required.
* Ability to develop strong interpersonal working relationships is required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.
  


**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


**Responsibilities:**


**Develop and implement of a Computerized System Validation (CSV) framework for Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems (REDCap) at the University of Manitoba:**


* Develop a Computerized System Validation Plan for REDCap systems at the University of Manitoba.
* Improve, write, and finalize Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and develop associated workflow processes described by the Validation Plan.
* Ensuring that the REDCap infrastructure administered by IT department complies with standards that the Analyst will help develop. Analyst will work directly with the IT department to configure REDCap application stack in compliance with the Validation Plan and SOPs. Analyst will be directly responsible for performing, documenting, and validating installations and upgrades to REDCap application stack.
* Develop and use a REDCap database project to identify Critical\-to\-Quality factors in EDC processes, determine associated risks, perform and record testing, and report validation status throughout the system lifecycle.
* Monitor current and emerging regulatory guidance (e.g., ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5\) and ensures that corresponding updates are implemented and documented within Validation Plan and SOPs.
* Collaborates with external institutions involved in centralized validation efforts, providing in\-kind consultation and testing support.
* Develops a genericized version of the Validation Plan/SOPs suitable for adoption by other institutions running REDCap, and takes an active role in a pan\-Canadian validation committee for institutions running REDCap. If possible, the analyst will be supported by the DCC Lead in the authorship of a publication in trade/scientific journals detailing their methodology.
* Develops and implements a framework for testing the accuracy, safety, and security of external software modules integrated into REDCap systems.
* Responds to audits and inspections by regulatory authorities. Provides, explains, and contextualizes CSV documentation in the context of regulatory inspections.
* Develops and enforces risk\-proportionate quality assurance (QA) standards and processes for statistical programming. Ensures that data processing activities and study status reports provided to investigators are accurate, validated, and compliant with established QA standards.


**Data Management:**


* Develops and maintains an understanding of REDCap project design in the context of clinical research and Health Canada regulated clinical trials.
* Designs, maintains, and improves REDCap database projects for research and operational uses.
* Performs basic descriptive analysis, data quality monitoring, data processing, and visualizations of imperfect datasets using the R programming language.
* Assists with REDCap system administration, including user and project management, compliance monitoring, and training coordination.
* Schedules and coordinates training events in consultation with relevant user and stakeholder groups.
* Responds to REDCap enquiries from the research community and troubleshoots research database issues.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.

Under the general direction of, and in collaboration with researchers and faculty members, positions at this level apply extensive knowledge in their field to lead high\-level, complex research projects. Senior Research Associates would normally hold a doctoral degree or have equivalent qualifications and experience.


**Position Overview:**



Dr. Caleb Hasler is looking to hire a Senior Research Associate on a **full\-time capacity (35 hours/week) for six weeks** to assist with ongoing research directives.

description:
Office of the Vice\-President(Research \& International)
University of Manitoba

Advertised until: June 5, 2026 or until filled  

**Job details**
---------------



The Associate Vice President (Research) is appointed for a five (5\) year fixed term, renewable once for an additional five (5\) year term, subject to performance and institutional requirements. This position is a senior administrative appointment and is not a tenure track or tenured position.



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: No
  

Work schedule:
40 Hour/Week

  

Salary:
Salary will be commesurate with qualifications and experience

  

Appointment Dates July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2031  


  

  

For more information please contact: Allison Stephen \- allison.stephen@umanitoba.ca



The Associate Vice\-President (Research) (AVP(R)) is a senior academic leader responsible for advancing the University of Manitoba’s research mission by providing institutional leadership and operational oversight across the full spectrum of research administration. The AVP(R) strengthens research quality and capacity, oversees internal funding mechanisms, ensures compliance with research policies and regulations, and supports researchers at all career stages. This role is primarily administrative in nature and constitutes a senior executive position.


**Qualifications**
------------------


* PhD and an established record of research achievement.
* Demonstrated leadership experience in research administration, academic governance, or compliance oversight.
* Demonstrated senior administrative experience.
* Strong understanding of Tri‑Agency policies, RCR frameworks, research ethics, animal care regulations, biosafety, and CFI processes.
* Experience chairing committees, leading complex evaluations, and managing research support units.
* Commitment to advancing inclusivity in research.
* Strong communication, analytical, and collaborative leadership skills.
**Key responsibilities**
------------------------



**Strategic Research Leadership \& Policy Development**


* Provide leadership in developing and implementing research policies, frameworks, and evaluation practices that enhance research excellence and integrity.
* Lead ongoing improvements to internal review and adjudication processes, ensuring fairness, transparency, and principles of inclusivity.
* Participate in and oversee academic reviews to ensure research quality and alignment with institutional standards.
* Contribute to institutional research planning and strategic initiatives led by the VPRI.


**Oversight of Internal Research Funding Programs**


* Lead the administration, design, and continuous improvement of internal funding programs.
* Chair the Internal Grants Program Committee, ensuring efficient operation of competition cycles and high\-quality adjudication.
* Chair the Internal CFI (Canada Foundation for Innovation) Committee, overseeing internal review and prioritization of CFI proposals.
* Monitor participation, success rates, and program performance to ensure internal funds effectively support research development and strategic priorities.


**Research Compliance, Integrity \& Ethics**


* Assist with the investigation phase of Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) cases, exercising delegated authority from the VPRI and ensuring adherence to institutional policies and national frameworks.
* Assure compliance in searches for externally funded research chairs (e.g., CRC, CERC, Canada Impact\+ Research Chairs), ensuring processes meet Tri‑Agency and institutional requirements.


**Oversight of Research Ethics, Animal Care \& Biosafety**


* Provide senior oversight of institutional Animal Care and Biosafety functions, ensuring regulatory compliance and best practices.
* Support and liaise with the responsible committees and units to maintain high standards for ethics, safety, and responsible conduct in research.
* Ensure alignment with national and international guidelines governing animal care and biohazardous materials.


**Direct Leadership of Research Administration Units**


* Provide direct supervision of the Office of Research Services (ORS), including internal grants, external funding support, and research development services.
* Provide direct supervision of human research ethics coordination, animal care, biosafety, and RCR administration.
* Ensure these units operate efficiently, consistently, and in alignment with institutional strategic priorities.


**Researcher Support \& Capacity Building**


* Strengthen support for researchers at all career stages through mentorship initiatives, internal funding, and equitable evaluation practices.
* Identify gaps in research support systems and lead initiatives to improve researcher success and grant competitiveness.
* Enhance participation in internal and external funding opportunities across faculties.


**Institutional \& Cross‑Campus Collaboration**


* Lead the development and delivery of major research\-focused seminar series and engagement programs such as the UM Knowledge Exchange, Canada Research Chair (CRC) Symposium, Early\-Career Researcher (ECR) Symposium, and the Best Practices in Research Management series.
* Serve as a university liaison to the Health Ethics Review Committee (HERC) and other regulatory or advisory bodies as required.
* Represent the VPRI portfolio on committees, task forces, working groups that influence research policy, compliance, and support, and the Senate and its sub\-committees, as required.
**Additional information**
--------------------------



The University of Manitoba is a driving force of innovation, discovery and advancement. Our momentum is propelled by our campus community – UM faculty, staff and students whose determination and curiosity shape our world for the better. Our teaching, learning and work environment is uniquely strengthened and enriched by Indigenous perspectives. With two main campuses in Winnipeg, satellite campuses throughout Manitoba, and world\-wide research, UM’s impact is global.

**Job details**

Comp System Validation \& Data Mgmt Analyst \- IT3 (AESES)

New Project Full\-Time (Budget Funded)

Full\-time:YesPermanent:NoWork schedule:35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:00 am to 4:00 pm)Salary:$33\.08 to $47\.72 per hour ($60,205\.60 to $86,850\.40 per annum)Appointment DatesJune 8, 2026 to June 4, 2027Trial/Probation period:840 hours worked

For more information please contact: Andrea Toyne \- andrea.toyne@umanitoba.ca

Qualifications

**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING REQUIRED:**

* Post\-secondary degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, IT Operations, Cybersecurity, Clinical Trials and Regulatory Affairs, or a related scientific/technological discipline.

**EXPERIENCE:**

* At least one year of direct experience in Computerized System Validation (CSV) where it was a primary \& full\-time responsibility, is a very strong asset.
* Direct experience with Quality Assurance in software development is preferred.
* Experience ensuring compliance with ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5, 21 CFR Part 11, the Tri\-Council Policy Statement on the Ethical Conduct for Research Involving Humans (TCPS), and the Personal Health Information Act of Manitoba (or equivalent local privacy legislation) is a strong asset.
* Experience with clinical research involving human participants is an asset.
* Experience with full\-stack web development/administration, including HTTP server technology, computer networking, server\-side scripting, database administration, and knowledge of Microsoft Internet Information services (IIS) and virtualization environments, are all assets.
* Experience with Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems, such as REDCap, is highly valued.
* Experience with Health Canada regulated clinical trials and the associated data collection processes is preferred.
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**

* A strong understanding of Computerized System Validation and software Quality Assurance principles, including risk\-based testing and validation documentation, is essential.
* Practical working knowledge of probability theory and statistics.
* Excellent oral and written communication skills are essential, including the ability to clearly communicate technical information to both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Ability to set priorities, multi\-task, and work effectively under time\-constrains required.
* Must be able to work in complex team environment with researchers, clinicians, and representatives from external agencies, such as government departments.
* Must be able to work independently with minimal supervision.
* Ability to diagnose and resolve software and system problems is required.
* Ability to develop strong interpersonal working relationships is required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.

Key responsibilities

**Responsibilities:** 

**Develop and implement of a Computerized System Validation (CSV) framework for Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems (REDCap) at the University of Manitoba:**

* Develop a Computerized System Validation Plan for REDCap systems at the University of Manitoba.
* Improve, write, and finalize Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and develop associated workflow processes described by the Validation Plan.
* Ensuring that the REDCap infrastructure administered by IT department complies with standards that the Analyst will help develop. Analyst will work directly with the IT department to configure REDCap application stack in compliance with the Validation Plan and SOPs. Analyst will be directly responsible for performing, documenting, and validating installations and upgrades to REDCap application stack.
* Develop and use a REDCap database project to identify Critical\-to\-Quality factors in EDC processes, determine associated risks, perform and record testing, and report validation status throughout the system lifecycle.
* Monitor current and emerging regulatory guidance (e.g., ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5\) and ensures that corresponding updates are implemented and documented within Validation Plan and SOPs.
* Collaborates with external institutions involved in centralized validation efforts, providing in\-kind consultation and testing support.
* Develops a genericized version of the Validation Plan/SOPs suitable for adoption by other institutions running REDCap, and takes an active role in a pan\-Canadian validation committee for institutions running REDCap. If possible, the analyst will be supported by the DCC Lead in the authorship of a publication in trade/scientific journals detailing their methodology.
* Develops and implements a framework for testing the accuracy, safety, and security of external software modules integrated into REDCap systems.
* Responds to audits and inspections by regulatory authorities. Provides, explains, and contextualizes CSV documentation in the context of regulatory inspections.
* Develops and enforces risk\-proportionate quality assurance (QA) standards and processes for statistical programming. Ensures that data processing activities and study status reports provided to investigators are accurate, validated, and compliant with established QA standards.

**Data Management:**

* Develops and maintains an understanding of REDCap project design in the context of clinical research and Health Canada regulated clinical trials.
* Designs, maintains, and improves REDCap database projects for research and operational uses.
* Performs basic descriptive analysis, data quality monitoring, data processing, and visualizations of imperfect datasets using the R programming language.
* Assists with REDCap system administration, including user and project management, compliance monitoring, and training coordination.
* Schedules and coordinates training events in consultation with relevant user and stakeholder groups.
* Responds to REDCap enquiries from the research community and troubleshoots research database issues.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.

description:
**Iris Construction Management Inc.**: Project Manager/Estimator

**Company Overview:** Iris Construction Management Inc., a leading commercial design and build firm based in Winnipeg, MB, is dedicated to providing comprehensive construction solutions that enhance business operations. Our commitment to efficient strategies, high\-quality design, transparent budgeting, and precise construction management distinguishes us in the industry. Renowned for our reliability and ability to deliver projects on budget and on schedule, Iris is the preferred partner for clients seeking exceptional construction services.

**Position Summary:** We seek a versatile and experienced professional to fill the dual role of Project Manager/Estimator. This hybrid position is crucial in driving our estimation efforts and managing construction projects. The ideal candidate will have a solid background in both project management and cost estimation, with a strong understanding of the construction industry and a proven track record of delivering accurate bids and effective project oversight.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* **Cost Estimation:**
* Prepare detailed cost estimates for construction projects, covering all expenses and contingencies.
* Collaborate with project managers, engineers, and architects to understand project requirements and develop precise budget forecasts.
* Utilize advanced estimating techniques and software to improve accuracy and efficiency.
* Conduct market research to stay updated on industry trends, material costs, and labour rates.
* Engage with subcontractors and suppliers to gather and analyze bids, ensuring compliance with project specifications and standards.
* Present and discuss cost estimates with senior management and clients, clearly explaining methodologies.
* Participate in bid reviews and negotiations, offering strategic insights to enhance Iris’s competitive edge.

**Project Management:**

* Plan, coordinate, and oversee construction projects from initiation to completion.
* Develop and maintain detailed project schedules and ensure timely delivery of project milestones.
* Manage project budgets, including tracking expenses and ensuring projects remain within financial constraints.
* Supervise and direct construction teams, subcontractors, and suppliers, ensuring adherence to project plans and safety standards.
* Conduct regular site inspections to monitor progress, quality, and compliance with safety regulations.
* Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise during the construction process.
* Communicate effectively with clients, stakeholders, and team members to provide project updates and address concerns.
* Prepare and present project reports, including progress updates, cost analyses, and risk assessments.
* Ensure that all project documentation is complete, accurate, and up\-to\-date.

**Qualifications:**

* Degree in Construction Management, Engineering, or a related field.
* Minimum of 5 years’ experience in construction estimating, particularly in commercial design and build projects.
* Minimum of 5 years’ experience in construction project management or coordination.
* Proficiency in estimating software (e.g., OnScreen Takeoff, Bluebeam) and MS Excel.
* Strong analytical and mathematical skills with meticulous attention to detail.
* Excellent leadership and communication skills, capable of guiding a team and collaborating effectively with stakeholders.
* Residency in or willingness to relocate to Winnipeg, MB.
* Valid driver’s license and readiness for minor travel as needed.

**Why Join Iris?**

* **Competitive Compensation:** Salary, based on experience, plus performance\-based bonuses.
* **Comprehensive Benefits:** Health, dental, and life insurance, along with on\-site parking.
* **Professional Development:** Opportunities for career growth in a supportive and inclusive environment.
* **Work\-Life Balance:** A stable 8\-hour shift schedule promotes employee well\-being.

Iris Construction Management Inc. is committed to diversity and inclusivity in the workplace. We encourage all qualified candidates to apply, particularly those who contribute to the diversity of our team.

**Application Process:** Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter, and responses to the following questions:

* Do you have experience in commercial construction estimating?

company: The George & Fay Yee Centre for Healthcare Innovation
description:
***Apply for this opportunity here:*** https://viprecprod.ad.umanitoba.ca/DEFAULT.ASPX?REQ\_ID\=45914

Emailed resumes may not be reviewed.

**Job details**

Comp System Validation \& Data Mgmt Analyst \- IT3 (AESES)

New Project Full\-Time (Budget Funded)

Full\-time:YesPermanent:NoWork schedule:35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:00 am to 4:00 pm)Salary:$33\.08 to $47\.72 per hour ($60,205\.60 to $86,850\.40 per annum)Appointment DatesJune 8, 2026 to June 4, 2027Trial/Probation period:840 hours worked

For more information please contact: Andrea Toyne \- andrea.toyne@umanitoba.ca

Qualifications

**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING REQUIRED:**

* Post\-secondary degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, IT Operations, Cybersecurity, Clinical Trials and Regulatory Affairs, or a related scientific/technological discipline.

**EXPERIENCE:**

* At least one year of direct experience in Computerized System Validation (CSV) where it was a primary \& full\-time responsibility, is a very strong asset.
* Direct experience with Quality Assurance in software development is preferred.
* Experience ensuring compliance with ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5, 21 CFR Part 11, the Tri\-Council Policy Statement on the Ethical Conduct for Research Involving Humans (TCPS), and the Personal Health Information Act of Manitoba (or equivalent local privacy legislation) is a strong asset.
* Experience with clinical research involving human participants is an asset.
* Experience with full\-stack web development/administration, including HTTP server technology, computer networking, server\-side scripting, database administration, and knowledge of Microsoft Internet Information services (IIS) and virtualization environments, are all assets.
* Experience with Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems, such as REDCap, is highly valued.
* Experience with Health Canada regulated clinical trials and the associated data collection processes is preferred.
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**

* A strong understanding of Computerized System Validation and software Quality Assurance principles, including risk\-based testing and validation documentation, is essential.
* Practical working knowledge of probability theory and statistics.
* Excellent oral and written communication skills are essential, including the ability to clearly communicate technical information to both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Ability to set priorities, multi\-task, and work effectively under time\-constrains required.
* Must be able to work in complex team environment with researchers, clinicians, and representatives from external agencies, such as government departments.
* Must be able to work independently with minimal supervision.
* Ability to diagnose and resolve software and system problems is required.
* Ability to develop strong interpersonal working relationships is required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.

Key responsibilities

**Responsibilities:** 

**Develop and implement of a Computerized System Validation (CSV) framework for Electronic Data Capture (EDC) systems (REDCap) at the University of Manitoba:**

* Develop a Computerized System Validation Plan for REDCap systems at the University of Manitoba.
* Improve, write, and finalize Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and develop associated workflow processes described by the Validation Plan.
* Ensuring that the REDCap infrastructure administered by IT department complies with standards that the Analyst will help develop. Analyst will work directly with the IT department to configure REDCap application stack in compliance with the Validation Plan and SOPs. Analyst will be directly responsible for performing, documenting, and validating installations and upgrades to REDCap application stack.
* Develop and use a REDCap database project to identify Critical\-to\-Quality factors in EDC processes, determine associated risks, perform and record testing, and report validation status throughout the system lifecycle.
* Monitor current and emerging regulatory guidance (e.g., ICH\-GCP, Health Canada Division 5\) and ensures that corresponding updates are implemented and documented within Validation Plan and SOPs.
* Collaborates with external institutions involved in centralized validation efforts, providing in\-kind consultation and testing support.
* Develops a genericized version of the Validation Plan/SOPs suitable for adoption by other institutions running REDCap, and takes an active role in a pan\-Canadian validation committee for institutions running REDCap. If possible, the analyst will be supported by the DCC Lead in the authorship of a publication in trade/scientific journals detailing their methodology.
* Develops and implements a framework for testing the accuracy, safety, and security of external software modules integrated into REDCap systems.
* Responds to audits and inspections by regulatory authorities. Provides, explains, and contextualizes CSV documentation in the context of regulatory inspections.
* Develops and enforces risk\-proportionate quality assurance (QA) standards and processes for statistical programming. Ensures that data processing activities and study status reports provided to investigators are accurate, validated, and compliant with established QA standards.

**Data Management:**

* Develops and maintains an understanding of REDCap project design in the context of clinical research and Health Canada regulated clinical trials.
* Designs, maintains, and improves REDCap database projects for research and operational uses.
* Performs basic descriptive analysis, data quality monitoring, data processing, and visualizations of imperfect datasets using the R programming language.
* Assists with REDCap system administration, including user and project management, compliance monitoring, and training coordination.
* Schedules and coordinates training events in consultation with relevant user and stakeholder groups.
* Responds to REDCap enquiries from the research community and troubleshoots research database issues.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.

Additional information

The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).

If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact UM.Accommodation@umanitoba.ca or 204\-474\-7195\. Please note this contact information is for accommodation reasons only.

Application materials, including letters of reference, will be handled in accordance with the protection of privacy provisions of "The Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act" (Manitoba). Please note that curriculum vitae will be provided to participating members of the search process.

Pay: $60,205\.60\-$86,850\.40 per year

Benefits:

* Casual dress
* Employee assistance program
* Wellness program
* Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Winnipeg, MB R3E 0T6

title: Senior Research Assistant, People, Places, Policies, and Prospects: Affordable Rental Housing For Those Most in Need
description:
Senior Research Assistants are normally graduate students, or University of Winnipeg undergraduate students, employed to assist in the execution and evaluation of research projects in their area of study. The positions may oversee Research Assistants in areas such as the use of proper techniques, analysis, and documentation.



Senior Research Assistants work under the general supervision or direction of more senior researchers or faculty members, and are expected to exercise judgment in prioritizing, planning and organizing their own work within defined parameters.


**Position Overview:**



The Sr. Research Assistant will work with the Researcher to analyse different largescale Statistics Canada datasets in the Research Data Centre (RDC) to understand outcomes of different types of rental housing across Canada.


**Duties:**


* The Sr RA will work with the researcher to prepare a literature review of Indigenous rental housing across Canada.
* The Sr RA will participate in interviews with our research partners.
* The Sr RA will work in the RDC with the researcher to understand different housing types and outcomes for renters in Canada.
* Working with the Researcher, the Sr RA will develop statistical models to analyse in Stata or R to examine outcomes of housing types.
* The Sr RA will write articles iwth the researcher based on findings.
* The Sr RA will work with a national team studying rental housing and participate in bi\-weekly online Zoom meetings to discuss progress, findings, and considerations in this work.


**Qualifications:**


* Must have completed requirements for undergraduate degree in business, social sciences, or social work.
* Must have attained a B grade in statistics I and II.
* Some training Stat or R is preferred.
* Preference will be given to qualified Indigenous students, please self\-identify within your application.

title: Senior Research Assistant, The Right to Housing for People with Disabilities in Canada
description:
Senior Research Assistants are normally graduate students, or University of Winnipeg undergraduate students, employed to assist in the execution and evaluation of research projects in their area of study. The positions may oversee Research Assistants in areas such as the use of proper techniques, analysis, and documentation.



Senior Research Assistants work under the general supervision or direction of more senior researchers or faculty members, and are expected to exercise judgment in prioritizing, planning and organizing their own work within defined parameters.


**Position Overview:**



The Research Assistant will be responsible for project organization. They may also assist with literature reviews, meetings, conducting research interviews and focus groups, gathering data, and data analysis.


**Duties:**


* Organize the various components of the project.

* Perform academic research, standard tests and experiments.
* Monitor and document research status and findings.

title: Project Coordinator
description:
**Project Coordinator – Residential Renovations**

Valdek Construction Inc. is a residential renovation company specializing in home additions, basement renovations, and home remodeling. We are looking for a highly organized, experienced, and motivated individual to join our team as a Project Coordinator.

This role is ideal for someone who understands residential renovations and additions, enjoys keeping projects moving, and is confident communicating with clients, trades, suppliers, and internal team members. The successful candidate will help coordinate day\-to\-day project activities, support active jobs, and assist with setting up future projects for success.

This is a team\-based role working closely with another Project Coordinator, our Designer, Lead Carpenters, subcontractors, suppliers, and Valdek leadership.

**What Valdek Construction Inc. will offer the successful candidate:**

* Competitive wage: $25–$35/hour, based on experience and performance
* Full\-time schedule
* Monday to Friday schedule
* No weekend work
* Eligibility for sick days and benefits after 90 days of employment
* Dental care
* Extended health care
* Vision care
* Vehicle allowance
* Gas card
* Crew meetings in\-house every 3rd Friday
* Positive growth environment; we want you to grow professionally as well as personally
* Positive, like\-minded team members and leaders who have a passion for growth and development
* Advancement opportunities
* Discrimination\-free workplace
* Comfortable work wear with Valdek logo

**Candidate Skills \& Requirements:**

The ideal candidate will be organized, proactive, experienced in residential renovations, and able to keep track of many moving parts at once. This role requires someone who understands how renovation projects actually come together, from planning and prep through day\-to\-day execution and close\-out.

Required skills and qualities:

* Residential renovation and/or addition experience is required
* Strong understanding of residential renovation projects, including the key steps involved in planning, coordinating, and executing the work
* Wide range of renovation knowledge, including experience with home additions, basement renovations, remodeling, structural work, finishing details, and general residential construction processes
* Ability to anticipate what needs to happen next on a project and identify potential issues before they become bigger problems
* Strong communication skills with clients, crew members, subcontractors, suppliers, designers, and leadership
* Highly organized and detail\-oriented
* Able to manage multiple active projects and upcoming tasks at the same time
* Comfortable coordinating with clients, crew members, subcontractors, suppliers, and internal team members
* Strong comfort level with technology is required
* Able and willing to use project management software daily to document project details, schedules, updates, tasks, photos, selections, changes, and communication
* Experience using JobTread or similar construction/project management software is considered a strong asset
* Punctual, dependable, and professional
* Positive attitude and strong work ethic
* Highly driven to learn and improve
* Respectful and presentable
* Class 5 driver’s licence and reliable vehicle required

**Job Duties \& Responsibilities:**

The Project Coordinator will help support the planning, coordination, communication, documentation, and execution of Valdek renovation projects.

Responsibilities may include:

* Assist with coordinating day\-to\-day project activities
* Help set up upcoming projects before work begins
* Coordinate materials, supplies, deliveries, and trade scheduling
* Communicate with clients in a friendly, professional, and positive manner
* Communicate project information to Lead Carpenters, crew members, subcontractors, suppliers, the Designer, and Valdek leadership
* Assist with scheduling and updating project timelines
* Use JobTread daily to document project details, tasks, schedules, notes, photos, updates, selections, change orders, and communication
* Help ensure project information is accurate, current, and easy for the team to access
* Review project details, scopes, selections, drawings, and site information before work begins
* Help ensure the right people have the right information before tasks are started
* Visit jobsites as needed to check progress, gather information, take photos, review details, and support the team
* Assist with coordinating deficiencies, follow\-up items, and project close\-out tasks
* Help track project details and communicate changes as needed
* Assist with ordering, pickups, and delivery coordination when required
* Help ensure projects are staying organized, efficient, and aligned with Valdek’s quality standards
* Work as part of a team with another Project Coordinator and Designer to help projects move smoothly from planning into production

**General Expectations:**

* Take ownership of tasks and follow through until they are complete
* Communicate clearly and early when something needs attention
* Help identify issues before they become bigger problems
* Support the crew by helping ensure jobs are prepared and organized
* Keep project details properly documented and updated in JobTread
* Maintain a professional and positive relationship with clients and team members
* Be willing to learn, adapt, and grow with the company
* Contribute to a positive, organized, team\-focused work environment

Our team members are passionate about growth, innovation, quality workmanship, and customer collaboration. We are constantly striving to improve and better ourselves so that we can better support our customers. If you have a growth mindset, strong organization skills, construction experience, and enjoy helping projects run smoothly, you are probably a great fit for our team.

**Valdek Construction Inc. Core Values**

OWNERSHIP – We take responsibility for making things better, not just getting through the day.

TEAMWORK – We help each other win by being prepared, dependable, and accountable.

QUALITY – We do the job right the first time because we planned it right before we started.

COMMUNICATION – The right people have the right information early enough to act on it.

WE SHOW UP – We show up prepared so execution is smooth, safe, and efficient.

**To be considered for this position, please highlight in your resume or CV:**

* Your residential renovation and/or addition experience
* Your experience coordinating trades, materials, schedules, clients, or project details
* Your experience with project management software, construction software, or documentation systems
* Any experience using JobTread or similar software
* Any leadership, site support, project management, or customer service experience

Please email your resume to mallory@valdekconstruction.com

Pay: $25\.00\-$35\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Dental care
* Extended health care
* Vision care

Licence/Certification:

* Driving Licence (required)

Location:

* Winnipeg, MB R3E 2V7 (required)

title: Senior Project Engineer - Asset Replacement and Refurbishment Program
description:
Under the direction of the Wastewater Planning and Project Delivery Branch Head, the Senior Project Engineer \- ARRP is responsible for management of the Department’s Asset Refurbishment \& Replacement Program (ARRP), including investment planning, project prioritization and project delivery of capital projects at the City’s three Wastewater Treatment Plants. The project work includes project definition, consultant selection, project management, administration, coordination, and implementation. Projects include engineering studies, designs, construction and contract administration. Capital projects under the ARRP are critical for safe and resilient operation of the City’s treatment facilities, and for continued compliance with the applicable Environmental Act Licenses.


**As the** ***Senior Project Engineer,*****you will:**


* Plan and initiate projects to ensure execution and products conform to Department and City\-wide broad objectives and needs.


* Ensure technical approach complies with appropriate standards and conforms to project objectives.


* Administer capital budgets and expenditures.


* Administer project schedules.


* Assign work, provide leadership, coach and mentor team members.
* Acquire goods and services from external organizations.


* Develop and maintain capital project delivery templates and process tools.


* Develop, initiate and administer risk management procedures.


* Coordinate project execution, approvals and implementation.


* Disseminate and receive project information.
**Your education and qualifications include:**


* Bachelor’s Degree in Science (Civil, Mechanical, or Biosystems Engineering).


* Master’s Degree in Environmental / Civil Engineering is an asset.
* 7 years prior experience in a field directly related to the duties of the position.
* Must have knowledge with project management processes for water / wastewater infrastructure capital projects and related procedures, including: scope definition; project accounting and cost management; scheduling; resource management; risk management; dispute avoidance and dispute resolution.


* Demonstrated experience with municipal infrastructure, planning, design, construction, and operations, including wastewater treatment systems, operations and servicing.


* Demonstrated experience with construction materials, methods and practices, including job site safety.


* Must be familiar with relevant municipal government policies and procedures, including purchasing policies, and legal and contractual requirements.


* Must possess strong computer skills, including Microsoft Office, Microsoft Project and related applications.


* Must have strong demonstrated problem solving and analytical abilities.


* Demonstrated ability to manage multiple time constraints and prioritize workloads.


* Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively, both verbally and in writing. Must have demonstrated technical and non\-technical verbal communication ability. Must be proficient at technical writing.


* Must have strong interpersonal skills, including leadership and the ability to work in teams, facilitate teambuilding, conduct meetings and possess strong negotiation skills.


* Must demonstrate an ability to provide direction effectively and efficiently in the planning and scheduling of work.


* Demonstrated ability to establish and maintain positive working relationships with employees at all levels of the organization, external contacts and the public, in accordance with the Respectful Workplace Standard.


* Knowledge and understanding of the occupational hazards, safety precautions, regulations and procedures that is applicable to the area of work.
* **IMPORTANT**: Applicants who have been educated outside of Canada must have education which is comparable to the minimum qualification in Canada. Applicants submitting foreign credentials require an official academic assessment report issued by a recognized Canadian assessment service https://canalliance.org/en/ at application.

**Conditions of employment:**


* The successful candidate must maintain legal eligibility to work in Canada. If the successful candidate possesses a work permit, it is their responsibility to ensure the permit remains valid.


* A Police Record Check satisfactory to the employer will be required from the successful candidate, at their expense.


* Must possess and maintain a valid Class 5 Manitoba Driver’s License. The successful applicant will be required to provide a driver’s notice in force document upon request.


* Professional Engineer designation and eligible for registration with Engineers Geoscientists Manitoba, (E.G.M.).

title: Junior Data Analyst – Energy & Sustainability
description:
**Overview**

Starting in early June, you'll spend 8 weeks helping build something real: a publicly accessible retrofit payback calculator that shows Manitobans exactly what home energy improvements could mean for their wallet and their carbon footprint. You'll be supported by SBM staff and contribute to a growing suite of public resources, including SBM's Home Retrofit Guides, that help Manitobans take action on home energy use. There's also potential for a part\-time extension after the placement for the right person.

**Duties**

* Build and refine a retrofit payback calculator estimating upfront costs, rebates, annual energy savings, and long\-term household savings
* Gather and organize Manitoba\-specific data on energy prices, typical retrofit costs, and equipment lifespans
* Prepare clear assumption tables and plain\-language explanations so any homeowner can understand the results
* Create simple charts and a short user guide to accompany the calculator
* Test SBM's existing Home Retrofit Guides for usability, readability, and mobile responsiveness, and summarize findings with practical recommendations

**Requirements**

**Essential**

* Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or person granted refugee protection \- aged 15 to 30 at the start of employment (Canada Summer Jobs requirement)
* Enrolled in or recently graduated from a post\-secondary program \- statistics, data science, environmental studies, economics, or related field
* Solid spreadsheet skills (Excel or Google Sheets) \- you can build a formula, structure a dataset, and make it readable
* Strong written communication \- you can translate numbers into plain language a homeowner would understand

**Assets (not required)**

* Familiarity with basic data visualization (charts, graphs, simple dashboards)
* Experience with user testing, usability review, or plain\-language writing
* Knowledge of home energy systems, retrofits, or Manitoba's energy landscape
* Any exposure to web publishing or simple online tools

**Personal qualities we're looking for**

* Curious and self\-directed, comfortable working independently and figuring things out
* Detail\-oriented without losing sight of the big picture
* Genuinely motivated by sustainability or climate action

**What You'll Gain**

* A published, publicly accessible tool for your portfolio
* Mentorship from energy modelling and sustainable building professionals
* Applied experience in data analysis, financial modelling, and user\-centered design
* A collaborative, mission\-driven work environment — with potential to continue part\-time

**Application deadline: Wednesday, May 20, 2025**

title: INTERNATIONAL STUDENT PROGRAM - 1,0 Student Success/Experience Coordinators ( 2 positions)
description:
**International Student Program (ISP):**  

**Student Success/Experience Coordinators (2 positions)**

**BULLETIN NUMBER: 101/E/26 DATE POSTED: May 8, 2026**  

**SCHOOL: MONTEREY BOARD OFFICE**  

**COMMENCING:** August 17, 2026 or to be arranged
  

**COMPENSATION:** Non\-union Salaried **$82,218 to $89,873**  

**HOURS:** 7\.0 hours per day
  

**JOB SUMMARY:**
  

Reporting to the International Department Director, Student Success/Experience Coordinators work with the Homestay
  

Coordinator to support ISP students’ retention and success, helping them adapt to their time in Winnipeg, living with a Canadian
  

family, and attending school in Canada. They liaise between students’ placement schools, homestay, the ISP office, and ISP
  

recruitment partners, organizing cultural events and activities, and responding to known and emerging ISP student needs. The
  

incumbents are required to travel regularly between LRSD Board Offices and LRSD school locations, and they may be required to
  

travel outside Winnipeg. Additionally, job may involve supporting LRSD’s Arts and Technology Centre to enroll and integrate
  

young adult international students enrolled in its Post Graduate Work Permit Eligible Certificate Programs.


**RESPONSIBILITIES AND DUTIES:**


As a Student Coordinator, you will:


* Liaise with LRSD high schools’ Student Services to ensure programming matches students’ study goals and origin

school academic requirements and to source English language, translation or academic services as required
* Encourage student engagement in extracurricular activities and local, in\-community sports, groups and events
* Collaborate with ISP’s Homestay Coordinator to develop students’ understanding of cultural differences and how to

navigate disputes arising from them, meeting and modelling conflict management strategies as necessary
  

* Support students to source medical care (mental and physical) as needed
* Monitor study permit and attendance compliance and track and report student performance and program
participation to internal and external stakeholders as required
  

* Support in\-print and online marketing of the LRSD Go Global experience, developing content to engage current and
future international students and current and future LRSD homestays
  

* Closely monitor and evaluate international student and recruitment partner satisfaction and success, acting to
improve outcomes as required
  

* Liaise with insurance provider, developing and sharing expertise in insurance policies, medical care access, and claims
submission processes
  

* Co\-plan and facilitate school intake and orientations sessions as well as local, monthly cultural events, and plan and
implement regional/national cultural experiences, including overnight fieldtrips and multi\-day and camp experiences
  

* Support the planning and implementation of ISP Summer Programming
* Comply with current legislation (Federal and Provincial) and LRSD Divisional Administrative Guidelines and protocols.
**QUALIFICATIONS (EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE):**

* Completion of post\-secondary education in a related field is required and five years’ experience working in high school

setting (preferred) or a combination of education and experience will be considered.
* Knowledge of international student educational goals and programming, the Manitoba high school timetabling and credit

system, and other countries’ education systems.
* Demonstrated ability to plan, develop, promote, facilitate, and evaluate programs, activities, and events.
* Demonstrated ability to use social media to create content to communicate and market student and homestay experiences

and to create community.
  

* Demonstrated experience accessing databases and using student information systems, specifically.
* Knowledge of federal and provincial legislation related to international education, including familiarity with Immigration,
Refugee, and Citizenship Canadian regulations.
  

* Ability to work flexible hours, evenings, weekends, and on\-call.
* Superior written communication skills, excellent communication skills and the ability to act with diplomacy.
* Intercultural competence and the ability to work with diverse groups and to demonstrate sensitivity to other cultures.
* Demonstrated accountability and the ability to work independently, often without direct supervision.
* Demonstrated ability to deal with confidential and sensitive matters.
* Demonstrated ability to organize and prioritize multiple tasks.
* Demonstrated ability to work with minimal supervision.
* Demonstrated ability to work effectively in a team environment.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint)
* Experience is a Post\-secondary environment is an asset
* Ability to speak multiple languages is an asset.
**CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT:**

* Satisfactory Criminal Record Check, including Vulnerable Sector Search.
* Clear report from the Child Abuse Registry.
* Review and comply with Divisional policies and guidelines.
* Continued employment is subject to maintaining legal eligibility to work in Canada.
* Valid Manitoba Driver’s License, current Driver’s Abstract, and access to a reliable vehicle is required.
* Valid passport and ability to travel within Canada and internationally.
* Required to work non\-standard hours, including evenings and weekends.
* Required to work after hours on\-call on a rotating basis.
* Respect In School or equivalent training is required.

**APPLICATION PROCEDURE:**  

The Louis Riel School Division is committed to employment equity and accessibility. We encourage applications from members
  

of equity\-seeking groups with diverse abilities, backgrounds, cultures, identities.
  

  

* Apply by using the LRSD online submission process at www.lrsd.net.
* Include in your resume and cover letter your strengths and abilities and clearly define how you meet eligibility for the role.
* Identify the names and contact information of three references.
* Address cover letter and resume to Staff Services, Louis Riel School Division.
* Quote the Bulletin Number on the application; a separate application is required for each job bulletin.
* Upload your resume and cover letter as one document.

  

We thank all applicants for their interest. Only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.
**APPLICATION DEADLINE: May 22, 2026**

title: Coordinator - Sun Life Indigenous Diabetes Prevention Program, Indigenous Student Services Centre *Term*
description:
**\*This is a 2\-year term position\***



The Indigenous Student Services Centre (ISSC) provides culturally grounded, holistic supports to Indigenous students at the University of Winnipeg. The Centre supports student success through programming focused on wellness, food security, cultural connection and community engagement.



The ISSC\-led Sun Life Indigenous Diabetes Prevention Program supports Indigenous students through diabetes prevention education, culturally relevant nutrition and physical activity programming, traditional food teachings, and access to healthy foods. The program aligns with Sun Life’s Theory of Change by emphasizing prevention, sustained behaviour change, and outcomes\-based impact measurement. **The Coordinator is a full\-time term** role responsible for program planning, coordination, delivery, evaluation, and reporting.


**Responsibilities:**


* Plan, coordinate, and implement culturally relevant diabetes prevention programming for Indigenous students.
* Coordinate workshops, presentations, and activities related to nutrition, physical activity, and traditional food systems.
* Ensure programming aligns with ISSC values, Indigenous protocols, and Sun Life’s Theory of Change.
* Manage program schedules, facilities, supplies, and logistics.
* Engage Indigenous students through relationship\-based outreach and culturally safe recruitment practices.
* Collaborate with campus and community partners to promote program participation.
* Coordinate participant accessibility needs and support inclusive participation.
* Coordination of food security initiatives.
* Coordinate the Emergency Food Pantry and Grab\-and\-Go Station.
* Manage food procurement, storage, distribution, and partnerships with campus food services and external suppliers.
* Coordinate food\-related programming, including workshops and events using traditional and diabetes\-friendly foods.
* Monitor usage and accessibility of food supports.
* Coordinate engagement with Indigenous Knowledge Keepers, Elders, and facilitators/dietitians, following appropriate cultural protocols.
* Support honoraria, logistics, and relationship\-building practices.
* Collaborate with campus partners.
* Support ethical and reciprocal partnerships aligned with Indigenous values.
* Collect and maintain accurate program data, including participation, engagement, and demographic information.
* Coordinate pre\-, post\-, and follow\-up surveys to measure outputs and outcomes.
* Track behaviour change indicators related to nutrition, physical activity, and knowledge.
* Prepare annual reports aligned with Sun Life’s Theory of Change and funding requirements.
* Support program promotion through social media, campus communications, and community outreach.
* Coordinate recognition of program partners and funders in collaboration with ISSC and the University of Winnipeg Foundation.
* Contribute to content development related to program activities and impact


**Qualifications:**


* Post\-secondary degree in Health Sciences, Indigenous Studies, Nutrition, Kinesiology, Social Sciences, or a related field.
* Experience coordinating community\-based or campus\-based programs.
* Experience working with Indigenous students and/or Indigenous communities.
* Experience in food security, health promotion, or wellness programming.
* Experience supervising staff, contractors, or volunteers.
* Strong organizational and coordination skills.
* Ability to work respectfully with Indigenous Knowledge Keepers, Elders, and community partners.
* Understanding of culturally safe and trauma\-informed practice.
* Ability to collect, manage, and report program data.
* Strong communication and relationship\-building skills.
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively.


**An equivalent combination of education, experience, skills, knowledge and abilities may be considered.**


**Condition(s) of Employment:**


* Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.


**The salary range for this position will be from $48,648\.60 to $68,741\.40 annually.**


**Note:** The work described in this posting will be conducted in\-person, with an opportunity to apply for Remote Work for up to one (1\) day per week.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion and recognizes that a diverse staff and faculty benefits and enriches the work, learning and research environments, and is essential to academic and institutional excellence. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and encourage women, racialized persons, Indigenous persons, persons with disabilities, and 2SLGBTQ\+ persons to confidentially self\-identify at time of application.



The University of Winnipeg is committed to ensuring employment opportunities are accessible for all applicants. If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.



The personal information of applicants is collected under the authority of the University of Winnipeg Act and 36(1\)(b) of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. All personal information collected via the recruitment process is used to assess the applicant’s suitability, eligibility, and qualifications for employment, and to otherwise support recruitment activities. This information will be provided to participating members of the recruitment process. Questions regarding the collection of your personal information may be directed to the Director, HR Services, 515 Portage Avenue, Winnipeg, MB, R3B 2E9 or human\_resources@uwinnipeg.ca.

Project Manager – Commercial & Industrial Construction
description:
We are seeking an experienced **Project Manager** to join our growing construction team. The successful candidate will lead and manage **commercial plaza and industrial warehouse projects** from initial planning through to final completion. This role requires strong leadership, solid construction knowledge, and the ability to coordinate effectively with subcontractors, engineers, architects, and other stakeholders to ensure projects are delivered **on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards**.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead and manage the **full project lifecycle**, including pre\-construction planning, design coordination, construction execution, and project close\-out.
* Develop and maintain **project execution plans**, detailed schedules, and work breakdown structures to ensure timely achievement of project milestones.
* Coordinate with **subcontractors, engineers, architects, consultants, and inspectors** to ensure accurate execution of design and construction requirements.
* Monitor project scope, schedule, and budget, proactively identifying risks and implementing mitigation strategies.
* Ensure full compliance with **applicable building codes, safety regulations, and company quality standards**.
* Conduct regular site meetings and progress reviews with internal teams and external stakeholders.
* Review and manage change orders, RFIs, progress draws, and project documentation.
* Manage **multiple projects simultaneously**, effectively prioritizing deadlines, resources, and subcontractor activities.
* Support project close\-out activities, including deficiency resolution, final inspections, and documentation handover.

**Qualifications \& Experience**

* Minimum 2**\+ years of experience** as a Project Manager in commercial and/or industrial construction.
* Proven experience managing **commercial plazas, industrial warehouses, or similar construction projects**.
* Strong understanding of construction drawings, specifications, contracts, and scheduling.
* Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills.
* Ability to work collaboratively with diverse project teams and stakeholders.
* Proficiency with project management and scheduling tools is an asset.
* PMP or equivalent certification is considered an asset (but not mandatory).

**What We Offer**

* Opportunity to work on **high\-quality commercial and industrial projects**
* Competitive compensation based on experience
* Supportive and growing team environment
* Long\-term career growth opportunities

Pay: $20\.00\-$40\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* On\-site parking
* RRSP match

Application question(s):

* Do you have valid Class 5 driver’s license and vehicle for transport?
* How many years of experience do you have managing commercial plaza or industrial warehouse projects?
* Do you have experience using Procore or any similar project management software, if yes which one
* Are you willing to relocate to winnipeg as we are based in winnipeg, also this position requires you to visit the sites in winnipeg and out of winnipeg based on project requirement?
* What is your legal status in Canada?
* Do you have knowledge of Manitoba building codes and construction regulations?

The Manitoba government recognizes the importance of building an exemplary public service reflective of the citizens it serves, where diverse abilities, backgrounds, cultures, identities, languages and perspectives drives a high standard of service and innovation. The Manitoba government supports equitable employment practices and promotes representation of designated groups (women, Indigenous people, persons with disabilities, visible minorities).


Employment Equity is a factor in selection for this competition. Consideration will be given to Indigenous people and persons with disabilities.


An eligibility list may be created for similar positions and will remain in effect for 12 months.


The Procurement and Supply Chain Division sets the strategic direction, policies, and processes for procurement and supply chain related functions across government. Procurement and Supply Chain provides a vital link between vendors and the Manitoba Government's purchasers of goods and services. Within the division, The Materials and Distribution Agency is looking to fill the Project Management Specialist position.


Duties:
  

The project manager specialist leads or supports multiple projects by defining clear scopes, engaging stakeholders, and ensuring technical feasibility. They evaluate programs, products, and services to identify improvement opportunities and track project performance using appropriate tools and methods. The specialist prepares high quality written materials that meet government standards and communicates effectively with managers, agencies, and departments throughout project development. They also assist in researching, designing, and producing high level documents, including proposals, service agreements, memorandums of understanding, newsletters, catalogues, signage, and web content.


Qualifications:
  

**Essentials:**


* Post\-secondary education in communications, public relations, journalism, design or multimedia specialization or equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.
* Experience in continuous improvement methodologies, operational excellence, data analysis and process mapping.
* Relevant project management experience.
* Experience analyzing client needs and developing solutions aligned with organizational goals
* Excellent verbal communications skills with the ability to present to a variety of stakeholders.
* Excellent written communications skills with the ability to prepare a variety of documentation.
* Knowledge of safety protocols, including the safe operation of equipment, proper material handling, hazard identification, and effective emergency response planning.
* Proficient with desktop publishing software, Adobe applications, web\- authoring applications, project management applications.

**Desired:**
* Six Sigma certification (Yellor or Green belt) is desired.

Conditions of Employment:


* Must be legally entitled to work in Canada
* Must be able to work in a warehouse environment.
